name: Effective Thesis
notes_head: Effective Thesis is an organization that aims to empower students and early-career individuals to focus their research and careers towards solving the world's biggest challenges. The organization was founded in July 2017 by David Janku, with initial support from Daniel Hnyk and members of the Czech EA Association. Since then, they have directly supported over 2,000 students across all services and coached more than 500 students one-on-one. The organization is based on the principles of Effective Altruism and is informed by the career advice organisation, 80,000 Hours.

name: Oxford Martin AI Governance Initiative
notes_head: The Oxford Martin AI Governance Initiative aims to understand and anticipate the lasting risks from AI through rigorous research into the technical and computational elements of AI development, combined with deep policy analysis. The Initiative is co-led by Professor Robert Trager, a social scientist specialising in international relations and frontier AI regulation, and Professor Michael Osborne, a specialist in machine learning. The Oxford Martin AI Governance Initiative employs 30 people (+65.0% YoY, +13 people) and operates in 7 countries (including United Kingdom, United States, China, Fr

name: Cooperative AI Summer School
notes_head: The Cooperative AI Summer School is an advanced programme for early-career professionals already working in or studying cooperative AI, taking place over five intensive days with talks from leading researchers and collaborative projects. The 2025 edition was held on the banks of the River Thames in Marlow, England, gathering over 65 students and early-career professionals from across the world. The Cooperative AI Foundation is committed to building a diverse and inclusive research community and is registered with the Charity Commission for England and Wales under charity number 1201294.

name: Leaf: Dilemmas and Dangers in AI
notes_head: Leaf: Dilemmas and Dangers in AI is an interdisciplinary online fellowship helping teenagers explore how to steer cutting-edge AI technology toward benefits for humanity. The program is designed for smart, curious, and ambitiously altruistic students aged 15-19 who haven't yet started university. 97% of participants would recommend the course, with 97% of 300+ Finalists ranking 7/10 or above on their likelihood to recommend this course. The fellowship runs for 5 weeks covering topics from AI foundations to personal impact strategies.

name: Mentorship for Alignment Research Students (MARS)
notes_head: MARS (Mentorship for Alignment Research Students) is a part-time programme where teams of 2–4 work with experienced mentors to produce published research, providing a kick-off week in Cambridge, compute, and research management. The program is run by Cambridge AI Safety Hub (CAISH) and is a volunteer-based program connecting aspiring researchers with experienced mentors to conduct AI-safety research. Previous participants have published at NeurIPS, ICML, EMNLP, and ControlConf, with mentorship from researchers at Redwood Research, Apollo Research, Google DeepMind, the University of Cambridge &

name: Supervised Program for Alignment Research (SPAR)
loc: Remote program run by Kairos (Berkeley, CA)
notes_head: The Supervised Program for Alignment Research (SPAR) is a part-time, virtual mentorship program that connects mentors and mentees for three-month AI safety and governance research projects. SPAR is run by Kairos, a nonprofit whose mission is to help society navigate the transition to transformative AI. The program accepts mentees with relevant technical or policy background at any level, from undergraduate students to mid-career professionals. SPAR research has been accepted at ICML and NeurIPS, covered by TIME, and led to full-time job offers for mentees.

name: NYU Alignment Research Group (ARG)
notes_head: The NYU Alignment Research Group (ARG) is a set of researchers doing empirical work with language models that aims to address longer-term concerns about the impacts of deploying highly-capable AI systems. The group overlaps and works closely with a variety of groups at NYU, including Machine Learning for Language (ML2), CILVR, the Center for Data Science, and The Department of Linguistics. The group was set up as a new lab by Sam Bowman, who stated 'I'm setting up a new lab! It's called the NYU Alignment Research Group'. Current leadership includes Sam Bowman as PI (on leave), with He He and M

name: Center for Human-Compatible AI (CHAI)
loc: Berkeley, California (multi-site: UC Berkeley HQ, Cornell, Michigan)
notes_head: The Center for Human-Compatible AI (CHAI) is a research center at the University of California, Berkeley focusing on advanced artificial intelligence safety methods. The center was founded in 2016 by a group of academics led by Berkeley computer science professor and AI expert Stuart J. Russell. CHAI's mission is to develop the conceptual and technical wherewithal to reorient the general thrust of AI research towards provably beneficial systems. The center has 23 employees according to LinkedIn data.

name: MIT Algorithmic Alignment Group
notes_head: Led by Assistant Professor Dylan Hadfield-Menell at MIT CSAIL. Team includes 1 postdoc, 4 PhD students, 2 Masters students, and numerous alumni. Research spans technical AI alignment, policy, and governance. 150+ publications since 2022 covering red-teaming, robustness, AI audits, and alignment frameworks. Funded by academic grants and philanthropic sources including Open Philanthropy connections.

name: Future of Humanity Institute (closed 2024)
notes_head: Founded 2005 by Nick Bostrom, closed April 2024 due to bureaucratic disputes with Oxford. Multidisciplinary institute focusing on long-term existential risks and AI safety. Received major funding from Open Philanthropy ($2M+ grants), Elon Musk, and other tech philanthropists. Key publications include Bostrom's 'Superintelligence' and numerous policy papers on AI governance. Staff included Toby Ord, Anders Sandberg, and other prominent longtermist researchers.

name: Tsinghua University (Institute for AI International Governance & Center for AI Governance)
notes_head: Two main AI governance centers: Institute for AI International Governance (I-AIIG) founded 2018, and Center for AI Governance (2024) under School of Public Policy & Management. Led by Dean Xue Lan. Hosts major international conferences, publishes policy reports, and won UNESCO-Uzbekistan AI Ethics Prize 2024. Collaborates with UN, produces Beijing AI Principles, trains next generation of AI governance researchers.

name: MIT FutureTech
notes_head: Founded 2019 by Neil Thompson, grew to 110+ researchers by 2025. Housed within MIT CSAIL and Initiative on Digital Economy. Attracted $25M+ funding from Open Philanthropy, NSF, IBM, Google, Microsoft, others. Key outputs include AI Risk Repository, computational limits research, and Moore's Law studies. Director Neil Thompson advises Congress, Federal Reserve, Pentagon on AI policy.

name: Center for Long-Term Cybersecurity
notes_head: Founded 2015 at UC Berkeley School of Information. Houses the AI Security Initiative (AISI) which develops risk management standards and evaluation frameworks. Key outputs include AI Risk Management Standards Profile for General-Purpose AI, Agentic AI Risk Management Profile, and research on intolerable risk thresholds. Funded by Hewlett Foundation, Open Philanthropy, Future of Life Institute, and tech companies. Runs AI Policy Hub training program for graduate students. Partners with NIST on AI risk management frameworks.

name: Centre for the Study of Existential Risk (CSER)
notes_head: Founded 2012 by Lord Martin Rees, Jaan Tallinn, and Huw Price. Interdisciplinary research centre at University of Cambridge with ~38 staff. Part of Institute for Technology and Humanity. Funded by Jaan Tallinn, Matt Wage, Victoria Elenowitz, and other philanthropic sources. Key research areas include AI risks, biotechnology risks, environmental risks, and global governance. Notable publications on international AI governance and frontier model regulation.

name: AI Safety Initiative at Georgia Tech
notes_head: Founded in 2022 as student-led initiative, now employs 19 people with faculty advisors from GT's computing and engineering schools. Runs educational fellowships, technical research programs, and policy analysis. Members collaborate with major AI safety orgs including Anthropic, Center for AI Safety, METR, and RAND. Published multiple papers in top venues (ACL, ICLR) and submitted formal policy recommendations to federal agencies. Supported by Open Philanthropy and Coefficient Giving.

name: Future Impact Group: Fellowship
loc: Oxford, United Kingdom (remote-first, operates in 12 countries)
notes_head: Founded 2023 by Suryansh Mehta and Callum Evans. 45 employees across research and operations. 12-week remote fellowship program matching postgraduate students with senior researchers. Three focus areas: AI Policy, Philosophy for Safe AI, and AI Sentience. Funded by Open Philanthropy and Longview Philanthropy. Project leads from Oxford, GovAI, DeepMind, and other leading institutions.

name: Global Challenges Project
loc: Boston, MA, USA (with operations in Philippines)
notes_head: Founded 2021, runs intensive 3-day residential workshops for university students exploring AI safety and biosecurity. 4-person team led by Director Liam Epstein (MA from Leverhulme Centre for Future of Intelligence). Funded by Open Philanthropy. Operates workshops globally with 20-35 attendees each. Part of Rethink Priorities Special Projects program providing operational support.

name: Arcadia Impact
notes_head: Founded 2022 as UK charity. Runs multiple AI governance and safety programs: AI Governance Taskforce (12-week policy research program), LASR Labs (technical AI safety research), AI Safety Engineering Taskforce, and Orion AI Governance Initiative. Primary funder is Coefficient Giving (£8M+ committed). Led by Joe Hardie and Erin Robertson. Partners with organizations like GovAI, UK AISI, SaferAI, and Future Society on research projects.

name: Computational and Biological Learning Lab (University of Cambridge)
notes_head: Part of University of Cambridge Department of Engineering, established research lab focused on computational neuroscience and machine learning. Contains multiple research groups including the Krueger AI Safety Lab (KASL) founded 2022. Key members include David Krueger (AI alignment researcher who worked with UK Frontier AI Taskforce), Carl Rasmussen, Adrian Weller, and other ML researchers. Conducts both biological learning research and AI safety work including alignment, interpretability, and policy engagement.

name: Krueger AI Safety Lab (KASL)
loc: Cambridge, UK (University of Cambridge) + Montreal, Canada (Mila)
notes_head: Founded ~2022, led by David Krueger (Assistant Professor at Cambridge). Dual affiliation with University of Cambridge and Mila (Quebec AI Institute). Team includes ~15 PhD students, postdocs, and research assistants. Key publications include 'Goal Misgeneralization in Deep Reinforcement Learning' (ICML 2022), 'Foundational Challenges in Assuring Alignment and Safety of LLMs' (2024). Runs paid AI safety internship programs. Previously based at University of Cambridge, now also affiliated with Mila.

name: Meridian Cambridge
notes_head: Coworking space and field-building nonprofit based in Cambridge, UK. Houses projects like CAISH and the ERA:AI Fellowship. Runs 3-12 month Visiting Researcher Programme for AI safety and governance researchers. Partners with Cambridge AI Safety Hub. Provides workspace, community, and research support including assistance with grant applications to Open Philanthropy and other funders. Contact: visitingresearchers@meridiancambridge.org

name: Alignment Research Engineer Accelerator (ARENA)
loc: London, UK (hosted at London Initiative for Safe AI)
notes_head: Founded 2022, runs 4-5 week intensive ML bootcamps 2-3 times per year. Each cohort accepts ~30 participants. Funded by Coefficient Giving and Open Philanthropy ($18,800 grant). Alumni outcomes include roles at Anthropic, OpenAI, METR, UK AISI, and MATS scholarships. Based at LISA in Shoreditch, covers all participant costs including travel, accommodation, and meals.

name: ML4Good Laboratory - New York University
notes_head: Academic research lab at NYU's Center for Urban Science and Progress, directed by Prof. Daniel B. Neill. Faculty includes experts in biostatistics, epidemiology, and applied statistics. Research applications include disease surveillance, opioid crisis response, criminal justice fairness, and urban analytics. Funded by NSF (including $1M NSF-Amazon Fairness in AI grant), collaborates with city agencies and public health organizations. Over 100 published papers on machine learning for social good applications.

name: Global Priorities Institute (University of Oxford)
notes_head: Founded 2018, closed in 2025. Was an interdisciplinary research center at Oxford conducting foundational research on global priorities and longtermism. Received $3.3M from Open Philanthropy in 2020. Notable publications include 'AI alignment vs AI ethical treatment: Ten challenges' (2024) and research on longtermism paradigm. Staff included Leopold Aschenbrenner (former OpenAI researcher who later founded $1.5B hedge fund) and Bradford Saad who co-authored key AI ethics research.

name: UChicago Existential Risk Laboratory (XLab)
notes_head: Founded at University of Chicago, directed by Daniel Holz (professor of physics, astronomy, astrophysics and chairman of Bulletin of Atomic Scientists). Runs 10-week Summer Research Fellowship producing 60+ alumni since 2022 who have gone to positions at OpenAI, RAND, Google DeepMind, congressional offices, and leading PhD programs. Funded by Open Philanthropy ($301,800 in 2024). Fellows receive $10,000 stipend plus housing and compute credits.

name: Cambridge ERA:AI Fellowship
notes_head: Founded 2021 as ERA Cambridge, non-profit fellowship program. Runs 8-10 week fully-funded research fellowships (Winter and Summer cohorts) accepting 30-35 fellows per cohort. £34,125 annual salary equivalent (pro-rated), plus accommodation, meals, visa support. 38 employees, operates across 9 countries. Alumni now work at UK AI Security Institute, RAND, Oxford Martin AI Governance Initiative, and other key AI policy institutions. Partners with University of Cambridge, provides mentorship from expert network.

name: Human-aligned AI Summer School
notes_head: Annual 4-day educational program held in Prague since at least 2022, organized by Alignment of Complex Systems research group and Center for Theoretical Study at Charles University. Led by Jan Kulveit and team. Attracts researchers, PhD students, and practitioners interested in AI alignment. Features speakers from major AI labs like DeepMind, Anthropic, and universities. Funded through EA/longtermist community channels including past Long-Term Future Fund grants.

name: Impact Research Groups
notes_head: 8-week mentored research program for London students run by Arcadia Impact. Operates 5 research streams: AI governance, technical AI safety, global health, biosecurity, and animal welfare. Led by Alicia Pollard. Awards £2,000 prize for best project. Part of broader Arcadia Impact ecosystem including LASR Labs and AI Safety Engineering Taskforce.

name: Non-Trivial
notes_head: Founded 2022 as Effective Ventures project. 8-week online fellowship for ages 14-20 tackling global challenges. Awards up to $30,000 in scholarships annually, with over $400,000 distributed since founding. Funded primarily by Open Philanthropy. Alumni include UN speakers and winners of major science competitions. 40 employees, 1,856 LinkedIn followers.

name: Pivotal Research Fellowship
notes_head: Founded 2021, operated by Pivotal Research organization with 16 employees. 9-week in-person fellowship program hosted at London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA). 7th cohort running 2026 Q1. Fellows receive £6-8k stipend plus housing/travel. Mentors from top AI safety organizations including Redwood Research, Google DeepMind, SecureBio, FAR AI. Program rated 8.7/10 by fellows with 88 Net Promoter Score. Research outputs include papers on AI liability, interpretability, safety evaluations.

name: PIBBSS Fellowship (Principles of Intelligent Behavior in Biological and Social Systems)
notes_head: Founded 2022, operates as 501(c)(3) nonprofit. Runs 3-month summer fellowship program with ~20 fellows annually, targeting PhD/postdoc researchers from diverse fields for AI safety career transitions. Funded by SFF, LTFF, Cooperative AI Fund, Foresight Institute. Key outcomes include fellows transitioning to AI safety labs, UK AISI, and academic positions. 2024 program produced multiple publication-ready research outputs in mechanistic interpretability, cooperative AI, and formal safety methods.

name: Orion AI Governance Initiative (Arcadia Impact)
notes_head: Founded 2024 as part of Arcadia Impact. UK-based talent development scheme with 3 employees. Offers 9-week AI Policy Accelerator program, mentorship scheme, and internship placements. Has trained 57 participants across programs and placed 2 people into AI policy roles at think tanks. Applications for 2026 program closed January 6, 2026. Project manager is Callum Hinchcliffe, who previously worked on AI governance research and policy.

name: Pathfinder Fellowship (Kairos)
notes_head: Launched 2025 by Kairos, replacing Open Philanthropy's University Organizer Fellowship and Kairos's prior Fieldbuilder Support Program. Supports ~70 AI safety university groups worldwide with funding, mentorship, and resources. 12 staff across 7 countries. Funded by Open Philanthropy grant, fiscally sponsored through BERI. Provides $500+ startup funding plus ongoing support and graduate student stipends.

name: Technical Alignment Research Accelerator (TARA)
loc: Australia & New Zealand (Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane) + Asia-Pacific expansion
notes_head: Founded 2024 by AI Safety Australia & New Zealand (AIS ANZ), led by Yanni Kyriacos. 14-week part-time program based on ARENA curriculum with weekly in-person Saturday sessions. Inaugural cohort targeted 25 participants across Australian cities. Funded by $14,901 grant from Ryan Kidd via Manifund. Expanding to Singapore, Manila, Tokyo, and Taipei in 2026. Program aims to develop technical talent for AI Safety Institutes and demonstrate community credibility to Australian government.

name: Stanford HAI
notes_head: Stanford HAI (Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence) is an interdisciplinary research institute at Stanford University, founded in 2019 by Fei-Fei Li and John Etchemendy, along with James Landay and Chris Manning. The institute advances AI research, education, policy, and practice with the goal of ensuring AI benefits humanity. HAI publishes the annual AI Index Report — the most widely cited benchmark of global AI progress, tracking technical capability, industry investment, policy activity, and public opinion. It has invested over $40 million across 300+ Stanford resea

name: Leverhulme Centre for the Future of Intelligence
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Interdisciplinary research center at the University of Cambridge exploring the nature, ethics, and impact of AI.

name: GovAI Fellowship
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. 3-month program by the Centre for the Governance of AI, designed to help professionals transition to working on AI governance.

name: Heron
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Working to bridge the gap between frontier AI models and the level of cybersecurity they need by connecting professionals to high-leverage opportunities in AI security.

name: Meridian
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Coworking space and field-building nonprofit based in Cambridge, UK. Houses projects like CAISH and the ERA:AI Fellowship, as well as visiting AI safety researchers.

name: AI Safety Asia
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Convening stakeholders, training civil servants and civil society leaders, and conducting regional AI policy research in Asia.

name: AI Safety, Ethics and Society
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Course from the Center for AI Safety (CAIS) covering a wide range of risks while leveraging concepts and frameworks from existing research fields to analyze AI safety.

name: Alignment Research Engineer Accelerator
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. 4–5 week ML engineering upskilling program, focusing on alignment. Aims to provide individuals with the skills, community, and confidence to contribute directly to technical AI safety.

name: Athena Mentorship Program for Women
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. 10-week remote mentorship program for women looking to strengthen their research skills and network in technical Al alignment research.

name: Cambridge Boston Alignment Initiative
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Helping students get into AI safety research via upskilling programs and fellowships. Supports AISST and MAIA.

name: Center for Human-Compatible AI: Internship
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Designed for students and professionals who are interested in research in human-compatible AI. Interns work on a research project supervised by a mentor.

name: Center on Long-Term Risk: Fellowship
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. 2-3 month summer research fellowship in London working on challenging research questions relevant to reducing suffering in the long-term future.

name: Foresight Fellowship
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. 1-year program catalyzing collaboration among young scientists, engineers, and innovators working to advance technologies for the benefit of life.

name: IAPS AI Policy Fellowship
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Fully-funded, 3-month program by the Institute for AI Policy and Strategy (IAPS) for professionals seeking to strengthen practical policy skills for managing the challenges of advanced AI.

name: London AI Safety Research Labs
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. 3-month program where participants work in teams of 3–4, supervised by an experienced AI safety researcher, to write an academic-style paper.

name: Mentorship for Alignment Research Students
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Part-time research program run by Cambridge AI Safety Hub (CAISH), connecting aspiring researchers with experienced mentors to conduct AI safety research for 2–3 months.

name: ML Alignment & Theory Scholars
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Research program connecting talented scholars with top mentors in AI safety. Involves 12 weeks onsite mentored research in Berkeley, and, if selected, 6–12 months extended research.

name: ML4Good
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. 8-day intensive in-person bootcamps upskilling participants in technical AI safety research, held in various locations around the world.

name: Orion AI Governance Initiative
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. London-based talent development scheme designed to equip outstanding students with the knowledge and skills to shape the future of AI governance.

name: PIBBSS Fellowship
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. 3-month interdisciplinary program connecting researchers from diverse fields with AI safety mentors, in order to help them transition their career to AI safety.

name: Supervised Program for Alignment Research
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. 3-month, part-time research program run by Kairos where mentees collaborate with researchers on impactful projects, with funding provided for compute and project expenses.

name: Talos Fellowship
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. 7-month program for graduates to launch EU policy careers reducing risks from AI. Comprised of an online reading group, a Brussels policymaking summit, and optional paid placement.

name: Technical Alignment Research Accelerator
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. 14-week part-time program building technical AI safety research skills through the ARENA curriculum. Consists of weekly in-person learning sessions followed by a 3-week project.

name: UChicago Existential Risk Laboratory Fellowship
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. 10-week, in-person summer research fellowship giving students the opportunity to produce high impact research on various emerging threats, including from AI.

name: 80,000 Hours Problem Profile
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Regularly-updated article with motivation and advice around pursuing a career in AI safety.

name: Apart Sprints
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Short hackathons and challenges, both online and in-person around the world, focused on important questions in AI safety.

name: Pathfinder Fellowship
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Fellowship run by Kairos supporting university organizers working on AI safety groups by offering funding, 1-on-1 mentorship, resources, and ecosystem connections.

name: Stanford RegLab
notes_head: Stanford RegLab (Regulation, Evaluation, and Governance Lab) partners with government agencies to design and evaluate programs, policies, and technologies that modernize government. The organization uses machine learning and data science to improve compliance, mass adjudication, policy making, and government services. RegLab has an extensive track record working with local and national government agencies including the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), Internal Revenue Service (IRS), Santa Clara County Public Health Department, and others. Daniel E. Ho is the Faculty Director of RegLab an

name: Wild Dolphin Project
notes_head: Founded in 1985 by Dr. Denise Herzing, conducting the world's longest-running underwater dolphin research project studying Atlantic spotted dolphins in the Bahamas. Collaborates with Georgia Tech and Google on AI-powered dolphin communication research, including the DolphinGemma model and CHAT (Cetacean Hearing Augmentation Telemetry) system. Has 40 years of underwater video and audio data from wild dolphin communities, funded by grants including Templeton World Charity Foundation.

name: IIT Madras
notes_head: Houses multiple AI research centers including RBCDSAI (founded 2017), CeRAI for Responsible AI, and AI4Bharat for Indian language AI. Prof. B. Ravindran chairs government AI governance committee and serves on UN AI panel. Wadhwani School of Data Science and AI leads national AI policy discussions with 'whole-of-government' approach recommendations.

name: Fred Hutchinson Cancer Center
notes_head: Founded 1975, Fred Hutchinson Cancer Center is a nonprofit research institution with 3,676 employees and $600M annual revenue. They coordinate the Cancer AI Alliance (CAIA) launched in 2024 with Dana-Farber, Memorial Sloan Kettering, and Johns Hopkins, backed by $40M+ from AWS, Microsoft, NVIDIA, and Deloitte. The center actively deploys AI tools including GI Genius for colonoscopy analysis and DataVoyager for clinical research, while maintaining strict data protection protocols for patient health information.

name: Stanford CRFM
notes_head: Stanford Center for Research on Foundation Models (CRFM), founded 2021 as part of Stanford HAI and led by Percy Liang. Coined the term ''foundation models'' and publishes influential work on model evaluation (HELM), transparency (Foundation Model Transparency Index), and governance. One of the most cited academic sources in U.S. AI policy debates.

name: Stanford Digital Economy Lab
notes_head: Stanford Digital Economy Lab, directed by Erik Brynjolfsson, researches the economic effects of AI on labor, productivity, and inequality. In Dec 2025 it published The Digitalist Papers Vol. 2 on transformative AI economics. Research shows early evidence of AI displacing entry-level workers, informing targeted labor and technology policy recommendations.

name: Stanford SIEPR
notes_head: Stanford Institute for Economic Policy Research (SIEPR) is Stanford''s flagship economic-policy center. While not AI-specific, it hosts significant AI-and-labor and AI-and-productivity research (including Erik Brynjolfsson''s Digital Economy Lab) and serves as a venue for economists engaging AI''s macroeconomic and workforce implications.

name: MIT Shaping the Future of Work
notes_head: MIT Shaping the Future of Work Initiative, co-led by Daron Acemoglu, David Autor, and Simon Johnson, studies how technology — particularly AI — shapes labor markets, wages, and inequality. A key academic source arguing for pro-worker AI policy, featuring prominently in congressional testimony and policy debates on AI''s employment impact.

name: MIT CSAIL
notes_head: MIT's Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory, directed by Daniela Rus, is one of the world's largest academic AI research labs. In 2025 CSAIL submitted formal AI Action Plan recommendations to the White House, advocating for sustained federal investment in basic AI research and maintaining U.S. strategic leadership.

name: MIT GOV/LAB
notes_head: MIT political science lab founded by Lily Tsai in 2014. Develops and tests innovations in citizen engagement and government accountability. Active AI work includes research on generative AI and democracy, a deliberation.io online platform, and a mini-syllabus on AI/ML and governance. Interested in the impact of AI on trust between citizens and governments.

name: MIT Media Lab
notes_head: MIT Media Lab is an interdisciplinary research lab at MIT funded primarily by 80+ corporate sponsors (~$45M/year budget). In 2025 it launched the AHA (Advancing Humans with AI) program studying human-AI interaction and promoting AI design that preserves human agency. It collaborates with OpenAI and publishes empirical research on AI's cognitive and social effects.

name: Princeton CITP
notes_head: Princeton University's Center for Information Technology Policy (CITP) is a leading interdisciplinary research center on technology and public policy. Under director Arvind Narayanan, it publishes the 'AI Snake Oil' critique of AI hype, runs AI Policy Precepts briefings for federal officials, and operates the Freedom to Tinker blog.

name: Harvard Ash Center
notes_head: Ash Center for Democratic Governance; Danielle Allen

name: Harvard Berkman Klein Center
notes_head: Harvard research center on internet and society. Active AI governance research covering agentic AI, algorithmic accountability, and LLM transparency. Funded by foundations including MacArthur and Knight; no current corporate funding.

name: UW Tech Policy Lab
notes_head: University of Washington Tech Policy Lab is an interdisciplinary research unit bridging the UW School of Law, the Information School, and the Paul G. Allen School of Computer Science. Co-founded by Ryan Calo, it produces research on robotics law, algorithmic accountability, and emerging-technology policy, and is an active voice in Pacific Northwest tech-policy discourse.

name: Creative Destruction Lab
notes_head: Seed-stage science and technology accelerator founded 2012 at Rotman School of Management, U of Toronto, by Ajay Agrawal, Joshua Gans, and Avi Goldfarb. Pioneered the first university AI startup stream; now operates 30+ global programs. CDL companies have created $51B+ CAD in equity value.

name: EconTAI
notes_head: EconTAI (Economics of Transformative AI) is a University of Virginia research group led by economist Anton Korinek focused on the macroeconomic, distributional, and welfare implications of transformative AI, including automation of cognitive labor, wage dynamics, and post-AGI economic design.

name: Stanford University
notes_head: Stanford University was founded in 1885 by Leland and Jane Stanford as a tribute to the memory of their only child, Leland Stanford Jr. The university officially opened in 1891 on the Stanfords' former Palo Alto farm. Stanford is organized around seven schools of study on an 8,180-acre campus, one of the largest in the nation. The university houses multiple AI-focused institutions including the Stanford Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (SAIL), founded in 1962, and the Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence (HAI), founded in 2019.

name: Massachusetts Institute of Technology
notes_head: The Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) is a private research university in Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States, founded in 1861. MIT's mission is to advance knowledge and educate students in science, technology, and other areas of scholarship that will best serve the nation and the world in the 21st century. The Institute has 11,886 total students as of 2024-2025, including 4,535 undergraduates and 7,351 graduate students. MIT employs 11,147 people and has an annual revenue of $3.9B.

name: Harvard University
notes_head: Harvard University was founded in 1636 and is the oldest institution of higher learning in the United States. The university is devoted to excellence in teaching, learning, and research, and to developing leaders in many disciplines who make a difference globally. Harvard employs 12,899 people and has an annual revenue of $6.1B. The university operates in 134 countries including United Kingdom, India, Canada, Brazil, and China.

name: Princeton University
notes_head: Princeton University is chartered in 1746, making it the fourth-oldest college in the United States. The University advances learning through scholarship, research, and teaching of unsurpassed quality, with an emphasis on undergraduate and doctoral education. Princeton employs 7,123 people and has an annual revenue of $3.6B. The Princeton Laboratory for Artificial Intelligence supports and expands the scope of AI research at Princeton, adopting a new organizational model designed to accommodate the challenges of working in this fast-moving field.

name: University of Cambridge
notes_head: The University of Cambridge is one of the world's oldest universities and leading academic centres, established in 1209. The University comprises 31 autonomous Colleges and over 100 departments, faculties and institutions. Its 25,000 students include around 11,000 international students. The University has an annual income of £2 billion.

name: University of Oxford
notes_head: The University of Oxford is described as "the oldest university in the English-speaking world" and "a world-leading centre of learning, teaching and research". Oxford has announced plans to expand its artificial intelligence capabilities through a five-year collaboration with OpenAI, providing students and faculty access to research grant funding, enterprise-level security and cutting-edge AI tools. The university is ranked number one in the world in the 2025 Times Higher Education World Rankings and is at the forefront of academic disciplines including medical sciences, mathematical and physi

name: UC Berkeley
notes_head: UC Berkeley is a public research university founded in 1868 and headquartered in Berkeley, California. The university employs 13,995 people with an annual revenue of $2.4B and operates in 83 countries. UC Berkeley is a leading institution in artificial intelligence research, hosting multiple AI-focused research centers including the Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research (BAIR) Lab, which brings together over 50 faculty and 300+ graduate students across areas like computer vision, machine learning, natural language processing, planning, control, and robotics. The university has nearly 50 gr

name: New York University
notes_head: New York University is a private research university in New York City, United States, chartered in 1831 and founded in 1832 by Albert Gallatin. NYU is one of the largest private universities in the United States by enrollment, with a total of 51,848 enrolled students in 2021. The university has degree-granting campuses in New York, Abu Dhabi, and Shanghai, with 12 global academic centers. NYU is ranked among the top institutions for artificial intelligence research, placing no. 15 worldwide according to the Nature Index 2024 AI ranking.

name: University of Virginia
notes_head: The University of Virginia is a public research university located in Charlottesville City and Albemarle County in Virginia, United States. It was founded in 1819 by Thomas Jefferson and contains his Academical Village, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The university has been a member of the Association of American Universities since 1904. UVA's Board of Visitors approved the new LaCross Institute for Ethical Artificial Intelligence in Business at UVA's Darden School of Business.

name: Vanderbilt University
notes_head: Vanderbilt University was founded in 1873 by Cornelius Vanderbilt, who gave $1 million to endow and build the university. The university is a private research university with approximately 6,900 undergraduates and more than 6,200 graduate and professional students. Vanderbilt has launched the Amplify Generative AI Innovation Center within the College of Connected Computing, which is co-led by Professor Jules White and Chief AI and Technology Officer Allen Karns. The university's core purpose is to create, preserve, and disseminate knowledge through teaching, research, and the arts.

name: University College London
notes_head: UCL was chosen as the sole UK partner in NVIDIA's Europe-wide initiative to help develop sovereign AI platforms, building on its strong existing relationship with NVIDIA and ongoing work creating a UK designed and trained large language model called BritLLM. UCL leads the EPSRC AI hub in Generative Models with £80 million investment, developing tools for responsible generative AI that will benefit the economy and society. UCL maintains strong partnerships with leading AI firms such as NVIDIA, Darktrace, Google DeepMind and Quantexa, as well as with the UK's AI Safety Institute. UCL is consiste

name: Brown University
notes_head: Brown University is a leading nonprofit research university founded in 1764 and located in Providence, Rhode Island. The university has 7,272 undergraduate students, 3,130 graduate students, and 866 regular faculty. Brown received a $20 million grant from the U.S. National Science Foundation to lead the AI Research Institute on Interaction for AI Assistants (ARIA) focused on developing trustworthy AI assistants for mental and behavioral health applications. The university operates with a Fiscal Year 2025 budget of $1.79 billion in revenues and $1.83 billion in expenditures.

name: Columbia University
notes_head: Columbia University offers a Master of Science in Artificial Intelligence program that combines core AI courses in Computer Science and Engineering with comprehensive concentrations. The university leads a $20 million NSF-funded AI Institute for Artificial and Natural Intelligence (ARNI), collaborating with academic and industry partners. Columbia operates the Center of Artificial Intelligence Technology in collaboration with Amazon, guided by the Engineering for Humanity vision. The university was founded as King's College in 1754 and renamed Columbia University in the City of New York in 189

name: University of Washington
notes_head: The University of Washington is comprised of three campuses: the Seattle campus with sixteen schools and colleges, plus the Bothell and Tacoma campuses. The primary mission of the University of Washington is the preservation, advancement, and dissemination of knowledge. The UW has been recognized as the #1 most innovative public university in the world and receives more federal research dollars than any other U.S. public university. The University was founded on November 4, 1861, making it one of the oldest state-supported institutions of higher education on the Pacific coast.

name: MIT Sloan
notes_head: MIT Sloan is the MIT Sloan School of Management, located in Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States. The school employs 1,398 people and operates in 49 countries. Richard M. Locke is the John C Head III Dean at MIT Sloan, beginning his appointment as dean in July 2025. MIT Sloan offers various programs including MBA, Executive Education, and specialized programs like the MIT Sloan Fellows MBA Program.

name: Mila
notes_head: Mila is the Quebec Artificial Intelligence Institute, recognized worldwide for its major contributions to AI and described as the world's largest academic research center for deep learning. The institute is a community of more than 1,400 researchers specializing in machine learning and dedicated to scientific excellence and innovation. Mila is the result of a unique collaboration between Université de Montréal and McGill University, in close collaboration with Polytechnique Montréal and HEC Montréal, and also hosts professors from several other universities. Founded by Professor Yoshua Bengio

name: National Bureau of Economic Research (NBER)
notes_head: The National Bureau of Economic Research (NBER) is a private, nonprofit organization that facilitates cutting-edge research on and analysis of major economic issues, founded in New York City in 1920 and today headquartered in Cambridge, Massachusetts. The NBER is a network of more than 1,800 academic economists, with 43 current or former NBER affiliates having been awarded the Nobel Prize in Economic Sciences, and 13 have chaired the President's Council of Economic Advisers. The organization recently conducted a study surveying nearly 6,000 top executives across the US, UK, Germany and Austral

name: DeepLearning.AI
notes_head: DeepLearning.AI was founded in 2017 by Andrew Ng, a global leader in AI. The company is an education technology company that empowers the global workforce to build an AI-powered future through world-class education, hands-on training, and a collaborative community. DeepLearning.AI is making a world-class AI education accessible to people around the globe. The company is headquartered in Mountain View, California and employs 237 people.

name: Coursera
notes_head: Coursera was founded by Stanford University professors Andrew Ng and Daphne Koller in 2012. The company has grown to serve 197 million registered learners as of December 31, 2025. Coursera partners with over 375 leading university and industry partners to offer courses, specializations, professional certificates, and degrees. The company went public on the New York Stock Exchange in March 2021.

name: University of Toronto
notes_head: The University of Toronto is described as a world-leader in Artificial Intelligence (AI) research, including machine and deep learning, natural language processing, and computer vision. The university has established the Hinton Chair in Artificial Intelligence with $10 million in funding from Google, with U of T matching Google's support with an additional $10 million in funding. U of T ranked 21st globally in the Times Higher Education World University Rankings 2025 and is the top-ranked university in Canada. The university was founded in 1827 and has three campuses: St. George, Mississauga,

name: Carnegie Mellon University
notes_head: Carnegie Mellon University was founded in 1900 by philanthropist Andrew Carnegie who donated $1 million to establish a school where students from all backgrounds could gain skills to uplift their careers and communities. The university was originally called Carnegie Technical Schools (1900-1912), then Carnegie Institute of Technology (1912-1967), and became Carnegie Mellon University in 1967 when Carnegie Tech merged with the Mellon Institute. Carnegie Mellon is described as the birthplace of AI over half a century ago and maintains the world's #1 ranked AI program.

name: Georgetown University
notes_head: Georgetown University is a Catholic and Jesuit, student-centered research university established in 1789. The university offers an online Master of Professional Studies in Artificial Intelligence Management through Georgetown SCS, which is a 30-credit program with tuition of $52,560. Georgetown has adopted Google Gemini for students, faculty and staff as part of its AI initiatives. The university has more than 22,000 undergraduate and graduate students who take classes at five locations: Main Campus, Medical Center, Law Center, School of Continuing Studies, and the university's Georgetown Qata

name: Yale University
notes_head: Yale University was founded in 1701 and is committed to improving the world today and for future generations through outstanding research and scholarship, education, preservation, and practice. Yale announced over $150 million in funding over five years to support faculty, students, and staff as they engage with artificial intelligence. The university is located in New Haven, Connecticut and has approximately 15,564 total students including 6,814 undergraduates and 8,750 graduate students. Yale's mission includes educating aspiring leaders worldwide who serve all sectors of society through the

name: University of Michigan
notes_head: The University of Michigan is a public research university in Ann Arbor, Michigan, United States, founded in 1817 and is the oldest institution of higher education in the state. The university reported record total enrollment of 52,855 students for fall 2024, with undergraduate enrollment of 34,454 students. U-M has annual research volume that exceeds $1.86 billion and employs 20,482 people. The university received a record number of applications for fall 2025 with more than 115,000 prospective first-year and transfer students.

name: UCLA
notes_head: UCLA has an AI Innovation Initiative that is a campus-wide effort to build AI fluency, foster innovation, and drive responsible AI adoption. The initiative includes strategic partnerships with leaders like OpenAI and Google to empower faculty, staff, and students. UCLA's primary purpose as a public research university is the creation, dissemination, preservation and application of knowledge for the betterment of our global society. The university operates on a quarter system with Fall, Winter, Spring quarters and Summer sessions.

name: Johns Hopkins University
notes_head: Johns Hopkins University offers multiple artificial intelligence programs including an online Master of Science in Artificial Intelligence developed with expertise from the Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Lab. The university also has a Johns Hopkins Data Science and AI Institute that focuses on research and education in data science, machine learning, and artificial intelligence across diverse fields. The university's mission is to educate students and cultivate their capacity for lifelong learning, to foster independent and original research, and to bring the benefits of discovery to the world.

name: University of Louisville
notes_head: The University of Louisville offers multiple artificial intelligence programs including a Graduate Certificate in Artificial Intelligence in Medicine and a Master of Science in Artificial Intelligence in Medicine. The university was established in 1798 and is a state supported research university located in Kentucky's largest metropolitan area. As of Fall 2024, the university has a total student enrollment of 24,123 with faculty and staff totaling 7,231. The university operates with an annual revenue of $1.3 billion and has three campuses including the 287-acre Belknap Campus.

name: American University
notes_head: American University is a private federally chartered research university in Washington, D.C., chartered by Congress in 1893. The university consists of eight schools and colleges, including the Kogod School of Business, School of International Service, College of Arts and Sciences, School of Communication, School of Professional and Extended Studies, School of Public Affairs, Linda A. and H. Kent Baker School of Education, and Washington College of Law. American University has 13,019 students as of fall 2023 and offers over 160 academic programs. The Kogod School of Business has launched the I

name: SUNY Albany
notes_head: SUNY Albany is also known as University at Albany, UAlbany, or SUNY Albany and is a public research university in Albany, New York, United States. The university was founded in 1844 and is one of four "university centers" of the State University of New York (SUNY) system. In 2024, the university enrolled 17,567 students in nine schools and colleges, which offer over 50 undergraduate majors and over 150 graduate programs. The university is classified among "R1: Doctoral Universities – Very high research spending and doctorate production".

name: USC
notes_head: USC is a private university founded in 1880 that is home to more than 48,000 students and over 4,400 full-time faculty. USC launched the Frontiers of Computing initiative in 2023, a $1B-plus initiative for advanced computation, quantum computing, AI and ethics as the largest, most comprehensive academic initiative in USC's history. USC has multiple AI research centers and initiatives, including the USC Center for AI in Society founded in 2016, which is a collaboration between USC's Suzanne Dworak-Peck School of Social Work and Viterbi School of Engineering. The university provides every studen

name: Wharton School
notes_head: The Wharton School of the University of Pennsylvania was founded in 1881 as the world's first collegiate business school. It has more than 235 renowned professors and 5,000 undergraduate, MBA, executive MBA, and doctoral students. In April 2025, Wharton announced a new undergraduate concentration and MBA major in Artificial Intelligence for Business. The school is led by Dean Erika James.

name: Tulane University
notes_head: Tulane University is a private research university in New Orleans, Louisiana, United States that was founded as the Medical College of Louisiana in 1834 by a cohort of medical doctors. The institution became private under the endowments of Paul Tulane and Josephine Louise Newcomb in 1884 and 1887. Tulane has been a member of the Association of American Universities since 1958 and is classified among "R1: Doctoral Universities – Very high research activity". The university has established multiple AI-related initiatives including the Connolly Alexander Institute for Data Science, the Jurist Cen

name: University of California, San Francisco
notes_head: The University of California, San Francisco (UCSF or UC San Francisco) is a public land-grant research university in San Francisco, California, United States that is part of the University of California system and is dedicated entirely to health science and life science. UCSF was founded as Toland Medical College in 1864, became affiliated with the University of California as its medical department in 1873, gained full administrative independence as a campus of the UC system in 1964, and gained its current name in 1970. UCSF's mission is to advance health worldwide through preeminent biomedica

name: Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research
notes_head: The Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research (BAIR) Lab brings together UC Berkeley researchers across the areas of computer vision, machine learning, natural language processing, planning, and robotics. BAIR includes over two dozen faculty and more than a hundred graduate students pursuing research on fundamental advances in the above areas as well as cross-cutting themes including multi-modal deep learning, human-compatible AI, and connecting AI with other scientific disciplines and the humanities. The lab was established in 1990 as part of the University of California, Berkeley. BAIR opera

name: Berkeley Institute for Data Science
notes_head: The Berkeley Institute for Data Science (BIDS) is a central hub of data-intensive research, open source software, and data science training programs at UC Berkeley that launched in 2013. BIDS is an integral part of UC Berkeley's College of Computing, Data Science, and Society (CDSS) and was made possible by grants from the Gordon and Betty Moore Foundation and the Alfred P. Sloan Foundation. The Institute is one of three Moore-Sloan Data Science Environments along with data science organizations at the University of Washington and New York University. BIDS is currently led by Fernando Pérez as

name: Université de Montréal
notes_head: Université de Montréal is a French-language institution of higher learning founded in 1878, regarded as one of the most important universities in Canada in terms of research and one of the largest by enrolment. The university has 16 faculties and two affiliated schools (Polytechnique Montréal and HEC Montréal), with an enrollment of nearly 67,600 students in over 600 programs. UdeM ranked 9th globally in Artificial Intelligence research according to the Shanghai Academic Ranking of World Universities in 2026. The university has an annual revenue of $212.0M and employs 7,578 people.

name: Mila - Quebec Artificial Intelligence Institute
notes_head: Mila - Quebec Artificial Intelligence Institute is the world's largest academic AI research center with over 1,400 researchers and 140+ professors. Founded in 1993 by Yoshua Bengio at Université de Montréal, it is an independent nonprofit research organization dedicated to advancing artificial intelligence through fundamental research and talent development. The institute is recognized worldwide for its major contributions to AI, particularly in deep learning, language modeling, machine translation, object recognition and generative models. Mila is part of the Pan-Canadian Artificial Intellige

name: McGill University
notes_head: McGill University was founded in 1821 from a generous bequest by James McGill, a prominent Scottish merchant. The university offers an Applied Artificial Intelligence Minor in the Faculty of Engineering that provides foundation for applying AI techniques in various fields. McGill has several AI-related organizations including the McGill Centre for Intelligent Machines, which has been a world leader in research on intelligent systems since 1985. The McGill Collaborative for AI and Society (McCAIS) aims to understand the impact of AI on society and develop evidence-based best practices for its r

name: Stanford Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (SAIL)
notes_head: One of the oldest AI research labs, founded at Stanford in 1962 by John McCarthy. Research spans NLP, computer vision, robotics, reinforcement learning, and computational biomedicine. In 2025, Carlos Guestrin became Director and SAIL formally joined forces with Stanford HAI, integrating safety and policy work alongside core capabilities research.

name: MIT Data to AI Lab
notes_head: MIT research group within the Laboratory for Information and Decision Systems (LIDS), founded in 2015 by Principal Research Scientist Kalyan Veeramachaneni. Develops large-scale AI and ML systems that work alongside humans in applications spanning healthcare, finance, education, and energy. Notable for the Synthetic Data Vault open-source project, spun out commercially as DataCebo.

name: MIT Schwarzman College of Computing
notes_head: MIT's cross-disciplinary computing college founded in 2018 with a $1B endowment anchored by a $350M gift from Stephen Schwarzman. Led by founding dean Daniel Huttenlocher, it integrates AI research across all MIT departments. Hosts the MIT AI Policy Forum and publishes governance-focused policy briefs; advocates for technically-informed, sector-specific AI regulation.

name: Kempner Institute
notes_head: Harvard University-wide institute for the study of natural and artificial intelligence, founded 2022 with $500M gift from Mark Zuckerberg and Priscilla Chan (Chan Zuckerberg Initiative). Co-directed by Sham Kakade (CS) and Bernardo Sabatini (neurobiology). Operates the Kempner AI Cluster at MGHPCC (1,144 H100 GPUs as of March 2026, ranked 85th on TOP500 in 2024). Research spans mathematical models of intelligence, cognitive theories, neurobiological basis of intelligence, and AI applications in science. Supports undergraduate research (KURE program), postdocs, and 10 new faculty lines. Sits at

name: The Princeton Laboratory for Artificial Intelligence
notes_head: Princeton's AI Lab supports and expands AI research across the university with a novel organizational model. Directed by Tom Griffiths; associate director Olga Russakovsky. Supports three research initiatives: Princeton Language and Intelligence (large AI models, led by Sanjeev Arora), AI for Accelerating Invention (AI^2, AI in science/engineering), and Natural and Artificial Minds (NAM, human-AI interrelationship). Shared infrastructure includes admin staff, AI Postdoctoral Fellows, research software engineers, data scientists, and a compute cluster with 300 H100 GPUs. Faculty span natural sc

name: ai@cam
notes_head: University of Cambridge flagship mission on AI, led by Neil Lawrence (DeepMind Professor of Machine Learning). Aims to develop AI-enabled innovation that benefits science, citizens, and society by leveraging Cambridge's interdisciplinary research community. Focuses on responsible AI development requiring expertise from research, business, policy, civil society, and affected communities. Engages in public dialogue on AI governance; research has shown public desire for AI systems with meaningful human oversight, transparency, accountability, and protection from harms.

name: Saïd Business School
notes_head: Business school of the University of Oxford. Offers a portfolio of AI-focused executive education programs: Oxford Artificial Intelligence Programme, Oxford Executive Diploma in AI for Business (master's-level), Oxford Leading AI Implementation Programme, and Oxford Generative and Agentic AI Programme (directed by Matthias Holweg and Mike Wooldridge). Draws on Oxford's research in multi-agent architectures, operational design, and responsible AI practice. Focused on business leaders understanding AI workings, possibilities, ethics, and implementation strategy.

name: Darden School of Business
notes_head: University of Virginia's graduate business school. Home to the LaCross Institute for Ethical Artificial Intelligence in Business, launched Sept 2024 with a $62M gift from the LaCross family (one of the largest AI gifts to any business school globally, from a broader $101M commitment). Academic directors: Professors Yael Grushka-Cockayne and Raj Venkatesan. The institute coordinates AI research, teaching, and engagement across UVA schools. Extends Darden's AI Initiative (est. 2022) and the Collaboratory for Applied Data Science in Business (partnership with UVA School of Data Science, 2021). Of

name: UVA Information Technology Services
notes_head: University of Virginia's central IT organization. Manages UVA's AI tools and responsible use guidelines. Provides UVA Copilot (enterprise GenAI tool operating within UVA's secure environment — data not retained or used for model training, approved for up to Sensitive data). Publishes AI guidelines including prohibition on DeepSeek AI applications on university devices/networks. Maintains the AI Tools Hub with responsible use resources. Supports the university's broader GenAI in Teaching and Learning initiative. Enforces data protection standards: highly sensitive data (PII, PHI) must never be

name: College of Connected Computing
notes_head: Vanderbilt University's College of Connected Computing (CCC), established March 2024, is the university's first new college since 1981. Dedicated to computer science, AI, data science, cybersecurity, cyber-physical systems/IoT, and robotics. Inaugural dean Matthew Johnson-Roberson (appointed May 2025) came from directing CMU's Robotics Institute. Houses the Amplify Generative AI Innovation Center (launched Feb 2026). Undergraduate programs launch fall 2027; graduate programs began fall 2026. Developing a generative AI minor (expected spring 2026). Aims to bridge computing with other discipline

name: University of North Carolina system
notes_head: Multi-campus public university system in North Carolina comprising 16 institutions and 240,000+ students. Pursuing systemwide AI integration: partnered with Google ($1B initiative across 100+ public universities) giving all students access to Gemini 2.5 Pro and Google Career Certificates. Launched a systemwide AI Skills Module and AI Advisory Council. UNC-Chapel Hill established Provost's AI Committee and AI Acceleration Program (Sept 2024) with five subcommittees. NC A&T won approval for the state's only standalone B.S. in Artificial Intelligence. Individual campuses running AI accelerators,

name: Dartmouth College
notes_head: Private Ivy League research university in Hanover, NH. Birthplace of artificial intelligence — hosted the 1956 Dartmouth Summer Research Project where the term 'artificial intelligence' was coined. Expanding AI across curriculum: new AI track in Master of Engineering, undergraduate AI concentration in B.E., and multiple AI electives in Tuck MBA (AI-Driven Analytics, AI for Managers, NLP/ML in Finance, AI & Ethics, AI for the C-Suite). Partnered with Anthropic and AWS (Dec 2025) for AI-enhanced career coaching, student access to Claude, and innovation programs. Launched generative AI teaching g

name: Baylor College of Medicine
notes_head: Baylor College of Medicine is a leading academic medical center in Houston, TX. In AI policy, BCM focuses on ethical and responsible AI in healthcare through its Center for Medical Ethics and Health Policy. Co-leads the NEH-funded Center for Humanities-based Health AI Innovation (CHHAIN) with Rice University ($500K grant), exploring how humanities-based insights can shape responsible health AI. Hosted an AI and Health Equity Symposium with Rice. Conducts stakeholder focus group research on ethical dilemmas of AI integration in clinical settings. Emphasis is on patient safety, health equity, an

name: City University of New York
notes_head: CUNY is the largest urban public university system in the U.S. (25 campuses). Awarded $3M via AI Innovation Fund (part of Governor Hochul's NY AI initiative) to 113 campus-led AI projects. Strategy spans five pillars: Education, Workforce, Operations, Research & Innovation, and Partnerships. Received $1M Google.org grant to advance AI literacy frameworks. Developing school-level AI Policy Frameworks for ethical, equitable AI use. Emphasis is on AI literacy, workforce readiness, and equitable access rather than external regulatory positions. Launched AI Academic Hub and new three-year AI curric

name: Allen Institute for AI
notes_head: Allen Institute for AI (Ai2) is a Seattle-based nonprofit AI research lab founded by Paul Allen in 2014, known for its fully open-source OLMo language models. Ai2 advocates for open AI research and transparency, and shapes policy on open-source AI, copyright, and AI safety. CEO Ali Farhadi stepped down in early 2026.

name: Polytechnique Montréal
notes_head: French-language engineering school in Montreal and a founding partner of both IVADO and Mila — Quebec's premier AI research institutes. Its researchers work on AI applications in health, transportation, and cybersecurity, supported by $20M+ in grants from CFI and the Quebec government.

name: HEC Montréal
notes_head: Montreal business school and founding partner of IVADO and Mila. Established a Chair in Organizational Ethics and AI Governance in 2024-25 and, with Tech3Lab and IVADO, launched a government-funded AI professional training program in January 2026. Faculty contribute to Mila and AI governance research.

name: People + AI Research
notes_head: Google's People + AI Research (PAIR) team, a multidisciplinary research group exploring the human side of AI. Launched in 2017 within Google Research; now part of the Responsible AI and Human-Centered Technology team. Conducts research on explainability, interpretability, fairness, data visualization, and human-centered AI. Publishes the PAIR Guidebook (design guidelines for AI interfaces) and develops open-source tools like the Learning Interpretability Tool (LIT). Produces educational materials (Explorables) to help practitioners understand ML behaviors. Focuses on making AI systems more und

name: NYU School of Medicine
notes_head: NYU Grossman School of Medicine at NYU Langone Health, a leading academic medical center. Notable for the fastMRI project—a collaboration with Facebook AI Research (FAIR) using deep learning to accelerate MRI reconstruction 10x. Also developed NYUTron, an LLM trained on clinical notes for in-hospital risk prediction.

name: University of Pittsburgh
notes_head: Major public research university in Pittsburgh. Close collaboration with Carnegie Mellon University on AI initiatives. Joint NVIDIA AI Tech Community with CMU: Pitt's center focuses on AI in health sciences, clinical medicine, and biomanufacturing. Co-hosted CMU-Pitt health/AI/technology summit (Oct 2025). Joined multi-institutional Sloan Foundation study on AI impact on labor markets (Jul 2025). Research strengths in health AI and computational medicine.

name: Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Laboratory
notes_head: Largest university-affiliated research center in the U.S., serving as a technical resource for DoD, Intelligence Community, DHS, and NASA. Over 60 years of AI R&D spanning autonomous systems, decision support, ML, and human-machine teaming. Intelligent Systems Center (ISC) has 130+ research scientists/engineers focused on AI, robotics, and neuroscience. Developed GenWar Lab (AI wargaming) and SAGE (Strategic AI Gaming Engine) now being built for classified DoD/IC networks. Focus on robust, trustworthy AI for national security.

name: Speed School of Engineering
notes_head: J.B. Speed School of Engineering at the University of Louisville. R1 research university engineering school with AI as a core research area. Houses Data Science & AI research cluster, Center for Human Systems Engineering (human-centric AI), and Autonomous Intelligent Mobile Systems Lab. Offers AI/ML coursework across bioengineering, computer science, and industrial engineering.

name: USC Center for AI in Society
notes_head: Joint research center between USC Suzanne Dworak-Peck School of Social Work and USC Viterbi School of Engineering. Mission: develop and demonstrate how AI can tackle difficult societal problems. Focuses on low-resource communities. Research areas: environment, health/wellbeing, homelessness. Partners with Partnership on AI on explainable AI research. Draws from Grand Challenges for Social Work, NAE Grand Challenges, and UN SDGs.

name: USC Suzanne Dworak-Peck School of Social Work
notes_head: USC's school of social work. Co-founded the Center for AI in Society (CAIS) with Viterbi School of Engineering, applying AI to social challenges including child welfare, homelessness, behavioral health, and human trafficking. Research emphasizes responsible AI for vulnerable populations.

name: USC Viterbi School of Engineering
notes_head: USC's engineering school. Major AI research hub with multiple centers: CAIS (AI for social good), Amazon Center on Secure and Trusted AI, Machine Learning Center, Center for Autonomy and AI, and Robotics and Autonomous Systems Center. Launched first US MS in CS-AI track. Partnered with USC School of Advanced Computing on BS in AI (Fall 2026). NSF-funded ORAI PhD program in AI and operations research.

name: USC Marshall School of Business
notes_head: USC's business school. Houses the Neely Center for Ethical Leadership and Decision Making, which received $4M from Knight Foundation for purpose-driven AI research. Initiatives: AI and social media indices, design codes for AI tools, youth advisory panel, leadership fellowships. Focuses on aligning emerging tech with ethical, human-centered values. Dean Geoff Garrett chairs USC President's AI Strategy Committee.

name: USC School of Advanced Computing
notes_head: USC's 23rd school, launched in 2024 as part of President Folt's $1B+ advanced computing initiative. Led by Prof. Gaurav Sukhatme. Recruiting 30 faculty by 2025, 60 more by 2030. Co-launching BS in AI with Viterbi (Fall 2026 cohort). New Ginsburg Human-Centered Computation Hall. Ethics at its core mission. First new USC school in over a decade.

name: University of Pennsylvania
notes_head: Ivy League research university with major AI research infrastructure. The ASSET Center (Penn Engineering) focuses on AI safety, explainability, and trustworthiness, with NSF funding. The Warren Center for Network and Data Sciences drives interdisciplinary data science research. Penn AI was launched in 2025 to coordinate responsible AI across the university.

name: Jurist Center for Artificial Intelligence
notes_head: AI research center at Tulane University, established by the Harold L. and Heather E. Jurist NC '64 Endowed Fund. Supports research and education in AI, machine learning, and data science with a focus on applications for a healthier, more connected global community. Faculty projects span machine learning, multi-agent systems, computer vision, NLP, visualization, and ethics. Part of Tulane's broader AI ecosystem alongside the Connolly Alexander Institute for Data Science and CEAI.

name: Center for Community-Engaged Artificial Intelligence
notes_head: Multi-disciplinary AI center at Tulane University and one of five Tulane Centers of Excellence. Team of scientists, engineers, students, and community partners dedicated to human-centered AI. Core principle: trust must be earned by designing AI systems that are demonstrably fair, transparent, and accountable, with meaningful community engagement from design through deployment. Cross-disciplinary research, project-based learning, and community outreach across healthcare, economics, education, and criminal justice. Partners with Court Watch NOLA on criminal justice transparency via $1.5M NSF-fun

name: Stanford Law School
notes_head: Home of CodeX (Stanford Center for Legal Informatics) and the Stanford Law AI Initiative co-directed by Prof. Nathaniel Persily. Hosts AI governance courses, the Law Policy Lab practicum on federal AI use in criminal justice, and applied research projects such as ComplianceTwin (2025–2026).

name: College of Computing, Data Science, and Society
notes_head: UC Berkeley college established July 1, 2023 — the first new college at Berkeley in over 50 years. Houses three academic majors: Computer Science, Data Science, and Statistics. Includes the Berkeley Institute for Data Science, Center for Computational Biology, Bakar Institute of Digital Materials for the Planet, and shares EECS with the College of Engineering and the D-Lab with Social Sciences. Parent organization of BAIR-affiliated units. Mission: develop ethics-oriented, accessible curricula in data science, computing, and statistics. The Gateway collaboration hub expected to open spring 202

name: University of Utah
notes_head: University of Utah hosts the One-U Responsible AI Initiative, a $100M transdisciplinary program led by the Scientific Computing and Imaging (SCI) Institute. A $50M AI supercomputer partnership with NVIDIA and HPE is slated to come online in summer 2026. Research focuses on healthcare, societal wellness, and public services.

name: Emory University
notes_head: Private research university in Atlanta, Georgia. Major AI initiative: AI.Humanity, which brings together full university resources to shape the AI revolution for better human health, economic value, and responsible applications. Key AI centers: Center for Artificial Intelligence Learning (AI literacy across campus), Empathetic AI for Health Institute (AI.Health — ML and big data for healthcare transformation), and multiple departmental AI labs. Research spans NLP, computer vision, geospatial AI, health informatics, and AI ethics. Interdisciplinary approach connects AI research with humanities,

name: Emory AI Group
notes_head: Research collective within Emory University encompassing multiple AI-focused labs and faculty across the Department of Computer Science and School of Medicine. Includes the Emory NLP group, AI and Simulation Lab (geospatial AI, computer vision, foundation models), MAIX Lab (health AI), and Cardiothoracic Imaging and AI Lab. Faculty research spans natural language processing, large language models, conversational AI, information retrieval, AI interpretability, and medical imaging AI. Part of the broader AI.Humanity initiative at Emory.

name: Block Center for Technology and Society
notes_head: The Block Center for Technology and Society at Carnegie Mellon University was established in 2019 to study how emerging technologies alter the future of work, how AI and analytics can be harnessed for social good, and how innovation can reduce inequality. Houses the Responsible AI Initiative (RAI), which translates AI research into actionable policy insights promoting fairness, accountability, and transparency. Runs community education programs like 'AI 101' for labor unions, media professionals, and government representatives. Collaborates with MIT FutureTech on labor implications of AI resea

name: Amii
notes_head: The Alberta Machine Intelligence Institute (Amii), one of Canada's three national AI institutes alongside Mila and Vector Institute, funded through the Pan-Canadian AI Strategy. Founded over 20 years ago, headquartered in Edmonton. Mandate to build technical/scientific capacity in machine learning and translate it into commercial applications across Alberta's economic sectors. Key partner in the Canadian AI Safety Institute (CAISI) alongside CIFAR, NRC, Mila, and Vector. Received $5M Google.org grant to establish a national AI literacy consortium across 25 post-secondary institutions reaching

name: Singapore Management University
notes_head: Singapore Management University (SMU) hosts the Centre for AI and Data Governance, established in 2019 at its School of Law. Received a S$4.5M grant from the National Research Foundation and Infocomm Media Development Authority for a five-year Research Programme on the Governance of AI and Data Use, structured around three streams: AI and Society, AI and Industry, and AI and Commercialisation. Publishes research on Singapore's evolving AI governance framework through SSRN. The Centre establishes expert panels drawing from academia, technology industry, and legal professions, with international

name: McGill Centre for Intelligent Machines
notes_head: Established in 1985 as an inter-departmental, inter-faculty research center at McGill University spanning Electrical and Computer Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, and Computer Science. One of the longest-running AI research centers in the world. Comprises ~20 professors and 150 graduate students. Research organized around five themes: Computer Vision, Robotics, Systems and Control, Human-Computer Interaction, and Graphics. Half of CIM professors engaged in AI research including robotic vision and medical image analysis. Notable projects include the Aqua amphibious robot for environmental m

name: McGill Collaborative for AI and Society
notes_head: The McGill Collaborative for AI and Society (McCAIS) is a strategic initiative of the Computational and Data Systems Institute (CDSI) at McGill University. Interdisciplinary, university-wide effort bringing all faculties together to understand AI's societal impact and develop evidence-based best practices for responsible AI use. Work organized around four pillars: Thought Leadership, Research to Action, Experiential Learning Opportunities, and Lifelong Learning. Partners with IVADO (Quebec AI research consortium) on courses in algorithmic fairness, privacy in AI, creativity in AI, and AI in he

name: MIT Jameel Clinic
notes_head: Abdul Latif Jameel Clinic for Machine Learning in Health, founded 2018 as a joint MIT–Community Jameel initiative. Conducts translational AI research across clinical AI, drug discovery, and epidemiology. Developed clinical AI tools MIRAI and SYBIL, and discovered antibiotics halicin and abaucin using AI-driven methods.

name: MIT Computer Science & Artificial Intelligence Laboratory
notes_head: MIT's largest AI research lab, directed by Daniela Rus, covering robotics, systems, and machine learning. In 2025 submitted AI Action Plan recommendations to the White House advocating federal investment in basic research, AI hardware co-design, and ASI-level capabilities. Published the 2025 AI Agent Index assessing transparency of deployed AI systems.

name: Cornell University
notes_head: Major research university with broad AI activities spanning Cornell Tech (NYC), the AI Initiative, and the MacArthur-funded AI, Policy, and Practice (AIPP) initiative focused on AI's societal impacts. In 2025 NSF awarded Cornell a $20M AI Materials Institute, and a new Vice Provost for AI Strategy was named in January 2026.

name: University of Notre Dame
notes_head: Private Catholic research university in Indiana. Major AI initiatives: AI@ND hub, Lucy Family Institute for Data & Society, Scientific AI Initiative. Received $50.8M Lilly Endowment grant for DELTA Network expanding faith-based AI ethics framework. Won $1M Bloomberg Mayors Challenge Award for human-centered AI in public service with City of South Bend. Pete Buttigieg is an alumnus.

name: Stanford Vision and Learning Lab
notes_head: Stanford Vision and Learning Lab (SVL), co-directed by Fei-Fei Li, focuses on computer vision, embodied AI, and robotics. Key projects include BEHAVIOR benchmark for household AI agents and the PAIR group for generalizable robot perception. Li has called for science-based AI governance.

name: Harvard Faculty of Arts and Sciences
notes_head: The primary academic division of Harvard University housing most arts, humanities, and sciences departments. Home to the Kempner Institute for the Study of Natural and Artificial Intelligence (launched 2022), a university-wide initiative with strong FAS ties bridging neuroscience and AI. FAS Research Computing supports the Kempner AI cluster.

name: Indian Institute of Technology, Kanpur
notes_head: Premier Indian technical university (est. 1959). Established Wadhwani School of Advanced AI and Intelligent Systems with Department of Intelligent Systems (2025), admissions starting 2026. Hosts Sustainable Cities Centre of Excellence in AI under Ministry of Education initiative. Research partnerships with TCS (urban planning), IBM (air quality monitoring via AIRAWAT Foundation). Active in AI for sustainable cities, robotics, cybersecurity. Growing AI policy and outreach center.

name: University of Tuebingen
notes_head: German research university and major European AI hub. Home to Tübingen AI Center (co-directed by Philipp Hennig and Matthias Bethge since July 2025) and Cluster of Excellence 'Machine Learning: New Perspectives for Science' (renewed 2025). Partners with Max Planck Institute for Intelligent Systems, ELLIS Institute Tübingen, and Cyber Valley ecosystem. ML Cloud serves 430+ active researchers. Focus areas: machine learning, computer vision, robotics, probabilistic methods.

name: Weizenbaum Institute
notes_head: German research institute for interdisciplinary digitalization research, formally 'Weizenbaum Institute for the Networked Society.' Founded 2017, funded by German Federal Ministry of Education and Research (~€50M over 5 years). Consortium led by Berlin Social Science Center (WZB), includes TU Berlin, FU Berlin, HU Berlin, UdK Berlin, Uni Potsdam, Fraunhofer FOKUS. Studies social impact of digitalization — AI ethics, data governance, platform economies. Named after Joseph Weizenbaum, German-American computer scientist who critiqued uncritical tech adoption.

name: Technische Universität Berlin
notes_head: Major German technical university (TU9 member). ~50 academic chairs in AI and computer science. Hosts BIFOLD (Berlin Institute for Foundations of Learning and Data), one of Germany's six national AI competence centers with permanent federal funding.
