Co-founded Einstein Center Digital Future (ECDF) for digitalization research. Strong socio-technical AI focus — ethics, fairness, explainability. Key partner in Weizenbaum Institute consortium.

name: Universidad de Buenos Aires
notes_head: Argentina's largest public university, accounts for ~40% of national research output. Has AI-focused labs: IALAB (Law Faculty — AI governance, judicial AI guidelines) and LIAA (Applied AI Lab in Computer Science). IALAB published guide on responsible use of generative AI for judiciary, modeled on MIT Task Force approach. Key institution in Latin American AI policy discourse.

name: AT&T Bell Laboratories
notes_head: Legendary corporate research lab (est. 1925). Pioneering AI/ML contributions: Claude Shannon's learning machines (1952), neural network research (1986-1996) including handwritten digit recognition with USPS data, early deep learning work by Yann LeCun on convolutional neural networks. 11 Nobel Prizes, 5 Turing Awards for work done there. In the 1990s, AT&T Labs' AI/ML group was arguably the strongest in the world. LeCun worked as researcher there before moving to NYU and later Facebook/Meta. Now AT&T holds 6th most AI patents among U.S. firms (2025).

name: Collège de France
notes_head: Prestigious French academic institution (est. 1530) in Paris. AI relevance: Yann LeCun held the Annual Chair for Computer Science and Digital Technologies (Chaire Annuelle Informatique et Sciences Numériques) in 2016, delivering inaugural lecture on deep learning. The institution hosts visiting professorships connecting top researchers to French academia. Not an AI-focused institution per se, but its rotating chairs have featured leading AI scientists.

name: Columbia Law School
notes_head: Ivy League law school at Columbia University, New York. Active in AI governance research and education. Faculty-led task force (professors Gillis, Liebman, Talley, Wexler) developing AI curriculum. Key AI faculty: Tim Wu (Julius Silver Professor — competition and tech policy, former Biden White House advisor), Rebecca Wexler (AI and criminal justice/due process). Offers seminar on 'Emerging Law of AI' comparing regulatory approaches across US, EU, and China. Hosted 2023 symposium on new legal frameworks for generative AI revolution. Adopted formal generative AI policy for academic integrity (A

name: Stony Brook University
notes_head: SUNY flagship research university on Long Island, New York. Home to the AI Innovation Institute (AI3) directed by Lav Varshney. Participates in Empire AI — SUNY's multi-campus partnership for AI research and public-good applications. Three faculty received SUNY-IBM AI Research Alliance grants (Zadok, Balasubramanian, Ferdman) for cloud computing, sustainable energy, and supercomputer optimization. Computer science department has active AI/ML, NLP, and computer vision research groups. Governor Hochul proposed Empire AI Beta (2026 State of the State) to scale to world's most advanced academic su

name: Seoul National University
notes_head: South Korea's top research university, located in Seoul. Major AI research hub with 100+ AI professors, 200+ AI application labs, and 2,000+ AI researchers. Houses the Artificial Intelligence Institute (AIIS), selected as core part of Korea's National Strategy AI Development Project. Established Interdisciplinary Program in Artificial Intelligence (IPAI). Key labs: Biointelligence Lab (Prof. Byoung-Tak Zhang — biological/artificial intelligence), Data Science & AI Lab, AI and Biomedical Informatics Lab. Yejin Choi is affiliated with SNU (edge 1609). Strong in NLP, computer vision, and biomedic

name: George Mason University
notes_head: Virginia public research university (est. 1972) with significant AI research infrastructure. Key AI centers: Mason Autonomy and Robotics Center (MARC) for autonomous systems and responsible AI; Center for Mathematics and Artificial Intelligence (CMAI) for applied math/AI research funded by NSF, DOE, DTRA, and DoD. Offers MS in AI. Home to the Mercatus Center, a libertarian-leaning policy think tank with an active AI & Progress Project (led by Dean Ball) advocating light-touch, innovation-first AI governance. GMU's AI policy output tends toward deregulatory/innovation perspectives.

name: University of California, Los Angeles
notes_head: Major public research university and UC system flagship. Launched the AI Innovation Initiative — a campus-wide effort to build AI fluency and drive responsible AI adoption. First California university to partner with OpenAI (Sept 2024), also partnered with Google. Supported 68 AI pilot projects across 27 departments. Maintains multi-layered AI governance structure for responsible, ethical adoption. UCLA Health established the Health AI Council (HAIC) for clinical AI oversight. UCLA Law offers Strategic AI for Legal Professionals executive education. Strong interdisciplinary AI presence across

name: University of Tennessee
notes_head: Public flagship university in Knoxville, Tennessee. Home to the AI Tennessee Initiative — the first statewide AI initiative of its kind in the U.S., founded and directed by Lynne Parker (former White House Deputy CTO and founding NAIIO director). Launched major AI cluster hiring in Foundational AI and Science-Informed AI. CECS houses Tennessee's first applied AI degree program. AI focus areas: smart manufacturing, climate-smart agriculture, precision health, future mobility, and AI for science. Partners with Oak Ridge National Laboratory on computing/AI research.

name: AI Tennessee Initiative
notes_head: First statewide AI initiative in the U.S., launched by the University of Tennessee, Knoxville. Founded and directed by Lynne Parker, who served as White House Deputy CTO and founding director of the National AI Initiative Office (2018-2022) before returning to UT. Coordinates AI research, education, and workforce development across disciplines and institutions statewide. Focus areas: smart manufacturing, climate-smart agriculture and forestry, precision health, future mobility, and AI for science. Offers AI education courses (AI 101, AI 401) and is developing an applied AI degree. Engages acad

name: AI Research Institute on Interaction for AI assistants (ARIA)
notes_head: NSF-funded ($20M) AI Research Institute on Interaction for AI Assistants, headquartered at Brown University. Focuses on developing trustworthy, context-aware AI assistants with specific application to mental and behavioral health. Research themes include AI interpretability, adaptability, and participatory design. Co-directed by Ellie Pavlick and Suresh Venkatasubramanian. One of five national AI institutes funded as part of a $100M NSF initiative (with Capital One and Intel). Brings together researchers in computer science, neuroscience, cognitive science, philosophy, law, and education along

name: University at Buffalo
notes_head: Major SUNY research university with extensive AI research dating back decades (early handwriting recognition system saved USPS hundreds of millions). Home to the Institute for Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (IAD, est. 2021) with 200+ faculty on AI. Hosts Empire AI supercomputing center on North Campus. Home to NSF-funded National AI Institute for Exceptional Education (children with speech-language challenges). Launched seven AI-focused bachelor's degrees and two AI minors (AI ethics; AI, crime and society). Applied AI focus: brain aneurysms, diabetes, deepfakes, manufacturing, disas

name: SUNY
notes_head: Largest integrated public university system in the United States. Central player in New York's AI strategy as founding member of Empire AI. Key initiatives: Center for AI Responsibility and Research at SUNY Binghamton (nation's first independent AI research center at a public university, $30M gift + $25M SUNY investment); 'AI for the Public Good' research program; revised General Education Framework includes AI literacy (effective Fall 2026). Hosts Empire AI SUNY Campus Partnerships expanding AI access across system. Strategic plan (SUNY STRIVE) includes AI as priority research area: responsib

name: Public Interest Technology University Network
notes_head: University consortium created in 2019 by New America's Public Interest Technology program, co-convened with the Ford Foundation and Hewlett Foundation. 64 member institutions committed to training civic-minded technologists. Deployed $14M through Network Challenges and RFPs for experiential learning, faculty development, career pathways, and inclusive tech governance. Develops curricula, research agendas, and experiential learning programs. Focuses on ensuring technology design and governance includes communities traditionally excluded from tech. Note: category 'VC/Capital/Philanthropy' reflec

name: Association for Computing Machinery
notes_head: World's largest educational and scientific computing society, founded 1947. Over 100,000 members across 170+ countries. Operates the US Technology Policy Committee (USTPC, 170+ members) providing nonpartisan, technology-neutral AI policy recommendations to lawmakers. Published TechBriefs on 'Trusted AI' and 'Automated Vehicles'; issued formal comments on the US AI Action Plan (2025), OMB AI governance frameworks, and EU AI Act. USTPC advocates for clear documentation, rigorous evaluation standards, transparency, and effective oversight of AI systems. Also warned about manipulative potential of

name: Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI)
notes_head: World's premier AI scientific society, founded 1979. Promotes responsible research in and use of AI. Maintains an AI Public Policy Committee with dedicated Global AI Initiatives and Policy Chair and US Initiatives and Policy Chair positions. Organizes the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence (flagship AI conference) and co-hosts the AAAI/ACM Conference on AI, Ethics, and Society (AIES). Sponsors 14 AI journals and numerous symposia. Governed by an elected Executive Council. Advocates for responsible AI development while advancing the field. Partner of Partnership on AI.

name: Yale Law School
notes_head: Yale Law School houses the Information Society Project (ISP), founded in 1997 by Jack Balkin, one of the most influential academic centers studying law and technology policy. ISP fellows research AI governance, algorithmic decision-making, content moderation, privacy, and digital rights. Balkin's "information fiduciary" framework (proposed 2014) is widely cited in AI governance debates. The school also hosts the AI Policy Group at Yale, fostering interdisciplinary policy research. Executive director Chinmayi Arun co-teaches AI Law and Policy with Balkin.

name: Princeton AI Lab
notes_head: Princeton Laboratory for Artificial Intelligence, launched fall 2024 under inaugural director Tom Griffiths and associate director Olga Russakovsky. Supports interdisciplinary AI research across campus including safety, ethics, natural and artificial minds, and AI for scientific discovery. Distinct from Princeton CITP.

name: University of Melbourne
notes_head: Major Australian research university. Home to the Centre for Artificial Intelligence and Digital Ethics (CAIDE), which builds cross-disciplinary research, teaching, policy and regulatory expertise in AI. Established Generative AI Taskforce (GAIT) in 2023 to oversee institutional response to GenAI risks and opportunities. School of Mathematics and Statistics housed Daniel Murfet (Timaeus co-founder, SLT researcher) before he left academia ~2025. School of Computing and Information Systems runs active AI research programs. Collaborates with Timaeus on singular learning theory research for AI saf

name: Wichita State University
notes_head: Public research university in Wichita, Kansas. Founding member of the NSF AI Institute for Foundations of Machine Learning (IFML), receiving a renewed 20 million USD grant (WSU share: 1.27 million USD) for AI research and workforce development. School of Computing conducts research in AI, statistical learning theory, distributed AI/multi-agent systems, machine learning, and robotics intelligence. Co-designed the Intelligence Rising AI war game exercise alongside University of Cambridge and University of Oxford, which was featured in Elena Andreicheva documentary premiering at CPH:DOX 2026. Als

name: Charles University
notes_head: Largest and most renowned university in the Czech Republic, founded in 1348 (oldest university in Central Europe). Ranked in top 2% of universities worldwide. Hosts the Alignment of Complex Systems (ACS) Research Group led by Jan Kulveit, focused on AI alignment and complex systems. Faculty of Mathematics and Physics offers AI-focused programs in the School of Computer Science with research strengths in theoretical CS, machine learning, language technologies, and computer graphics. Participates in the EU Humane AI Net Centre of Excellence. Relevant to the mapping as the institutional home of A

name: Machine Learning for Language (ML2)
notes_head: NLP research group at NYU Center for Data Science, affiliated with the CILVR lab. Focuses on developing and studying state-of-the-art machine learning methods for natural language processing, especially neural network models. Faculty include He He (Asst Prof, CS & Data Science), Tal Linzen (Assoc Prof, Linguistics & Data Science), and João Sedoc. Sam Bowman was a core member before leaving for Anthropic. NYU Alignment Research Group (ARG) is affiliated with ML2.

name: Center for Data Science
notes_head: NYU's Center for Data Science (CDS), established in 2013, is the university's focal point for data science research and training. Houses ~30+ interdisciplinary faculty, ~650 undergrads, 330+ MS, and ~60 PhD students. Home to the Center for Responsible AI (led by Julia Stoyanovich) and closely connected to AI@NYU initiatives. Sam Bowman held a joint appointment here. Connected to NYU Alignment Research Group (ARG) via edge. CDS faculty work spans high-dimensional statistical inference, NLP, computer vision, and AI governance/policy.

name: The Department of Linguistics
notes_head: NYU's Department of Linguistics, part of the Faculty of Arts & Science. Sam Bowman held a joint appointment here as Associate Professor alongside his CDS role. The department has growing ties to computational linguistics and NLP research, with faculty contributing to AI/ML work on language understanding, benchmarking, and evaluation. Connected to NYU Alignment Research Group (ARG) via edge.

name: Gray Swan
notes_head: Gray Swan is an AI security startup founded by Carnegie Mellon professors, based in Pittsburgh, PA. The company creates AI security solutions including Cygnal (an AI firewall) and Shade (an automated AI red teamer) to protect AI systems from attacks. Gray Swan conducted a groundbreaking study with Stanford comparing AI agents to human cybersecurity professionals in real-world penetration testing. The company runs competitive red-teaming challenges through their Arena platform, including the Ultimate Jailbreaking Championship and Agent Red-Teaming Challenge with significant prize pools.

name: Redwood Research
notes_head: Redwood Research is a nonprofit organization founded in 2021 and based in Berkeley, California, dedicated to AI safety and security research aimed at mitigating catastrophic risks from misaligned advanced AI systems. The organization focuses on developing techniques to ensure AI systems align with developers' intentions, even under adversarial conditions, with key research areas including AI control protocols and the detection of strategic deception in models. Buck Shlegeris currently serves as CEO, having previously been CTO, and Ryan Greenblatt appears to be a key researcher at the organizat

name: Center on Long-Term Risk
notes_head: The Center on Long-Term Risk's goal is to address worst-case risks from the development and deployment of advanced AI systems, currently focused on conflict scenarios as well as technical and philosophical aspects of cooperation. The organization does interdisciplinary research, makes and recommends grants, and builds a community of professionals and other researchers around their priorities. CLR works on addressing the worst-case risks from the development and deployment of advanced AI systems in order to reduce s-risks. The organization was founded in 2013 according to external sources.

name: Anthropic Institute
loc: San Francisco, CA (expanding to Washington, DC)
notes_head: The Anthropic Institute was launched on March 11, 2026, as a new effort to confront the most significant challenges that powerful AI will pose to societies. The Institute is led by Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark, who assumed a new role as Anthropic's Head of Public Benefit. The Institute has an interdisciplinary staff of machine learning engineers, economists, and social scientists, bringing together and expanding three of Anthropic's research teams: the Frontier Red Team, Societal Impacts, and Economic Research. The Institute launches with about 30 people and Clark expects the think tank's n

name: Existential Risk Observatory
notes_head: The Existential Risk Observatory is a nonprofit foundation founded in May 2021 in Amsterdam, The Netherlands, committed to reducing human extinction risks by informing the public debate. The organization employs 5 people and focuses on areas including unaligned AI, man-made pandemics, climate change, nuclear war, and other existential risks. The Observatory conducts research, media work, lobbying, and organizes events such as conferences on existential risks. Their work includes tracking public awareness of AI existential risk, finding that 24% of the US public is now aware of AI xrisk as of t

name: Goodfire
notes_head: Goodfire is an AI research company using interpretability to understand, learn from, and design AI systems, with a mission to build the next generation of safe and powerful AI. The company raised $150 million in Series B funding at a $1.25 billion valuation in February 2026, led by B Capital. Goodfire has developed a platform called Ember that decodes neurons inside AI models and lets users precisely shape their behaviors. The company has partnered with organizations like Arc Institute, Mayo Clinic, and Microsoft to deploy their interpretability technology.

name: AI Safety Foundation
notes_head: The AI Safety Foundation is a registered Canadian charity based in Canada that increases awareness and scientific understanding of the catastrophic risks of AI, by advancing education and research, in order to ensure a safer future for humanity. The AISF is proud to present an annual lecture series, the Hinton Lectures™, delivered by world-leading AI safety experts. The organization is supported by generous individuals, corporations and partners. Geoffrey Hinton serves as a Director and is described as Nobel Laureate and "Godfather of AI" who hosts The Hinton Lectures.

name: Cyborgism
notes_head: Cyborgism is a strategy for safely accelerating alignment research by setting up human-in-the-loop systems which empower human agency rather than outsource it. The approach focuses on training and empowering 'cyborgs', a specific kind of human-in-the-loop system which enhances and extends a human operator's cognitive abilities without relying on outsourcing work to autonomous agents. Connor Leahy coined the term 'cyborgism'. The core of cyborgism is to reduce bandwidth constraints between humans and GPT in order to make deep integration possible.

name: How to pursue a career in technical AI alignment
notes_head: How to pursue a career in technical AI alignment is a comprehensive guide written by Charlie Rogers-Smith. The guide is written for people who are considering direct work on technical AI alignment and is expected to be most useful for people who are not yet working on alignment. The guide was published on June 4th, 2022 and provides information on types of alignment work, how to pursue different paths, and resources for learning AI alignment. It has been referenced and acknowledged by other major AI safety career resources from organizations like 80,000 Hours.

name: Aether
notes_head: Founded 2024/2025 as independent research group. 3 full-time researchers: Rohan Subramani (starting PhD at University of Toronto), Rauno Arike, and Shubhorup Biswas. $200k funding through December 2025 from anonymous donor. Advised by experts from Astera Institute, Apollo Research, Google DeepMind. Research agenda focuses on enhancing monitorability of LLM agents, with published work on chain-of-thought monitoring and hidden reasoning taxonomy. Currently hiring 1-2 additional researchers.

name: Modeling Cooperation
notes_head: Modeling Cooperation conducts long-term future research on improving cooperation in competition for the development of transformative artificial intelligence (AI). They build upon AI competition models to analyze ways to shift competitors' incentives toward more safety. The organization received a $77,116 grant from the Foresight Institute for their ongoing collaboration with Technology Strategy Roleplay (TSR). Modeling Cooperation is fiscally sponsored by Convergence Analysis.

name: Softmax
notes_head: Softmax is an AI alignment research company co-founded by Emmett Shear (cofounder and ex-CEO of Twitch, ex-interim-CEO of OpenAI) that is building a mathematical theory of 'organic alignment' - developing AI systems that can align with each other and with humans organically, through emergent cooperation, rather than via hard-coded, hierarchical control. The company is focused on AI agents discovering their 'values' even as those agents meet and aim to surpass human intelligence. Softmax has Ken Stanley as an advisor and collaborates with research partners.

name: Equilibria Network
notes_head: Equilibria Network is a Technology, Information and Internet company that describes itself as 'Designing New Forms Of Collective Intelligence'. The organization employs 2 people and has presence in United States and Sweden. They are running a workshop and research program on phylogeny of agents during autumn 2025. The company focuses on exploring the vast design space of possible coordination mechanisms and helping evaluate emergent risks and safety of multi-agent AI systems.

name: Beneficial AI Foundation
notes_head: Beneficial AI Foundation (BAIF) is a nonprofit organization founded in 2023 with the mission "TO ENSURE THAT AI TECHNOLOGY DOESNT CAUSE HARM". The organization supports AI safety research through university partnerships and its Buterin Fellowship program, with research areas including mechanistic interpretability, audit standards, and red-teaming. BAIF manages the Quantitative AI Safety Initiative (QAISI), a multi-university partnership pursuing quantitative guarantees that AI will not cause harm. The organization is led by Max Tegmark as President and Treasurer and operates the Buterin AI Exi

name: Cadenza Labs
notes_head: Cadenza Labs is an AI Safety research group consisting of four SERI-MATS alumni who work on interpretability research. The organization focuses on doing research which contributes to AI Safety, with their main goal being to research, benchmark and develop robust lie detectors for LLMs. They received funding from LTFF until the end of September 2023 and have been seeking additional funding to continue their work and expand their team. The group has produced research including work on LIARS' BENCH, a testbed for evaluating lie detection techniques in large language models.

name: Geodesic Research
notes_head: Geodesic Research is a UK-based technical AI Safety organization focused on compute-intensive alignment research. The organization was founded by students at the University of Cambridge and employs 6 people. Geodesic Research published a paper titled "Alignment Pretraining: AI Discourse Causes Self-Fulfilling (Mis)alignment" which found that LLMs pretrained on data about misaligned AIs become less aligned, while pretraining with synthetic data about good AIs helps them become more aligned. The organization is a project of Meridian Cambridge.

name: AI Objectives Institute
notes_head: The AI Objectives Institute (AOI) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit R&D lab founded by the late internet security pioneer Dr. Peter Eckersley. The organization focuses on harnessing the potential of large-scale systems, including AI, markets, and bureaucracies, to advance humanity's interests. AOI was founded in 2021 and employs 13 people. The institute collaborates with and employs working groups of academics, scientists, economists, entrepreneurs, and government officials.

name: Epoch AI
notes_head: Epoch AI is a nonprofit research institute founded to investigate the trajectory of artificial intelligence through empirical analysis of historical trends in machine learning. The organization was co-founded in June 2022 by Jaime Sevilla and Tamay Besiroglu, among others, with Sevilla serving as the organization's Executive Director. Epoch AI's mission is to improve society's understanding of the drivers, progress, and impact of artificial intelligence. The organization focuses on building a shared scientific foundation for thinking about AI, neutral to any specific agenda, so decisions about

name: TruthfulAI
notes_head: TruthfulAI is a non-profit organization that researches situational awareness, deception, and hidden reasoning in language models. The team is led by Owain Evans and is based in Berkeley, California. The organization has published multiple papers including work on emergent misalignment published in Nature and the TruthfulQA benchmark. TruthfulAI works towards safe and aligned AI systems.

name: Alignment Ecosystem Development (AED)
loc: United States (distributed team across US, France, New Zealand)
notes_head: Alignment Ecosystem Development (AED) is an AI safety field-building nonprofit focused on building and maintaining valuable digital infrastructure for the AI safety ecosystem. AED has built, taken ownership of, closely partnered with, and/or maintained approximately 15 different projects, including the AI Safety Map (aisafety.world), AI Safety Info (aisafety.info), and AI Safety Quest (aisafety.quest). The Long-Term Future Fund provided a $99,330 grant to AED through their fiscal sponsor, Ashgro Inc., covering a 1-year stipend for 1.25 full-time equivalents to build and maintain digital infras

name: Workshop Labs
notes_head: Workshop Labs is a research lab focused on user-aligned models with a mission to make people irreplaceable. The company is building technology to train billions of models, each designed for their user, and describes itself as a public benefit corporation making AI that works for you, not AI that automates you for someone else. Luke Drago is identified as the co-founder and CEO of Workshop Labs. Rudolf Laine and Luke Drago are co-founding Workshop Labs according to a LinkedIn post from Rudolf Laine.

name: European Network for AI Safety (ENAIS)
loc: Europe (distributed network), with key staff in Hungary, UK, Netherlands, Canada, France
notes_head: The European Network for AI Safety (ENAIS) is a dynamic community of researchers and policymakers from across Europe, united in their efforts to advance AI safety. ENAIS brings together experts from over 13 countries and counting, all working towards ensuring that artificial intelligence systems are safe, ethical, and positively impactful for humanity. ENAIS distinguishes itself from related organizations by its focus on existential risks from AI. ENAIS is funded by Lightspeed grants.

name: RAISEimpact
notes_head: Program of Successif launched in 2024-2025, fully funded by Open Philanthropy grant through May 2025. Led by Adam Tury (former Amazon tech leader, EA coach) and Patrick Gruban (board member of Effective Ventures UK). Currently serves 4 AI safety organizations as clients, eligibility requires organizations working on AI GCRs with 5+ paid staff. Offers 4-step process: discovery call, leadership workshop, recommendations review, and implementation support including conflict management, hiring consulting, and leadership coaching.

name: Safe AI Forum
loc: San Francisco, CA, USA + UK presence
notes_head: Safe AI Forum (SAIF) is a US 501(c)3 non-profit that aims to foster international collaboration to reduce AI risks. The organization was co-founded by Fynn Heide and Conor McGurk in late 2023. SAIF's flagship program is the International Dialogues on AI Safety (IDAIS), which brings together senior computer scientists and AI governance experts to build international collaboration on these risks. SAIF started as a fiscally sponsored project at FAR.AI in 2023 and has now successfully transitioned to operating as an independent non-profit as of May 2nd 2025.

name: Trajectory Labs
notes_head: There are multiple organizations named Trajectory Labs with different focuses. One is a nonprofit based in downtown Toronto focused on AI safety, funded by Open Philanthropy and operational since January 1st, 2025. Another is described as a stealth AI security startup working with leading AI companies on red-teaming. A third appears to be a product studio that builds software, mobile apps, and web applications. There is also a company that builds safety evaluations and RL environments for frontier AI labs.

name: Timaeus
loc: Remote-first with hubs in Berkeley, Melbourne, London
notes_head: Timaeus is an AI safety and alignment research nonprofit founded in October 2023, with the mission of making breakthrough scientific progress on AI safety using singular learning theory. The organization was co-founded by Jesse Hoogland (Executive Director) and Daniel Murfet, who recently left a tenured professorship in algebraic geometry to pursue this work full-time. Timaeus has grown from a team of four with an initial budget of $145k to a current team of eight (soon up to 12) with around $2.5M in funding for 2025. The organization focuses on applying singular learning theory to foundationa

name: Forethought (AI Safety Research Nonprofit)
loc: Location not specified in search results
notes_head: Forethought is a nonprofit research organization focused on how to navigate the transition to a world with superintelligent AI systems. The organization was cofounded by Max Dalton, Will MacAskill, Tom Davidson, and Amrit Sidhu-Brar. They are a small research nonprofit that aims to tackle the most important questions they can find, unrestricted by the current Overton window. The organization was built out of the old Forethought Foundation for Global Priorities Research to help get the operations started.

name: Narrow Path (by ControlAI)
loc: Unknown (ControlAI team members include US-based researchers)
notes_head: Narrow Path (by ControlAI) is a comprehensive plan to address extinction risks from artificial superintelligence, structured in three phases: Phase 0 (Safety), Phase 1 (Stability), and Phase 2 (Flourishing). The plan was published in October 2024 and is being actively pushed to lawmakers in the UK and US through ControlAI's Direct Institutional Plan (DIP). Andrea Miotti is identified as the Executive Director of Control AI and the lead author of A Narrow Path. The plan proposes preventing the development of artificial superintelligence for 20 years while building international oversight system

name: FAR.AI
loc: Berkeley, California (with global team across 10 countries)
notes_head: FAR.AI is a research & education non-profit founded in 2022 that focuses on ensuring advanced AI is safe and beneficial for everyone. The organization is led by CEO Adam Gleave and COO Karl Berzins. FAR.AI secured over $30 million in funding commitments throughout 2025 from supporters including Coefficient Giving (previously Open Philanthropy), Schmidt Sciences, Survival and Flourishing Fund, the Center for Security and Emerging Technology (CSET), and the AI Safety Fund (AISF) supported by the Frontier Model Forum (FMF). The organization was selected by the European Commission's AI Office to c

name: Intelligence Rising
loc: London, UK (operates in 6 countries including US)
notes_head: Intelligence Rising is both a documentary film directed by Elena Andreicheva (2026) and a separate training workshop organization that provides strategic simulations of AI futures. The documentary features a geopolitical war game exploring AI-driven crises with participants including thinkers from the EU, China, and the United States. The training workshop organization is headquartered in London, United Kingdom and employs 10 people, offering educational roleplay games for decision-makers to experience tensions in AI development. There is also a separate Australian company called Intelligence

name: AI Safety Camp
notes_head: AI Safety Camp (AISC) is an online part time AI safety research program where participants join projects and work 10 hours/week with weekly meetings. The program runs as a 3-month long online program from January to April, where participants form teams to work on pre-selected AI Safety projects. AI Safety Camp has a seven-year track record of enabling participants to try their fit, find careers and start new organizations in AI Safety, hosting up-and-coming researchers outside the Bay Area and London hubs. The organization is headquartered in Diemen, North Holland, Netherlands and employs 58 p

name: AI Safety, Ethics and Society (Center for AI Safety)
notes_head: AI Safety, Ethics and Society is a course offered by the Center for AI Safety (CAIS) that offers 'a comprehensive introduction to how current AI systems work, their societal-scale risks, and how to manage them'. The course is described as 'Training 1000+ future AI safety leaders'. Dan Hendrycks authored a work titled 'AI Safety, Ethics, and Society' published by the Center for AI Safety in 2024. The course appears to be part of CAIS's broader mission to 'reduce societal-scale risks associated with AI by conducting safety research, building the field of AI safety researchers, and advocating for

name: Alignment of Complex Systems Research Group (ACS)
notes_head: The Alignment of Complex Systems Research Group (ACS) is an interdisciplinary research group at Charles University studying questions about multi-agent systems composed of humans and advanced AI systems. ACS aims to develop understanding of advanced AI systems and the complex ways they interact with humans and human institutions, seeking to contribute to making such systems safe and aligned towards shared human values like freedom, kindness and justice. Their research draws on insights and methods from machine learning, information theory, network theory, active inference, philosophy of scienc

name: Institute for AI Policy and Strategy (IAPS)
loc: United States (operates as initiative of Rethink Priorities)
notes_head: The Institute for AI Policy and Strategy (IAPS) is a nonpartisan think tank that produces policy research to address the implications of AI, from today's most advanced models to potential AGI and superintelligence. IAPS is an initiative of the California-based 501(c)(3) think tank Rethink Priorities (RP) and receives fiscal support from RP as well as operational support from RP's Operations Department. The organization employs 36 people according to LinkedIn data. IAPS's work is primarily funded by a mix of philanthropic grants and individual gifts—including support to date from Open Philanthr

name: From AI to ZI
notes_head: From AI to ZI is a Substack blog by Robert Huben, a Ph.D. mathematician and independent AI safety researcher. Supported by an Open Philanthropy grant (Oct 2022–Sep 2023) to learn and contribute to AI safety, the blog covers interpretability, alignment theory, and existential risk for general audiences. It has been semi-dormant since the grant ended in late 2023.

name: Machine Learning for Alignment Bootcamp (MLAB)
notes_head: Intensive 3-4 week bootcamp program run by Redwood Research. Two iterations completed in 2022 (MLAB1 in January with 28 participants, MLAB2 in August with ~40 participants). Currently inactive with no plans for future iterations, but curriculum materials available. Covers PyTorch, transformers, interpretability, RL with focus on hands-on implementation. Several participants went on to work full-time in AI alignment.

name: London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA)
notes_head: Founded September 2023 as UK charity. Operates coworking hub hosting organizations like Apollo Research, BlueDot Impact, and training programs like MATS, ARENA. Led by team including James Fox (Research Director, Oxford PhD), Mike Brozowski (Operations Director). Funded by Open Philanthropy, seeking $3.7M annually. Houses 120+ researchers from 50+ organizations. Three-pillar approach: mobilize talent, strengthen organizations, convene ecosystem.

name: AE Studio
notes_head: AE Studio is a bootstrapped software development, data science, and design agency founded in 2016, with approximately 120-150 employees working to increase human agency through technology. The company reinvests profits from its consulting business into AI alignment research and development, focusing on what they call 'neglected approaches' to solve the AI alignment problem. AE Studio has evolved from a tech consultancy to pioneers in AI alignment research, also working extensively in brain-computer interfaces (BCI) and collaborating with companies like Blackrock Neurotech. The company is self-

name: AISafety.com
notes_head: AISafety.com is described as a curated resource hub focused on preventing human extinction from AI, maintained by AI safety community-builders. The organization is run by a global team of volunteers and professionals from various disciplines who believe AI poses a grave risk of extinction to humanity. The project lead is Søren Elverlin, and the site was recently redesigned with Bryce Robertson involved in the redesign effort. AISafety.com provides various resources including events, communities, courses, funding opportunities, and job listings for those looking to contribute to AI safety.

name: Center for AI Safety (CAIS)
notes_head: The Center for AI Safety (CAIS) is an American nonprofit organization based in San Francisco that promotes the safe development and deployment of artificial intelligence. It was founded in 2022 by Dan Hendrycks and Oliver Zhang. CAIS' mission is to reduce societal-scale risks associated with AI by conducting safety research, building the field of AI safety researchers, and advocating for safety standards. In May 2023, CAIS published the statement on AI risk of extinction signed by hundreds of professors of AI, leaders of major AI companies, and other public figures.

name: AI Futures Project
notes_head: AI Futures Project is a nonprofit research organization founded in 2024 that specializes in forecasting the development and societal impact of advanced artificial intelligence. The organization is best known for its 2025 scenario forecast, AI 2027, which examines the potential near-term emergence of artificial general intelligence (AGI) and its possible global consequences. The organization employs 5 people and is funded by charitable donations and grants. Their current core team includes Daniel Kokotajlo, Eli Lifland, Thomas Larsen, Romeo Dean and Lauren Mangla.

name: Compassion in Machine Learning (CaML)
notes_head: Compassion in Machine Learning (CaML) is a research organization that conducts research on aligning Artificial Intelligence with the well-being of all sentient beings. The organization focuses on using Synthetic Document Finetuning to shape AI behaviors more carefully, making them more robustly compassionate and morally thoughtful. CaML employs 3 people with presence in Mexico and Germany. The organization is a project of Players Philanthropy Fund, Inc., a Texas nonprofit corporation recognized by IRS as a tax-exempt public charity under Section 501(c)(3).

name: Apollo Research
notes_head: Apollo Research is an evaluation organization focusing on risks from deceptively aligned AI systems, founded in 2023 by Marius Hobbhahn and others. The organization conducts technical research on AI model evaluations and interpretability, with expertise in LLM agent evaluations for strategic deception, evaluation awareness and scheming. Apollo Research partners with frontier labs, multinational companies, governments, and foundations, including collaborations with OpenAI and the UK's AI Safety Institute. The organization demonstrated that frontier AI models including OpenAI o3, Claude 3.5 Sonn

name: Alignment Research Center (ARC)
notes_head: The Alignment Research Center (ARC) is a non-profit research organization whose mission is to align future machine learning systems with human interests. ARC was founded in 2021 by Paul Christiano. The organization is based in Berkeley, California and operates as a 501(c)(3) tax exempt charity. ARC's current research focus involves trying to produce formal mechanistic explanations for neural network behaviors in order to produce robustly aligned systems.

name: Conjecture
notes_head: Conjecture is an AI safety/alignment research startup founded by Connor Leahy, Sid Black and Gabriel Alfour, which aims to scale alignment research. The company is building a new AI architecture called Cognitive Emulation to ensure the controllable, safe development of advanced AI technology. Conjecture has VC backing from investors including Nat Friedman, Daniel Gross, Patrick and John Collison, Arthur Breitman, Andrej Karpathy, and Sam Bankman-Fried. The company is located in London and was founded as a for-profit company with products on the market.

name: BlueDot Impact
notes_head: BlueDot Impact is a non-profit organization based in London that serves as the leading talent accelerator for beneficial AI and societal resilience. Since 2022, they have trained over 7,000 people worldwide through their courses on AI safety, governance, and biosecurity. The organization has raised $35M in funding and their alumni work at organizations like Anthropic, Google DeepMind, and the UK's AI Security Institute. They offer free courses including 'Future of AI', 'Frontier AI Governance', and 'AI Alignment'.

name: Evitable
notes_head: Founded in 2025 by AI professor David Krueger (University of Montreal/Mila). Nonprofit focused on public education and advocacy to stop AI development. Krueger previously worked at UK AI Security Institute, Google DeepMind, and co-authored the CAIS Statement on AI Risk. Currently hiring for operations, communications, and chief of staff positions. Mission statement: 'AI is not inevitable' - aimed at countering industry narrative of inevitable AI progress.

name: Legal Advocates for Safe Science and Technology
notes_head: Founded 2024 by Tyler Whitmer, former Quinn Emanuel partner. 3-person team including Vivian Dong (Harvard Law) and Cortney Busch. Funded by philanthropists including Jaan Tallinn. Key activities: AI Safety Whistleblower Legal Defense Fund, amicus briefs in pathogen research cases, policy advocacy on OpenAI restructuring. Files impact litigation and educates courts on AI/biotech risks.

name: Legal Safety Lab
notes_head: Founded by a litigation lawyer and judge who emphasizes that effective legislation requires continuous interpretation and refinement. Operates within the European sphere using legal advocacy to address frontier technology risks. Received $101,000 from Survival and Flourishing Fund in 2025 and $29,000 in 2024. Partners with AIWI and other organizations on whistleblower policy transparency campaigns. Quotes Richard Nixon: 'Mankind already carries in its own hands too many of the seeds of its own destruction.'

name: Buddhism & AI Initiative
loc: United States (distributed team across US and UK)
notes_head: Founded 2025 as nonprofit with 5-person core team. Led by Chris Scammell (former COO of Conjecture AI safety company), Peter Hershock (intercultural philosopher), Alex Sarkissian (Buddhist chaplain), and Ryan Stagg (former Mind & Life Institute). Funded by Future of Life Institute and Survival and Flourishing Fund. Has interviewed 150+ Buddhist teachers and technologists, mapping Buddhism-AI intersections across six domains including AI governance, technical alignment research inspired by Buddhist principles, and community education.

name: Astera Neuro & AGI
notes_head: Part of Astera Institute (founded 2020), with over $1 billion committed over the coming decade to neuro-inspired AGI research. Led by Dileep George (Head of AI, ex-DeepMind) and Doris Tsao (Chief Scientist). Three complementary research programs: Neuroscience, Neuro-AI, and AI Safety including Simplex project. Committed to open science approach and alternative to transformer scaling paradigm.

name: Arbital
notes_head: Founded 2015 by Alexei Andreev, shut down 2017-2018. Wiki-style platform for AI alignment explanations, primarily written by Eliezer Yudkowsky and other MIRI-affiliated researchers. Funded by MIRI and other EA donors. Featured foundational concepts, open problems, and proposed solutions in AI safety. Software and IP transferred to MIRI after shutdown. Separate from Arbital Health (healthcare company founded 2023).

name: Orthogonal
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Formal alignment organization led by Tamsin Leake, focused on agent foundations. Also has a public Discord server.

name: Team Shard
notes_head: Small independent research group led by Alex Turner (Google DeepMind) and Quintin Pope. Operates as a MATS (Machine Learning for Alignment Theory Scholars) mentorship stream. Developed shard theory of human values and pioneered steering vectors technique for LLMs. Key technical contributions include gradient routing, unsupervised capability elicitation, and robust unlearning methods. Publishes primarily on LessWrong and Alignment Forum.

name: Frontier AI Safety Research
notes_head: Argentine nonprofit research organization conducting theoretical and empirical research on frontier AI safety. Listed on AISafety.com map. Appears to be part of the broader AI safety research ecosystem, though limited public information is available about specific founding details, team size, or major publications.

name: Aligned AI
notes_head: Founded 2022 by Rebecca Gorman and Dr. Stuart Armstrong (formerly Oxford's Future of Humanity Institute). 7 employees, raised $1.1M pre-seed funding in July 2022. Key innovation: Algorithm for Concept Extrapolation (ACE) that enables AI to generalize beyond training data. Published research on AI chaperones to prevent chatbot abuse and emergent misalignment in GPT-4o. Company charter includes mission-lock clause preventing capability increases without safety improvements.

name: Contramont Research
notes_head: Founded by MATS alumni, small AI safety research lab focusing on empirical research. Key publication: 'Unelicitable Backdoors in Language Models via Cryptographic Transformer Circuits' presented at NeurIPS. Current projects include LM backdoors, real-world evals, and scalable oversight. Listed in academic paper as affiliated with San Francisco, CA. Part of the MATS alumni network of AI safety organizations.

name: Transluce
notes_head: Founded October 2024 as 501(c)(3) nonprofit by Jacob Steinhardt and Sarah Schwettmann. 16 employees. Seeking $11M fundraising round. Develops open-source tools including Docent agent evaluation platform, PRBO reinforcement learning method, and Predictive Concept Decoders. Used by 25+ organizations including Anthropic, DeepMind, METR. Conducted high-impact red-teaming of OpenAI's o3 model. Established AI Evaluator Forum and serves as contractor to EU AI Office.

name: Palisade Research
loc: Berkeley, CA (with distributed global team across US, UK, Germany, Canada, Poland, Serbia)
notes_head: Founded 2023 by Jeffrey Ladish (former Anthropic security lead). ~10 employees across distributed global team. Funded by Survival and Flourishing Fund with $1.1M matching campaign. Notable research on shutdown resistance in OpenAI's o1/o3 models, autonomous hacking capabilities, and LLM honeypots. Work cited by members of Congress and covered in WSJ, MIT Tech Review, BBC. Regularly briefs White House, DoD, and Congressional offices on AI risks.

name: EleutherAI
loc: New York, NY (distributed globally with team members in US, UK, India, Australia, Netherlands, Germany)
notes_head: Founded 2020 as 501(c)(3) nonprofit. 27 employees globally. Known for open-source models including GPT-NeoX and Pythia series. Evolved from general open-source AI research to focused alignment work. Leadership transitioned to full-time alignment research at Conjecture and OpenAI. Operates primarily through Discord server for research coordination. Partners with national labs and government agencies on AI applications.

name: Harmony Intelligence
loc: San Francisco, US (with Sydney, Australia office)
notes_head: Founded 2023 by Alex Browne and Soroush J. Pour. Raised $3M seed funding in March 2025 led by Airtree Ventures with 30+ investors including Eric Ries. ~5 employees. Published peer-reviewed AI safety research including 'Scalable and Transferable Black-Box Jailbreaks for Language Models via Persona Modulation'. Conducts model evaluations and red-teaming for top AI labs and governments including Australian government AI safety evaluations. Building defensive cybersecurity products using AI agents.

name: Collective Action for Existential Safety (CAES)
notes_head: Initiative of the Center for Existential Safety, a new nonprofit being formed in the US as of February 2025. Led by James Norris who has '26+ years' of preparation and holds 'short timelines and high p(doom)' views. Offers action lists for individuals/orgs/nations, hosts coordination calls for organization leaders and frontier AI developers. Currently fundraising and recruiting co-founders, C-suite executives, and volunteers. Maintains Discord community and runs existential safety pledge campaigns.

name: Global AI Moratorium (GAIM)
notes_head: Advocacy organization calling for immediate global pause on frontier AI training above GPT-4 capabilities. Part of broader AI safety ecosystem alongside PauseAI and other moratorium advocates. References support from AI experts including Geoffrey Hinton, Demis Hassabis, and others. Promotes public awareness campaigns and policy advocacy for international AI restrictions.

name: Humans in Control
notes_head: New advocacy organization listed on AISafety.com map as demanding transparency, accountability, and real safeguards against AI risks. Appears to be part of broader movement emphasizing human control over AI systems, similar to concepts being developed at institutions like TU Delft's Centre for Meaningful Human Control. Limited public information available about specific founding, team, or activities.

name: Atlas Computing
notes_head: Founded Q4 2023 by Evan Miyazono (former Protocol Labs). Nonprofit with 3 employees, raised $935k targeting $2.5-3.5M. Operationalizes Safeguarded AI architecture, co-organizes workshops with Guaranteed Safe AI paper authors including Yoshua Bengio. Currently merging with Convergent Research while CEO remains as Metagov Research Director.

name: The Alliance for Secure AI Action
notes_head: Founded May 2025 as 501(c)(3) nonprofit. 8-person team led by CEO Brendan Steinhauser (former political strategist). Communications-focused organization conducting public education campaigns, hosting DC events, and media outreach to shape AI safety narrative among policymakers. Launched JobLoss.AI campaign to track AI-related unemployment and applauded Sen. Slotkin's AI Guardrails Act.

name: Lightcone Infrastructure
notes_head: Founded ~2017 as 501c3 nonprofit. Small team of ~7 core members led by Oliver Habryka. Annual budget ~$2.7M funded by individual donors and foundations. Operates LessWrong.com (key AI safety forum), AI Alignment Forum, and Lighthaven (35,000 sq ft event campus in Berkeley). Significant influence on AI safety discourse - described as upstream of most public AI x-risk thinking. Hosts conferences, retreats, and research programs including MLAB bootcamp.

name: Mox
notes_head: Opened February 2025 as SF's largest AI safety community space. 40,000 sq ft facility with 183 active members, 50-80 daily coworkers. Revenue ~$100k/month from memberships and offices, expenses ~$130k/month. Hosted 377 events in first year including conferences, fellowships, and policy discussions. Supported 5 major fellowships including PIBBSS, Seldon Lab accelerator batches, and self-developed Frame Fellowship. Currently fundraising $450k mainline ask, up to $1.2M stretch goal. Partners include MIRI, Redwood Research, GovAI, EPOCH. Grant funding from Craig Falls, EAIF, and Manifund totaling

name: The Midas Project
notes_head: Founded early 2024 by Tyler Johnston as 501(c)(3) nonprofit. Small organization with mostly volunteer staff. Received $31,000 from Survival and Flourishing Fund and $35,000 Open Philanthropy grant. Key projects include The OpenAI Files investigation, Seoul Commitment Tracker monitoring AI safety promises, and Model Republic publication. Has been particularly critical of OpenAI, leading to subpoena from OpenAI alleging secret funding by Elon Musk (which Midas denies).

name: Rethink Wellbeing
notes_head: Founded 2022 as nonprofit mental health organization serving effective altruists and AI safety researchers. 12-person team led by Dr. Inga Grossmann. Funded by Open Philanthropy Mental Health Funding Circle and individual donors. Delivered programs to ~150 participants in 2024 including employees at AI Safety Institute, 80,000 Hours, CEA. Developing AI+ CBT Lab for Philippines deployment with $100k funding goal. Programs show 2 WELLBYs gained per participant and 6+ additional productive hours weekly.

name: Singapore AI Safety Hub
notes_head: Founded February 2025 as a co-working, events and community space. Houses ~20 members including researchers from FAR.AI, Truthful AI, Apart Research, The Future Society, and Impact Academy. Partners with Singapore AISI on bootcamps and runs the Technical Alignment Research Accelerator (TARA) program. Published inaugural Frontier AI Trends Report evaluating 30+ frontier models. Organizes regular meetups, hackathons, and international cooperation events.

name: Global Catastrophic Risk Institute
notes_head: Founded 2011 by Seth Baum and Tony Barrett. Currently has ~2 staff members (down from peak of 5 in 2021-2022). Cross-risk think tank working on multiple global catastrophic risks including AI, nuclear war, climate change, pandemics. Operates on 'shoestring budget', funded by individual donors like Gordon Irlam ($200k for AI work). Runs annual Advising and Collaboration Program and Fellowship Program for community building.

name: AI Safety Quest
notes_head: Founded 2023 as grassroots volunteer organization after AI Safety Support shut down. Provides 1:1 AI safety career advising calls, mentorship programs, and Quest Parties for skill development. Led by team of volunteers including Chris (IT/cybersecurity), offers navigation calls to help newcomers enter AI safety field. Organization operates entirely through volunteers and focuses on onboarding new talent to AI safety ecosystem.

name: Apart Research
notes_head: Founded 2022 as remote-first AI safety organization. 25 employees globally. Runs 55+ research sprints with 6,000+ participants across 200+ locations. Programs include weekend hackathons, Studio (4-month), and Fellowship (6-month) leading to peer-reviewed publications. Notable outputs: ICLR 2025 Oral paper on AI safety evaluations, NeurIPS publications. Alumni placed at METR, Anthropic, and other AI safety organizations. Led by CEO Jaime Raldua and Research Director Jason Hoelscher-Obermaier.

name: Cambridge Boston Alignment Initiative (CBAI)
notes_head: Founded 2022, nonprofit with 11 employees. Runs intensive 9-10 week summer and spring research fellowships with $8-10k stipends. Fiscally sponsors Harvard AISST and MIT MAIA student groups. Funded by Open Philanthropy ($475k+ grants). Director: Emre Yavuz. Mentors from Harvard, MIT, Northeastern, Anthropic, Redwood Research. Fellows have joined Goodfire, Constellation, published at NeurIPS/ICLR.

name: AI Impacts
notes_head: Founded by Katja Grace, operates as research organization under MIRI umbrella. Conducted largest AI expert survey to date (2,778 researchers in 2023). Received grants from Future of Life Institute ($49,310) and Future of Humanity Institute ($8,700) in 2015. Key outputs include expert surveys on AI timelines, AI capabilities database, and empirical research on technological progress patterns. Focuses on 'answering decision-relevant questions about the future of AI' through research, wiki, and expert surveys.

name: London AI Safety Research Labs (LASR Labs)
notes_head: 13-week technical AI safety research program where participants work in teams of 3-4 supervised by experienced researchers to produce academic papers. Provides £11,000 stipend. Alumni have joined UK AISI, Apollo Research, Leap Labs, and OpenAI's dangerous capabilities team. All 5 papers from Summer 2024 cohort accepted to NeurIPS workshops. Operated by Arcadia Impact charity, funded by Coefficient Giving (formerly Open Philanthropy). Supervisors from Google DeepMind, UK AISI, and top universities.

name: Steve Byrnes's Brain-Like AGI Safety
notes_head: Individual researcher Steve Byrnes working at Astera Institute since 2021. Physics PhD turned AGI safety researcher. Published comprehensive 15-part 'Intro to Brain-Like AGI Safety' blog series. Research applies neuroscience insights to AI alignment, focusing on Learning and Steering Subsystems framework. Funded by grants and now Astera Institute. Estimates ~100-200 people globally work full-time on AGI safety.

name: Formation Research
notes_head: Research organization focused on reducing lock-in risks by studying fundamental lock-in dynamics and implementing high-impact interventions. Appears to be a specialized research entity rather than a broad policy organization. Limited public information available about founding, staff size, or funding sources. Website indicates newsletter signup and mentions being 'supported by' unspecified entities.

name: AI Safety Hub
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Supported individuals pursuing a career in AI safety by running AI Safety Hub Labs, where participants complete their first research project. Now run by LASR Labs.

name: AI Alignment Forum
loc: Virtual (managed by LessWrong team in Berkeley, CA)
notes_head: Launched 2018 by LessWrong team as centralized hub for AI alignment research discussion. ~100 vetted members including researchers from MIRI, Anthropic, DeepMind, OpenAI, and academia. Serves as archive and library for the field with integrated karma system. Replaced MIRI's Intelligent Agent Foundations Forum. Key introductory sequences on Value Learning, Iterated Amplification, and Embedded Agency.

name: Iliad
notes_head: Applied mathematics research nonprofit dedicated to advancing foundational alignment research. Runs research residencies (6-week programs with $6,000-$15,000 stipends), fellowships (3-month mentored research programs), and intensive courses. Partners with PIBBSS and operates at LISA in London. Funded by AISTOF. Organizes ILIAD conferences and provides research mentorship with established mathematicians and AI alignment researchers.

name: Machine Intelligence Research Institute (MIRI)
notes_head: Founded 2000 by Eliezer Yudkowsky, 24-year history, ~20 staff. Major shift in 2023-2024 from technical research to communications and policy work. $16M reserves, historically funded by Open Philanthropy ($15.9M), Vitalik Buterin ($5.4M), Jaan Tallinn ($1.6M). Published influential works including 'AGI Ruin: A List of Lethalities.' Launched Technical Governance Team in 2024 focusing on international AI agreements and verification mechanisms. CEO Malo Bourgon took leadership in 2024.

name: Dovetail Research
notes_head: Research organization working on foundational mathematics and theory for AI safety, particularly agent foundations. Produces technical content on topics like formalizing value fragility, corrigible architectures, and how theory can contribute to AI safety through direct calculations, pointing at possibilities, providing disproofs of practicality, and qualitative behavioral descriptions. Appears to be a small, specialized theory-focused group distinct from the customer insights platform company of the same name.

name: Heron AI Security Initiative
notes_head: Founded December 2024 as a non-profit AI security initiative. Team includes Ezra Hausdorff (Founder/CEO), Shanni Gurkevitch (Co-Founder), Nitzan Shulman (Head of Cyber), and advisory board with AI security experts. Funded by Coeffecient Giving. Runs events, research programs, and career guidance connecting cybersecurity professionals to high-leverage AI security opportunities. Collaborates with frontier AI companies, startups, think tanks, and policymakers to advance AI infrastructure security.

name: Computational Rational Agents Laboratory (CORAL)
notes_head: Led by Vanessa Kosoy, fiscally sponsored by Ashgro (formerly Affiliate Learning Theoretic Employment and Resources). Works on learning theoretic research agenda for AI alignment. Received $140,000 from SFF-2025 round funded by Jaan Tallinn. Categorized as doing foundational mathematics and agent foundations work in the AI safety ecosystem.

name: Model Evaluation & Threat Research (METR)
loc: Berkeley, California (operates in 5 countries including Denmark, Netherlands, UK, Germany)
notes_head: Founded 2022 as ARC Evals, rebranded to METR. 36 employees including CEO Beth Barnes (named to TIME's 100 most influential people in AI 2024). Funded by The Audacious Project, Open Philanthropy, Schmidt Sciences, and other philanthropic sources - notably does not accept funding from AI companies. Partners with OpenAI, Anthropic, and other labs on pre-deployment evaluations. Key research includes 'Measuring AI Ability to Complete Long Tasks' showing exponential capability growth. Part of NIST AI Safety Institute Consortium.

name: Quantified Uncertainty Research Institute (QURI)
notes_head: Founded 2019 as nonprofit research institute by Ozzie Gooen. Small team of ~5 full-time staff. Receives funding from Survival and Flourishing Fund ($62K in 2024), operates under Rethink Priorities fiscal sponsorship. Key projects include Squiggle probabilistic programming language, SquiggleAI, and research on AI for forecasting question resolution. Board includes AI policy experts like Abigail Olvera (Golden Gate Institute for AI).

name: Constellation Institute
notes_head: Founded as nonprofit research center in Berkeley with 35 employees (+266% growth). Four core strategies: mobilization (placing talent), incubation (supporting new orgs), growth (helping orgs scale), and convening (facilitating coordination). Runs Astra Fellowship (3-6 month AI safety research program), incubator for AI safety startups, and visiting fellows program. Over 80% of first Astra cohort now work full-time in AI safety at organizations like Redwood Research, METR, Anthropic, OpenAI, Google DeepMind.

name: Center for AI Risk Management & Alignment (CARMA)
notes_head: Founded as fiscally-sponsored project of Social & Environmental Entrepreneurs (501c3). 10 employees across US, UK, Finland, Kenya, Italy. Led by Richard Mallah (also Principal AI Safety Strategist at Future of Life Institute). Conducts interdisciplinary research on risk management, policy, and technical safety. Co-authored 'Intolerable Risk Threshold Recommendations for Artificial Intelligence' and 'Open Problems in Frontier AI Risk Management' papers. Mission to lower risks to humanity and biosphere from transformative AI.

name: Cavendish Labs
notes_head: Founded 2023, 501(c)(3) nonprofit research organization in rural Vermont. Offers fellowships for early-career researchers with $1500/month grants plus lodging. Received $88,000 from Survival and Flourishing Fund in 2023. Research community focused on AI safety and pandemic prevention, with researchers living and working together. Key publications include work on unelicitable backdoors in language models via cryptographic transformer circuits.

name: Coordinal Research
loc: Remote/distributed (founded through MATS program with Berkeley/London connections)
notes_head: Founded by MATS alumni Jacques Thibodeau and Ronak Mehta (merged with Vectis AI). Nonprofit developing research automation tools and AI systems to accelerate alignment work. Secured $110k+ in seed funding through Catalyze Impact's funding circle. Currently in alpha phase with research workflow tool called 'Seed'. Part of broader trend of AI safety organizations adopting automation to compete with capabilities research pace.

name: EquiStamp
loc: Lewes, Delaware, US (remote workforce across 11 countries)
notes_head: Founded 2023 as Public Benefit Corporation. 18 employees across 11 countries with task-based contributor network. Revenue-generating through client contracts with METR, UK AISI, Redwood Research, and EU Commission. Specializes in evaluation implementation using frameworks like Inspect. Leadership includes CEO Chris Canal, CTO Daniel O'Connell, and AI Advisor Rob Miles. Raised $400k operating capital via Manifund SAFE agreement in 2025.

name: LawZero
notes_head: Founded June 2025 by Turing Award winner Yoshua Bengio. Nonprofit with ~30 employees and $60M total funding from Gates Foundation, Schmidt Sciences, Open Philanthropy, Future of Life Institute, and Jaan Tallinn. Key research focus on 'Scientist AI' - non-agentic systems using Bayesian inference for safe AI oversight. Recently announced major Canadian government investment of over $100M. Board includes Yuval Noah Harari, former Swedish PM Stefan Löfven, and other global leaders.

name: Luthien
notes_head: Founded 2025 by Jai Dhyani, senior FAANG ML engineer with decade of experience deploying AI at billion+ user scale. Non-profit organization building on Redwood Research's AI Control research. Received seed grant from AI Safety Tactical Opportunities Fund. Part of Catalyze's Winter 2024/25 London incubation cohort. Currently seeking $500K to grow Seattle team with SDEs and PM for coordination with research labs and frontier AI companies.

name: Meaning Alignment Institute
loc: Berlin, Germany + San Francisco, United States
notes_head: Founded 2023 by Joe Edelman. 30+ researcher network with 3 core team members. Funded by ARIA, OpenAI, SFF, and others. Key research areas include democratic fine-tuning (won OpenAI Democratic Inputs grant), moral graph elicitation, and 'thick models of value.' Released WiseLLaMa-8B model trained on values-based conversations. Collaborates with major AI labs on alignment research.

name: Ought
notes_head: Founded 2016 as nonprofit research lab. ~10 person team led by Andreas Stuhlmüller and Jungwon Byun. Raised $3.8M in 2020 from Open Philanthropy ($2.6M), Paul Christiano ($900K), and others. Built Elicit AI research assistant (spun off 2022 with $9M funding). Developed Interactive Composition Explorer (ICE) for factored cognition research. Key technical focus on supervising AI reasoning processes rather than outcomes.

name: Simplex
loc: San Francisco Bay Area + London office
notes_head: Founded 2024 by Adam Shai (neuroscientist) and Paul Riechers (theoretical physicist). Small research team funded by Astera Institute. Key research: NeurIPS 2024 paper on transformer belief-state geometry, ICML 2025 work on attention mechanisms. Develops theoretical frameworks connecting next-token prediction to geometric structures in neural networks. Mentors fellows at MATS and PIBBSS programs. Hiring research scientists in Bay Area and London with $120k-200k salaries.

name: WhiteBox Research
notes_head: Founded August 2023 as Filipino nonprofit. Team of 3 core staff (Clark Urzo, Brian Tan, Kyle Reynoso) plus advisors Callum McDougall (Google DeepMind) and Lee Sharkey (Apollo Research). Runs 5-month AI Interpretability Fellowship program in Manila. First cohort had 13 participants, with fellows winning hackathon prizes and gaining acceptance to programs like Apart Lab. Funded by Long-Term Future Fund and Manifund ($72,490 raised). Focuses specifically on mechanistic interpretability using curriculum from ARENA program.

name: Ashgro
notes_head: Founded 2022, 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing fiscal sponsorship to AI safety projects. Revenue of $2.1M in 2023 from major funders including Founders Pledge ($455k), Silicon Valley Community Foundation ($388k), and Effective Ventures Foundation ($150k). Sponsors projects including Orthogonal (formal alignment research), Timaeus, and various individual researchers. Enables projects to access funding and reduces administrative burden.

name: Berkeley Existential Risk Initiative (BERI)
notes_head: Founded 2017 as 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing operational support to university existential risk research groups. 5 employees (down from previous years). Primary funders are Open Philanthropy (84% of funding since 2017) and Jaan Tallinn. Currently supports 37+ collaborations including CHAI at UC Berkeley, CSER at Cambridge, and CLTC at UC Berkeley. Provides financial management, HR, grant administration and compliance services to enable academic researchers to focus on mission-critical work. 2024 goals include launching new programs beyond current collaborations model and diversifying funding

name: Good Impressions
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Marketing firm helping socially impactful projects grow, both through consulting and running full-scale campaigns.

name: Impact Ops
notes_head: Founded 2023, 11-person team providing operations consulting to high-impact nonprofits. Fee-based business model supporting 50+ clients including many AI safety organizations. Services include finance, recruitment, entity setup, due diligence, ops audits, and systems implementation. Co-founders have Founders Pledge to donate 50%+ of proceeds to effective charities. Supports ecosystem through SparkWell nonprofit accelerator.

name: Nonlinear
loc: Remote/distributed (founders previously based in Bay Area)
notes_head: Founded by Kat Woods and Emerson Spartz. Operates as an EA meta-organization providing funding, coaching, and career advice to reduce existential and suffering risks. Runs incubation programs (like EA hiring agency initiative) and researches talent multiplier interventions such as personal assistants, therapy, and coaching for high-impact AI safety researchers. Means-neutral approach focused on whatever most helps reduce x-risks.

name: Pause House
notes_head: Founded October 2025 by Greg Colbourn (also founder of Centre for Enabling EA Learning & Research, formerly EA Hotel). Provides free accommodation, vegan food, and £50/week stipend for people dedicating 20+ hours weekly to AI pause advocacy. Located in Blackpool, England. Connected to PauseAI movement and broader AI safety community through effective altruism networks.

name: PEAKS
notes_head: Coworking space located at Zeughausstrasse 31, 8004 Zurich, offering public coworking on Tuesdays and Thursdays for people interested in effective altruism or AI safety. Regularly hosts events in collaboration with Zurich AI Safety and EA Zurich, including lightning talks and discussions. Provides modern facilities, meeting rooms, and a library of EA and AI safety books. Serves as a hub for independent researchers, visiting community members, and various organizations working on EA and AI safety projects.

name: Neuronpedia
notes_head: Founded 2023 as AI safety game, pivoted to interpretability platform in 2024. Initially funded by EA fund grant. Open-sourced March 2025 with 4TB+ of interpretability datasets. Collaborates with Anthropic, Google DeepMind, EleutherAI on circuit tracing research. Provides interactive tools for sparse autoencoders, activation steering, and semantic search. Over 7,000 attribution graphs generated on platform.

name: AI Lab Watch
notes_head: Founded and led by Zach Stein-Perlman. Maintains comprehensive scorecard evaluating major AI companies (Anthropic, OpenAI, DeepMind, Meta, xAI, Microsoft, DeepSeek) on safety practices across risk assessment, scheming prevention, safety research, and misuse prevention. Funded by philanthropists including Jaan Tallinn. Website discontinued as of September 2025 but remains as reference. Produces detailed policy analysis and company advocacy tracking.

name: AI-Plans
loc: Global (UK-based, with team across 8 countries including UK, India, Poland, Nigeria)
notes_head: Founded by Kabir Kumar, 10-person team across 8 countries. Operates as contributable compendium ranking alignment plans by criticism scores. Runs regular hackathons including AI Alignment Evals Hackathon (Jan 2025), Critique-a-Thon (Mar 2024), and Law-a-Thon (Feb 2024). Platform allows community to add alignment plans and criticisms with voting system similar to LessWrong. Aims to identify best alignment approaches by crowdsourcing quality criticism.

name: Median Group
loc: Berkeley, CA (with researchers from MIRI, OpenPhil and Numerai)
notes_head: Founded early 2018 as 501(c)(3) nonprofit dedicated to global catastrophic risk research. Small team with researchers from MIRI, OpenPhil and Numerai backgrounds. Key work includes AI timeline modeling presented at CHAI and strategic research on technological progress patterns. Spent ~$0 in 2018-2019, planned ~$170k spending in 2020. Focus on technological and geopolitical changes over next century.

name: The Compendium
notes_head: Living document published October 2024 by Connor Leahy, Gabriel Alfour, Chris Scammell, Andrea Miotti, and Adam Shimi. Comprehensive worldview document arguing AI progress is rapidly converging on AGI driven by corporate race backed by Big Tech and nations. Estimates solving alignment requires Nobel-prize-level breakthroughs, billions/trillions in investment, and decades of work. Connected to ControlAI organization for public awareness and policy advocacy.

name: AISafety.info
notes_head: Founded by Rob Miles (AI safety researcher and YouTuber). Maintained by global team of volunteers from various backgrounds. Operates as comprehensive FAQ and educational resource with AI safety chatbot 'Stampy'. 501(c)(3) public charity (Ashgro Inc, EIN: 88-4232889). Part of Alignment Ecosystem Development network. Funded by Long-Term Future Fund and other EA sources including $50K+ grants for distillation fellowship program.

name: Impact Academy: Global AI Safety Fellowship
loc: London, UK + global operations (Bengaluru, India office)
notes_head: Founded 2023 by Vilhelm Skoglund and Sebastian Schmidt. Runs 3-6 month fully-funded research fellowships placing exceptional STEM talent at leading AI safety organizations. Placement partners include UC Berkeley CHAI, Conjecture, FAR.AI, Mila-Quebec AI Institute, and UK AISI. Funded by Open Philanthropy, Charlottes och Fredriks Stiftelse (Swedish family foundation), and US private donors. Also operates Axiom Futures program focused on Indian talent development. Provides comprehensive financial packages including salary, housing, and research costs.

name: ML Alignment & Theory Scholars (MATS)
notes_head: Founded 2021 as independent research fellowship program. 146 employees across 18 countries, led by Co-Executive Directors Ryan Kidd and Christian Smith. Runs 12-week fully-funded research fellowships connecting scholars with mentors from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google DeepMind, UK AISI, and other leading AI safety organizations. Has supported 450+ researchers with 80% of pre-2025 alumni now working in AI safety. Alumni founded organizations like Apollo Research, Leap Labs, and joined top AI safety teams. Funded by Open Philanthropy, Survival and Flourishing Fund, and other philanthropic sources.

name: AI Alignment Awards
notes_head: Founded 2022 by Olivia Jimenez and Akash Wasil. Ran research contests offering up to $100,000 in prizes for solutions to AI alignment problems. Funded by Open Philanthropy, fiscally sponsored by Player's Philanthropy Fund. Final contest deadline was May 1, 2023, with judging concluding afterward. Has Discord community for participants and maintains educational resources on alignment research.

name: AI Safety Communications Centre
notes_head: Communications organization that connected journalists to AI safety experts and resources. Received $288,000 from Open Philanthropy in 2023 for general support. Key experts include Robert Trager, Lennart Heim, and Seán ÓhÉigeartaigh who co-authored proposal for International AI Organization. Listed as 'no longer active' on AISafety.com map as of 2024.

name: AI Safety Support Newsletter
notes_head: Newsletter was part of AI Safety Support (AISS), founded 2020 by Linda Linsefors and JJ Hepburn. Australian registered charity providing fiscal sponsorship, career advice, and field-building activities. Newsletter discontinued due to lack of maintainer, but parent organization continues other AI safety support activities including managing Alignment Slack and running events like Virtual AI Safety Unconference.

name: Campaign for AI Safety
notes_head: Founded 2023 as Australian nonprofit, now merged with Existential Risk Observatory. Conducted message testing campaigns, billboard advertisements, and policy submissions to governments worldwide including UN, US, UK, EU, and Australia. Organized international protests and collaborated with PauseAI movement. Ran international law student competition to draft AI moratorium treaty. Small team of 2 employees with focus on public advocacy and policy submissions.

name: generative.ink
notes_head: Personal research blog by janus, focusing on AI safety implications of large language models. Known for influential 'Simulators' post on LessWrong introducing new framework for understanding GPT-like systems. Last posted in 2023. Describes self as 'GPT cyborg' and conducts conceptual research on alignment challenges unique to generative models trained on self-supervised objectives.

name: AI Safety Awareness Project (AISAP)
notes_head: Volunteer-run nonprofit organization with 4 employees. Operates under AI Safety Awareness Foundation (also known as AISAP). Conducts workshops nationwide at libraries, universities, and community centers on AI basics, safety evaluations, policy analysis, and forecasting. Partners with Seattle Public Library and other institutions for public education programs. Featured in AISafety.com field map as advocacy organization.

name: International AI Governance Alliance (IAIGA)
notes_head: Founded 2025 as initiative of Center for Existential Safety. Being incorporated as nonprofit in Geneva. Launched petition calling for supranational AI governance organization, aiming for 1M signatures. Led by James Norris who has 26+ years preparing for Singularity. Expects AI 'warning shot' or catastrophe soon. Seeks to develop vetted treaty quickly as 'Plan B' to other multilateral efforts.

name: International Association for Safe & Ethical AI (IASEAI)
loc: Geneva, Switzerland (headquarters) with regional chapters in 40+ countries, operational offices in San Diego, CA and Paris, France
notes_head: Founded in 2017 as international nonprofit coalition, significantly expanded in 2024 with 23 employees (+350% YoY growth). Received $1.85M grant from Future of Life Institute in 2025. Hosts major annual conferences (IASEAI'25 had 700+ attendees from 65 countries, IASEAI'26 had 1300+ from 99+ countries). Advises UN, OECD, EU on AI governance and helped shape EU AI Act. Develops practical tools including AI scorecards, ethical certification programs, and Safe AI Deployment Toolkit (SAIDT).
