Key leaders include Stuart Russell (President) and Mark Nitzberg (Interim Executive Director).

name: Centre pour la Sécurité de l'IA (CeSIA)
notes_head: Founded March 2024 by Charbel-Raphaël Ségerie and team. ~20 employees plus volunteers. Funded by Survival and Flourishing Fund, Longview Philanthropy, Swedish foundation, Effektiv Spenden. No AI company funding to avoid conflicts. Provides Europe's first university AI safety courses at ENS Ulm/Paris-Saclay. Initiated Global Call for AI Red Lines with 300+ signatories including Nobel laureates. Co-organized symposiums with Yoshua Bengio and Stuart Russell.

name: Centre for Enabling EA Learning & Research (CEEALAR)
notes_head: Founded 2018 by Greg Colbourn, registered charity in England & Wales since 2020. Provides subsidized accommodation and workspace in Blackpool for EA researchers and entrepreneurs at £750/month. Supports ~20 residents at a time with 4-6 month average stays. 66 grantees in 2025 (+36% YoY). Notable alumni include Chris Leong (Principal Organiser for AI Safety New Zealand) and Hoagy Cunningham (Stanford Existential Risks Initiative). Formerly known as 'EA Hotel', rebranded in 2020. Currently facing £330k funding gap and needs £30k for urgent roof repairs.

name: AI Governance and Safety Institute (AIGSI)
notes_head: The AI Governance and Safety Institute (AIGSI) is a nonpartisan 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization on a mission to ensure artificial intelligence and other technologies benefit humanity and are developed safely, securely, and in alignment with human values. The organization aims to improve institutional response to the risk that future AI might cause human extinction and ensure the enormous potential benefits of AI are realized. They conduct research, engage in outreach, and educate stakeholders and the general public. The organization focuses on key technical concepts including machine learning

name: AI Safety Asia (AISA)
loc: Hong Kong (global operations across 16 Asian countries)
notes_head: Founded 2024 by Supheakmungkol Sarin (former Google AI lead and World Economic Forum Head of Data & AI) and Edward Tsoi. Team of 13 employees across 7 countries. Has engaged 2,000+ AI governance professionals across 16 Asian countries. Co-hosted sessions at India AI Impact Summit 2026 on crisis diplomacy and contributed to International AI Safety Report 2026. Focuses on building durable governance infrastructure through regional research, structured peer learning, and implementation-oriented engagement.

name: Athena Mentorship Program for Women (AI Alignment Research)
loc: Remote program with rotating in-person retreats (previously Oxford UK, location varies)
notes_head: Founded 2023 by Claire Short. 10-week remote mentorship program with 1-week in-person retreat. Targets women transitioning into or early in AI alignment research careers. Funded through Manifund ($20k goal reached), Lightspeed grants. Requires 20+ hours/week commitment. Provides mentorship from established AI safety researchers, weekly research talks, networking opportunities, and potential placement in research groups post-program. Has run multiple cohorts with positive participant feedback.

name: Center for AI Policy (CAIP)
notes_head: Founded 2023, shut down in September 2025 due to lack of funding. Was a 501(c)(4) advocacy organization that conducted over 406 meetings with Congressional staffers, hosted 8 congressional briefings reaching 150+ staffers, and developed the 'Responsible AI Act' model legislation. Funded primarily by Survival and Flourishing Fund. Executive Director Jason Green-Lowe continues to offer legislative review services and seeks funding to revive the organization.
From AISafety.com map. 12/14-week program run by Fifty Years, helping scientists and engineers build startups working on AI safety.

name: Collective Action for Existential Safety
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Aiming to catalyze collective effort towards reducing existential risk, including through an extensive action list for individuals, organizations, and nations.

name: Computational Rational Agents Laboratory
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Research group studying agent foundations in order to create the mathematical tools to align the objectives of an AI with human values.

name: Dovetail
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Research group working on foundational mathematics that provides an understanding of the nature of AI agents.

name: Computational and Biological Learning Lab
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Research group using engineering approaches to understand the brain and to develop artificial learning systems.

name: Krueger AI Safety Lab
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. AI safety research group at Mila, led by David Krueger. Previously based at the University of Cambridge.

name: Model Evaluation & Threat Research
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Researching, developing, and running evaluations of AI capabilities, including broad autonomous capabilities and the ability of AI systems to conduct AI R&D.

name: NYU Alignment Research Group
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Group of researchers at New York University doing empirical work with LLMs in order to address risks posed by advanced AI.

name: Centre pour la Sécurité de l'IA
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. French nonprofit dedicated to research, education, and awareness-raising about the most extreme risks of advanced AI.

name: Berkeley Existential Risk Initiative
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Providing flexible funding and operations support to university research groups working on existential risk, enabling projects otherwise hindered by university administration.

name: Centre for Enabling EA Learning & Research (EA Hotel)
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Free or subsidised accommodation and board in Blackpool, England, for people working on/transitioning to working on global catastrophic risks.

name: Constellation
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Center for collaborative research in AI safety, supporting promising work through fellowships, an incubator, and hosting individuals and teams.

name: European Network for AI Safety
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Community of researchers and policymakers from over 13 countries across Europe, united in their efforts to advance AI safety.

name: London Initiative for Safe AI
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Coworking space hosting organizations (including BlueDot Impact, Apollo Research), acceleration programs (including MATS, ARENA), and independent researchers.

name: Forethought
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Small research nonprofit focused on how to navigate the transition to a world with superintelligent AI systems.

name: AI Safety Ideas
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Crowdsourced repository of possible research projects and testable hypotheses, run by Apart Research.

name: Arkose
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. AI safety field-building nonprofit. Ran support programs facilitating technical research, did outreach, and curated educational resources. Closed due to lack of funding.

name: AI Timeline
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Visual overview of the major events in AI over the last decade, from cultural trends to technical advancements. No longer maintained.

name: Global Priorities Institute
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. University of Oxford research center conducting foundational research to inform the decision-making of those seeking to do as much good as possible. Closed in 2025.

name: Machine Learning for Alignment Bootcamp
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Bootcamp aimed at teaching ML relevant to doing alignment research. Run by Redwood Research for two iterations in 2022.

name: AI Safety Awareness Project
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Raising awareness about modern AI, highlighting its benefits and risks, and letting the public know how they can help – mainly through workshops.

name: Safe AI London
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Supported individuals in London interested in working on AI safety by raising awareness of the risks and providing high-quality resources and support.

name: Alignment Ecosystem Development
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Building and maintaining key online resources for the AI safety community, including AISafety.com and AISafety.info. Volunteers welcome.

name: Compassion in Machine Learning
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Working on research to make transformative AI more compassionate towards all sentient beings through new data generation methods.

name: Open Machine
notes_head: Founded by CEO Allie K. Miller, focuses on CPU-first AI deployment without GPU requirements. Develops edge-native AI solutions with unified multimodal runtime. Emphasizes data sovereignty and offline capability. Claims sub-60ms recall performance with <2.4GB memory footprint on commodity hardware.

name: Samaya AI
notes_head: Founded 2022 as AI platform for financial services, not AI safety/alignment. Raised $43.5M Series A led by NEA in 2025. Team includes 30+ members from Google Brain, Meta AI, AWS, Stanford. Develops specialized AI agents for investment research and financial analysis workflows, deployed at Morgan Stanley and major banks with 10,000+ professional users.

name: FutureHouse
notes_head: Founded November 2023 as nonprofit by Sam Rodriques and Andrew White. Backed by Eric Schmidt. Mission to build 'AI Scientists' for biology research over 10 years. Launched platform with specialized AI agents (Crow, Falcon, Owl, Phoenix) in May 2025. Spun out commercial entity Edison Scientific in November 2025 raising $70M. Operates wet lab for human-AI collaboration.

name: Credo AI
notes_head: Enterprise AI governance platform company founded by Navrina Singh. Provides software and advisory services for AI risk management and regulatory compliance. Named leader in Forrester Wave AI Governance Solutions 2025 and recognized in Gartner Market Guide. Has partnerships with Microsoft, McKinsey, and Databricks.

name: Bayesian Logic Inc.
notes_head: Bayesian Logic Inc. is co-founded by Nimar Arora, who serves as Chief Executive Officer, Secretary, Chief Financial Officer, and Director. Stuart Russell serves as a Director and is described as founder and Vice-President of the company. The company was officially filed as a General Corporation in California on October 24, 2011, and maintains active status. Bayesian Logic Inc. developed NET-VISA, a Bayesian seismic monitoring system for detecting nuclear explosions, which has been deployed by the CTBT organization of the U.N. and achieves around 60% reduction in missed events compared to curre

name: Responsible AI Initiative
notes_head: Multiple organizations operate under the name "Responsible AI Initiative" with different scopes and affiliations. The University of Utah operates the One-U Responsible AI Initiative, which aims to advance AI to achieve societal good while protecting privacy, civil rights, and civil liberties. Emory University launched a Responsible AI website as part of their enterprise-wide Responsible AI initiative, led by the Emory AI Group with input from the Responsible AI Executive Governance Committee. Carnegie Mellon University launched a Responsible AI initiative out of the Block Center for Technology

name: International Association for Safe and Ethical AI
notes_head: The International Association for Safe and Ethical AI (IASEAI) is a non-profit organization founded in 2024 with headquarters in Paris, France. The organization's stated mission is to address the risks and opportunities associated with advances in artificial intelligence and to ensure that AI systems operate safely and ethically, benefiting all of humanity. IASEAI held its inaugural conference, IASEAI '25, on February 6–7, 2025 in Paris, which brought together experts from academia, civil society, industry, and government to discuss developments in AI safety and ethics. The organization is led

name: Stanford Center for AI Safety
notes_head: Stanford University research center founded in 2018 by Clark Barrett, David Dill, Mykel Kochenderfer, and Dorsa Sadigh, operating as an industrial affiliates program. Conducts research in formal verification, robust decision-making, and trustworthy AI; distinct from the independent nonprofit Center for AI Safety (safe.ai) run by Dan Hendrycks. Focus spans technical safety, fairness, accountability, and policy.

name: Eleos AI
notes_head: Nonprofit research org focused on AI welfare, consciousness, and moral patienthood. Co-founded by Kyle Fish, Robert Long, Kathleen Finlinson. Published landmark report "Taking AI Welfare Seriously." Commended Anthropic's model welfare efforts. Five research priorities: welfare interventions, human-AI cooperation, leveraging AI for welfare research, standardized evaluations, credible communication.

name: Model Evaluation and Threat Research
notes_head: Nonprofit AI safety eval org (formerly ARC Evals, spun off Dec 2023). Founded by Beth Barnes (ex-OpenAI). Evaluates frontier model capabilities for catastrophic risk. Worked with OpenAI (o3, GPT-4.5) and Anthropic (Claude). Does NOT accept lab funding to avoid conflicts of interest. Budget ~$13-15M/year. Funded via Audacious Project (TED) and donations. Paul Christiano was originally involved but stepped back for AISI role.

name: Trust::Data Alliance
notes_head: MIT-based consortium (Trust::Data Consortium) founded by Alex 'Sandy' Pentland. Builds open-source tools for secure, privacy-preserving data sharing using blockchain and AI. Mission: create trusted internet-based network for safe data exchange. Focus areas: data cooperatives, privacy-preserving identity systems, safe distributed computation. Advocates collective data governance model over individualized data control. Part of MIT Connection Science ecosystem.

name: Trail of Bits
notes_head: Leading cybersecurity research, engineering, and consulting firm founded in 2012. Pioneered AI security assurance practice combining safety and security methodologies for ML/AI risk assessment — Engineering Director of AI Assurance team is Heidy Khlaaf. Won $3M second-place prize in DARPA's AI Cyber Challenge (AIxCC) with their 'Buttercup' system. Clients include Facebook/Meta, DARPA, and major cryptocurrency protocols. Strong open-source ethos — builds and maintains public security tools across static/dynamic analysis, AI/ML security, and fuzzing. Member of OpenSSF. As of 2026, has 94 plugins

name: SERI-MATS
notes_head: ML Alignment & Theory Scholars (MATS, formerly SERI MATS) — independent research and educational seminar program connecting talented researchers with top AI alignment mentors. Originally ran under the Stanford Existential Risks Initiative (SERI), now independent. Three-phase structure: remote Training Phase, in-person Research Phase in Berkeley, and Extension Phase in London/Berkeley. Provides funding, housing, training, office space, and research assistance. Alumni have gone on to Anthropic, OpenAI, Google DeepMind, and founded research groups like Apollo Research, Timaeus, and Cadenza Labs.

name: AI Safety Dublin
notes_head: Grassroots AI safety community in Dublin, Ireland, working to reduce risks from advanced AI through awareness, upskilling, and community building. Part of the European Network for AI Safety (ENAIS). Runs 8-week reading/discussion groups for newcomers to AI safety, an Intro to AI Safety Governance program, and an AI Safety Alignment technical program. Hosts 48-hour Alignment Jam Sessions for collaborative research. Welcomes AI enthusiasts, researchers, and those concerned about AI futures.

name: Dutch Network for AI Safety
notes_head: Field-building organization launched in collaboration with ENAIS to establish a national AI safety community in the Netherlands. Builds connections and pathways to reduce catastrophic AI risk. Focuses on lowering barriers to entry, linking participants to courses, mentoring, and career opportunities in AI safety research, policy, and governance. Plans to expand into policy engagement with European partners, research exchanges, and development of a dedicated AI safety hub in the Netherlands. Growth is iterative, based on evidence and community needs.

name: Cambridge AI Safety Hub
notes_head: AI safety community hub at the University of Cambridge, co-directed by Gaurav Yadav and Justin Dollman. Runs MARS (Mentorship for Alignment Research Students), an AI Safety Fast-Track program, a writing programme, and regular paper reading groups with external researchers.

name: Berkeley AI Safety Initiative for Students (BASIS)
notes_head: Student-run AI safety organization at UC Berkeley. Runs a DeCal course on AI safety, a research program, AI Policy Reading Group, and tech education workshops. Affiliated with SPAR (Supervised Program for Alignment Research) and the broader EA-adjacent safety community.

name: Center for AI Safety Action Fund
notes_head: 501(c)(4) advocacy arm of the Center for AI Safety (CAIS/safe.ai). Nonpartisan advocacy org focused on AI policy to maintain U.S. leadership and protect against AI-related national security threats. Policy built on three pillars from "Superintelligence Strategy" (Hendrycks, Schmidt, Wang): Nonproliferation (chip export controls, location verification), Deterrence (mandatory reporting for frontier models, intelligence monitoring), Competitiveness (domestic chip production, AI talent immigration). Led by Varun Krovi (Exec Dir) and Brian McGrail (Policy Lead). Member of ASAP Coalition. Distinct f

name: Electric Sheep
notes_head: Nonprofit (501(c)(3) fiscally sponsored) dedicated to harnessing AI for animal welfare, environmental protection, and social good. Runs Futurekind Fellowship (12-week AI for Animal Protection program) and Code for Compassion Campus (in-person accelerator in Bengaluru). Founded by Ash, former Chief Economist at Royal Society of Arts who co-founded Forum for Ethical AI with DeepMind. Partner of CaML (Compassion in Machine Learning). Focus areas: safe AI/AGI, alignment for environmental/social/animal protection.

name: Arkose Labs
loc: San Mateo, CA (with offices in Brisbane, London, Costa Rica)
notes_head: Founded 2013 by Kevin Gosschalk. 250+ employees. Raised $114M+ from SoftBank Vision Fund, Microsoft M12, PayPal, Sony Innovation Fund. Commercial cybersecurity company developing fraud prevention platform 'Arkose Titan' with specialized focus on AI agent detection and classification. This appears to be a different organization from the AI safety nonprofit 'Arkose' mentioned in the original notes that closed due to lack of funding.

name: Amazon
notes_head: Amazon has developed frontier AI models through its Nova family and operates Amazon Bedrock AI service. Published comprehensive Frontier Model Safety Framework in 2024 with critical capability thresholds for CBRN weapons, cybersecurity, and automated AI R&D. AWS CEO Andy Jassy projects AI could help AWS reach $600 billion in annual sales by 2036, double previous estimates. Major cloud infrastructure provider enabling AI development across the industry.

name: Cassava Technologies
notes_head: Founded by African entrepreneur Strive Masiyiwa, headquartered in London with operations across 94 countries. Built 110,000km of fiber infrastructure and 9 operational data centers. Launched Africa's first AI factory in 2024/2025 powered by NVIDIA, with partnerships including Microsoft, Google, AWS, and Anthropic. 1,848 employees as of 2025.

name: Scale AI
notes_head: Founded 2016 by Alexandr Wang at age 19, focuses on AI data infrastructure and labeling. Over 900 employees, valued at $14B after $1B Series F in May 2024. Major DoD contracts including multi-million dollar 'Thunderforge' AI agents deal. Wang stepped down as CEO in June 2025 to lead Meta's 'Superintelligence' unit as part of $14.3B Meta investment.

name: Midjourney
notes_head: Founded in 2022 by David Holz (Leap Motion co-founder). Operates with only 11 full-time employees, generating ~$300M revenue in 2024 without venture capital. Faces major lawsuits from Disney/NBCUniversal over alleged copyright infringement in AI training data. Released V7 model in April 2025 after nearly year-long gap.

name: Salesforce
notes_head: Founded 1999, publicly traded CRM company with 70,000+ employees. Launched Einstein AI platform in 2016, Agentforce AI agents in 2024. Chief Ethical and Humane Use Officer Paula Goldman sits on U.S. National AI Advisory Committee. Has comprehensive AI governance framework with cross-functional AI Council and established Trusted AI principles in 2018.

name: Unitree Robotics
notes_head: Founded 2016 by Wang Xingxing, ~500 employees. Ships over 5,500 humanoid robots annually (2025), leading global market. Products include G1 ($16k) and H1 humanoid robots, plus quadruped models. Subject of US Congressional hearings over cybersecurity vulnerabilities and alleged military ties. Planning IPO with Chinese government 'national champion' status.

name: Mobileye
notes_head: Founded 1999, focusing on autonomous driving and ADAS technologies. Develops EyeQ system-on-chip processors and safety frameworks like Responsibility-Sensitive Safety (RSS). Over 230 million vehicles built with Mobileye technology through 2025. Listed independently from Intel in 2022, generated $1.894B revenue in 2025. Agreed to acquire Mentee Robotics in 2026 to expand into humanoid robotics.

name: Tools for Humanity
loc: San Francisco, California and Munich, Germany
notes_head: Founded 2019 by Alex Blania (CEO) and Sam Altman (Chairman). Develops iris-scanning 'Orb' devices for World ID verification system. Over 500 employees, $2.5B valuation, raised $240M+ from investors including Andreessen Horowitz. 17.5 million verified users as of 2024, targeting 1 billion users.

name: ElevenLabs
notes_head: Founded 2022, raised $281M total including $180M Series C (Jan 2025) at $3.3B valuation. 120 employees across London, New York, Warsaw. Specializes in AI voice generation, cloning, and conversational agents. First company to secure AI agent insurance through AIUC-1 certification. Partners with governments including Ukraine, Czech Republic, and UK on public services.

name: Agility Robotics
notes_head: Founded 2015 as Oregon State University spinout, grew to 300+ employees. Launched Digit humanoid robot in 2020, first commercially deployed humanoid. Raised $400M in 2025 at $1.75B valuation. Manufacturing facility 'RoboFab' in Salem with 100% US assembly.

name: Pony.ai
notes_head: Founded 2016, specializes in autonomous driving technology for robotaxis and robotrucks. Raised $800M+ via Hong Kong IPO in 2025, operates 1,446 vehicle fleet as of March 2026. Achieved unit economics breakeven in Guangzhou and Shenzhen. Targets 3,000+ robotaxis across 20+ global cities by end of 2026.

name: KoBold Metals
notes_head: Founded 2018, uses AI for mineral exploration targeting copper, lithium, nickel, and cobalt. Raised $1.2B total including $537M Series C (Jan 2025), valued at $2.96B. Discovered major copper deposit in Zambia (Mingomba project) expected to produce 300,000+ tons annually by 2030s. Backed by Bill Gates, Jeff Bezos, Andreessen Horowitz.

name: Adobe
notes_head: Founded 1982, major creative software company that has integrated AI into products for over a decade. Developed Firefly generative AI model trained exclusively on licensed content, offers enterprise indemnification. Key AI products include Firefly, GenStudio, and AI assistants across Creative Cloud. Founded Content Authenticity Initiative in 2019 with 5,000+ members to combat misinformation through Content Credentials technology.

name: Surge AI
notes_head: Founded 2020 by Edwin Chen (ex-Google/Facebook/Twitter). Bootstrapped to $1.2B revenue in 2024 serving OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, Meta with high-quality human data labeling. 250 employees managing 50K+ expert contractors globally. Seeking first external funding of $1B at ~$24B valuation in 2025.

name: Tryolabs
notes_head: Founded 2009 as AI/ML consulting company, 15+ years before mainstream AI adoption. Grew from 4 people to 100+ employees serving enterprise clients globally. CEO Alan Descoins named in TIME's '100 Most Influential People in AI' 2025. Focuses on custom AI solutions, MLOps, and 'AI for Good' projects including conservation and sustainability initiatives.

name: Splice
notes_head: Founded 2013, music creation platform with 600K+ paying subscribers generating $100M+ annual revenue. Acquired Spitfire Audio for ~$50M in 2025. Hit 350M sample downloads in 2024, focuses on AI-powered music creation tools that amplify rather than replace human creativity.

name: Shield AI
notes_head: Founded 2015, mission to protect service members with autonomous systems. Raised $240M at $5.3B valuation (March 2025), reportedly seeking $1B at $12B valuation. ~$267M revenue in 2024. First AI pilot deployed in combat (2018), selected for Air Force Collaborative Combat Aircraft program. Products include Hivemind autonomy software, V-BAT drones.

name: Anduril Industries
notes_head: Defense technology company founded in 2017 by Palmer Luckey, building AI-powered autonomous weapons, surveillance systems, and battlefield software (Lattice OS). Awarded a $20B Army AI contract in 2026. Luckey publicly opposes private corporate restrictions on military AI use, arguing elected government—not corporate executives—should set AI weapons policy.

name: Tesla
notes_head: Tesla announced a joint project with xAI called Digital Optimus, also nicknamed 'Macrohard,' designed to automate complex office workflows by observing and replicating human interactions with computers. As part of this collaboration, Tesla made a $2 billion investment in xAI in 2026. Tesla's financial performance declined significantly in 2025, with profits falling 46 percent and revenue dropping for the first time in the company's history. The company's automotive revenues fell by 11 percent to $17.7 billion in Q4 2025, though energy storage business grew 25 percent.

name: Stripe
notes_head: Stripe was founded in 2010 by two Irish brothers, Patrick Collison (CEO) and John Collison (President), who came to the US in 2006 at ages 18 and 16 respectively. In 2025, businesses running on Stripe generated $1.9 trillion in total volume, up 34% from 2024, and equivalent to roughly 1.6% of global GDP. Stripe now powers more than 5 million businesses directly or via platforms, including 90% of the Dow Jones Industrial Average and 80% of the Nasdaq 100. The company has developed AI-focused products including the Stripe integration benchmark for evaluating AI agents' ability to build complete

name: Palantir
notes_head: Palantir Technologies is a data analytics and AI company that builds software platforms for data integration and analysis, serving government and commercial clients. The company was founded in 2003 by Peter Thiel and others, with Alex Karp joining as CEO in 2004. Palantir's Maven AI system has been adopted by the Pentagon as a core military system for weapons targeting and battlefield data analysis. The company reported Q4 2025 revenue of $1.41 billion, up 70% year-over-year, with a market capitalization of approximately $100.6 billion.

name: Coinbase
notes_head: Coinbase is joining the x402 Foundation alongside tech titans like Amazon, Google, and Stripe. Coinbase has launched crypto wallet infrastructure that allows AI agents to spend, earn and trade crypto. The company's mission is to increase economic freedom for more than 1 billion people by providing a trusted platform for crypto assets. Coinbase delivered strong Q4 and full year 2024 results, with full year revenue of $6.6 billion, net income of $2.6 billion, and Adjusted EBITDA of $3.3 billion.

name: LinkedIn
notes_head: Microsoft-owned professional networking platform (~1B members). Major deployer of AI in hiring and workforce tools. Launched 'Hiring Assistant' (Oct 2024), its first AI agent product, handling job description creation, candidate sourcing, and screening — live with AMD, Canva, Siemens, Zurich Insurance. AI-Assisted Candidate Discovery uses GenAI to analyze job requirements and match candidates via natural language queries. In Sept 2024, updated Terms of Service to allow member data for GenAI training (opt-out, default ON), drawing privacy concerns. Reports 60% of recruiters say AI helps find 'h

name: Darktrace
notes_head: UK-headquartered AI cybersecurity company founded in 2013 in Cambridge. Acquired by private equity firm Thoma Bravo in October 2024 for $5.3B (previously publicly traded on London Stock Exchange FTSE100). Uses self-learning AI to detect and respond to cyber threats in real time. Published responsible AI principles for cybersecurity, informed by collaboration with academia and governments and analysis of NIST AI RMF, EU AI Act, UK AI White Paper, and OECD AI Principles. Engages proactively with regulators; AI optimist supporting pro-innovation policy while emphasizing cybersecurity as a prerequ

name: Quantexa
notes_head: UK-based AI decision intelligence company founded in 2016, headquartered in London. Raised $175M Series F (March 2025) at $2.6B valuation, led by Teachers' Venture Growth (Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan); other investors include Warburg Pincus, Dawn Capital, BNY, British Patient Capital. Specializes in connecting and analyzing siloed data for customer intelligence, risk management, financial crime, and fraud detection. Processes data for major banks and governments. Works with UK Cabinet Office and Public Sector Fraud Authority (PSFA) on AI-powered fraud detection. CEO Vishal Marria met Chance

name: Evolution AI
notes_head: UK-based AI document processing company founded in 2015 by Martin Goodson and Rafal Kwasny, headquartered in London (Covent Garden). Combines computer vision and NLP to extract data from financial statements, invoices, bank statements, and other documents. Received the UK Government's largest-ever grant for commercial AI R&D in 2017, with further substantial government grants in 2019 and 2020. Processes 1.5M+ pages per day. Clients include NatWest, Deutsche Bank, Dun & Bradstreet. Won Credit Strategy 'Best Technology Partner' (2023) and AI Breakthrough Awards 'Best Machine Learning Company' (2

name: IBM
notes_head: IBM is a global enterprise technology company offering AI via its watsonx platform. It advocates 'precision regulation'—risk-based, use-case-specific rules rather than broad AI restrictions. IBM reported $12.5B in generative AI bookings as of early 2026 and positions AI governance software as a primary growth driver.

name: Khan Academy
notes_head: Khan Academy is a major nonprofit educational technology organization that has become a prominent AI deployer through Khanmigo, its AI-powered tutoring assistant built on OpenAI's GPT-4. Launched by Sal Khan, Khanmigo reached 1.4 million users by 2025, though Khan has acknowledged that for many students it was a "non-event." The tool uses Socratic design principles — guiding students rather than giving answers. Khan Academy partnered with Microsoft for free educator access via Azure OpenAI. The organization maintains strict data privacy: student data is not sold and OpenAI cannot train on it.

name: Formation Bio
notes_head: Formation Bio is an AI-driven pharmaceutical development company that acquires clinical-stage drugs and accelerates their development using a proprietary tech platform. Its platform integrates AI-driven drug development, a clinical trial engine, and a universal data platform to automate medical writing, protocol development, regulatory submissions, and biostatistics. In November 2024, Formation launched Muse in partnership with OpenAI and Sanofi for AI-powered drug asset identification. The company raised $372M in Series D funding and was named to the 2025 CNBC Disruptor 50 list. Funded by Eme

name: Perplexity AI
notes_head: Perplexity AI is a VC-backed AI-powered search and answer engine founded in 2022, valued at ~$20B as of late 2025. CEO Aravind Srinivas has resisted content restrictions and embraced government partnerships, including a FedRAMP-approved deal with the U.S. GSA.

name: Alphabet Inc.
notes_head: Parent company of Google, Google DeepMind, and other subsidiaries. One of the world's largest AI developers and deployers. Published AI Principles in 2018; controversially removed pledges against AI use in weapons and surveillance in February 2025, drawing criticism from lawmakers and civil society. Supports 'multi-stakeholder' AI governance: risk assessments for high-risk systems, sector-specific regulation, but lobbied for 10-year ban on state-level AI regulation in U.S. House package. Publishes annual Responsible AI Progress Reports. Google DeepMind leadership (Hassabis, Manyika) stated dem

name: Cognizant
notes_head: Cognizant is a major IT services and consulting company (NYSE: CTSH) with a significant AI practice. Positions itself as an "AI builder" creating full-stack AI solutions for enterprise clients. In March 2026, launched Cognizant AI Factory — an enterprise-grade multi-tenant platform powered by Dell and NVIDIA, claiming 50-60% lower TCO and 30% faster AI processing. Acquired 3Cloud (leading Microsoft Azure services provider) in November 2025 to strengthen AI enablement on Azure. Published "New Work, New World 2026" report on AI reshaping work. Recognized as a leader in Everest Group's AI and Gen

name: Workera
notes_head: Workera is an AI-powered skills intelligence platform that verifies skills for any role, enabling enterprise workforce development and talent decisions. Founded by Kian Katanforoosh, who was mentored by Andrew Ng at Stanford. Andrew Ng provides strategic guidance on responsible AI in education and workforce development. Raised $44.5M over 4 rounds. Clients include U.S. Air Force, Marine Corps, Booz Allen Hamilton, Siemens Energy, BCG, Accenture, and Eli Lilly. Launched AI agent "Sage" in late 2024 (GA March 2025). Published 2026 AI Workforce Preview showing 76% of Americans plan to learn new A

name: LandingAI
notes_head: LandingAI is an AI company founded by Andrew Ng focused on computer vision for industrial applications. Flagship product LandingLens is an end-to-end, low-code platform for labeling, training, and deploying custom vision models. Primary industries: manufacturing, automotive, electronics, medical devices — companies relying on visual inspection and quality control. In 2025 released VisionAgent, a generative visual AI application builder with agentic object detection that eliminates manual data labeling and model training. Ng serves as Executive Chairman. Ng is a prominent advocate of "data-cent

name: Clarifai
notes_head: Enterprise AI platform founded in 2013 by Matt Zeiler, headquartered in Washington, D.C. One of the first deep learning platforms, specializing in computer vision, NLP, and audio analysis. Core technology based on convolutional neural networks. Key government/defense work: participated in Project Maven (DoD AI program), signed R&D agreement with U.S. Army for EO/IR data labeling, created subsidiary Neural Net One for dedicated government contracting. Raised $100M total ($60M Series C in 2021, led by New Enterprise Associates). Named a Leader in Forrester Wave Computer Vision Tools Q1 2024. Pla

name: ServiceNow
notes_head: Public enterprise software company (NYSE: NOW) specializing in workflow automation. Heavily invested in AI via 'Now Assist' and autonomous AI agents; targeting $1B AI ACV by 2026. Advocates responsible AI governance and compliance with EU AI Act and NIST AI RMF.

name: Apple
notes_head: Consumer technology company deploying AI via Apple Intelligence across its device ecosystem. Pursues an on-device, privacy-first AI strategy using Private Cloud Compute for sensitive tasks. Partnered with OpenAI (ChatGPT integration) and Google (Gemini for Siri). Avoids public regulatory advocacy but leverages privacy standards as a competitive differentiator.

name: X
notes_head: X (formerly Twitter), owned by Elon Musk, integrates the Grok AI chatbot developed by xAI. In March 2025, xAI formally acquired X in an all-stock deal. X has faced EU and UK regulatory investigations over Grok-generated deepfake images and has deployed content restrictions in response.

name: Jukedeck
notes_head: British AI music startup (founded 2012 by Ed Newton-Rex) that built a platform for generating royalty-free music using AI. Raised $2.5M from Cambridge Innovation Capital, Parkwalk Advisors, Backed VC, Playfair Capital. Acquired by ByteDance/TikTok in July 2019; site went offline post-acquisition. Technology integrated into ByteDance's AI music capabilities. Newton-Rex became director of ByteDance AI Lab, then VP Audio at Stability AI (later resigned over copyright stance).

name: ByteDance
notes_head: Chinese tech giant (est. 2012), parent company of TikTok. Major AI capabilities in recommendation algorithms, content moderation, generative AI. Spent $5.4M on US lobbying in H1 2025 (41 lobbyists). Acquired AI music startup Jukedeck (2019). Subject to PAFACA law requiring divestiture of US TikTok operations; deal closed Jan 2026 creating TikTok USDS (with Oracle, Silver Lake, MGX). Developing large language models and AI tools internally. One of the most heavily scrutinized tech companies in US-China AI competition.

name: TikTok
notes_head: Short-form video platform owned by ByteDance, central to US-China tech tensions. Subject to PAFACA ban law (signed April 2024, effective Jan 2025). After multiple Trump executive order delays, US operations divested to TikTok USDS (Jan 2026) consortium including Oracle, Silver Lake, MGX. Uses AI extensively: recommendation algorithm, content moderation (85%+ AI removal rate), creator tools. Requires AI-generated content labeling. Revised Community Guidelines (Sept 2025). ~150M US users. Acquired Jukedeck (2019) for AI music tech.

name: Adaptive Security
notes_head: AI-native cybersecurity startup founded by serial entrepreneurs Brian Long (CEO) and Andrew Jones. Builds platform simulating AI-powered attacks — deepfake voice/video phishing, smishing, and GenAI social engineering — to train corporate defenders. Raised $136M+ total: $43M round co-led by OpenAI and Andreessen Horowitz (OpenAI's first cybersecurity investment), plus $55M Series A (April 2025) led by a16z and OpenAI Startup Fund. Platform includes real-time threat triage, personalized training, and AI-driven risk scoring. Also partnered with Elliston Berry and Pathos Consulting Group to create

name: Mayo Clinic
notes_head: World-renowned academic medical center with 200+ AI projects across clinical, research, and operational applications. Unified EHR system supports AI at scale; operates an AI factory on Google Cloud Vertex AI for 250+ projects. AI applications include early detection of cardiovascular disease, breast cancer, pancreatic cancer, neuromuscular disease, and mental health conditions. Runs Mayo Clinic Platform for data-driven innovation and responsible AI in healthcare. Partnership with Goodfire for AI interpretability in genomic medicine. Harper Family Foundation AI Education in Medicine Program tra

name: Radical AI
notes_head: NYC-based autonomous materials science discovery company founded in 2024. Uses AI to screen billions of material compositions, predict structures and properties, then synthesize and characterize candidates in a closed-loop self-driving lab. Claims 370x acceleration over traditional 15-25 year materials discovery timelines. Lab produces 25+ alloys per day. Raised $55M Series Seed+ led by RTX Ventures. Signed MOU with U.S. Department of Energy for Genesis Mission. Establishing fully autonomous labs at Brooklyn Navy Yard (115 new jobs). Partners with Goodfire for AI interpretability in materials

name: Piramidal
notes_head: AI neurotech startup building a foundation model for understanding brainwaves (EEG data). Founded in 2024 by Dimitris Fotis Sakellariou and Kris Pahuja. Y Combinator W24 batch. Raised $6.5M led by YC with participation from Adverb Ventures, Lionheart Ventures, and angel investors. Initial focus on neuro ICU applications for real-time seizure and brain dysfunction detection. Piloting in hospitals in England (King's College and Saint Thomas). Headquartered in San Francisco.

name: Elicit
notes_head: AI research assistant that uses language models to automate parts of researchers' workflows, including finding papers, extracting data, and synthesizing findings. Originally incubated at Ought (a nonprofit ML research lab), spun off as an independent public benefit corporation in September 2023. Raised $9M seed led by Fifty Years (with Basis Set, Mythos Ventures, and angels including GitHub co-founder Tom Preston-Werner and Dropbox co-founder Arash Ferdowsi), then $22M Series A at $100M valuation led by Spark Capital and Footwork. Searches over 126M academic papers. Well-known in EA/research c

name: EliseAI
notes_head: NYC-based AI company (founded 2017) building conversational AI agents for housing and healthcare. Co-founded by CEO Minna Song (MIT CS) and Tony Stoyanov. Platform automates leasing, maintenance, and resident communications for property managers. Powers ~10% of U.S. apartment market, 600+ operators including 75% of NMHC Top 50. Raised $250M Series E (led by a16z, Sep 2025) at $100M+ ARR, 300+ employees. Previously raised $75M Series D (Aug 2024) at $1B valuation. NOT a VC firm — should be reclassified as Deployers & Platforms.

name: Partnership on AI
notes_head: Partnership on AI (PAI) is a nonprofit global community of academic, civil society, and industry organizations who come together to address the most important questions in AI. PAI is an independent, nonprofit 501(c)(3) organization and funded by charitable contributions from philanthropy and corporate entities. The organization has 141 partners from 19 countries and focuses on advancing the safety and responsibility of AI systems. PAI was originally established by a coalition of representatives from technology companies, civil society organizations, and academic institutions, and supported ori

name: NoHarm
notes_head: Search results show NOHARM as a medical AI safety evaluation framework developed by academic researchers, not a traditional ethics/rights organization. Also found #NoHarmware campaign by Tāhono Trust (New Zealand) focused on online safety regulation. No evidence of a US-based Ethics/Rights Organization specifically named 'NoHarm' found in search results.

name: Humane Intelligence
notes_head: 501(c)(3) nonprofit founded by Dr. Rumman Chowdhury and Jutta Williams, with Mala Kumar as Interim Executive Director as of August 2025. Specializes in AI red teaming, bias bounties, and contextual evaluations. Has conducted 30+ evaluations with 5,250+ participants across 55+ countries. Plans to release red teaming app as open source in 2026.

name: Human Rights Watch
notes_head: Founded 1978, international human rights organization with staff in 40+ countries. Co-founder of Stop Killer Robots campaign (270+ civil society organizations). Published major 2025 report 'A Hazard to Human Rights: Autonomous Weapons Systems and Digital Decision-Making'. Led joint statement with 86 organizations urging U.S. Congress to regulate AI in 2023.

name: Mozilla Foundation
notes_head: Founded 1998 as nonprofit. President Mark Surman leads trustworthy AI initiative since 2019. Has ~$1.4B in reserves and operates Mozilla Ventures ($35M initial fund) investing in 55+ AI startups. Key initiatives include Common Voice (100+ language datasets), Mozilla Technology Fund ($1.3M for trustworthy AI projects), and advocacy for EU AI Act transparency provisions.

name: DAIR
notes_head: Distributed AI Research Institute founded December 2021 by Timnit Gebru after her dismissal from Google. Documents AI harms on marginalized communities globally, with focus on Africa. Originated the TESCREAL critique arguing AGI ideology has eugenicist roots. Funded by Ford Foundation, MacArthur Foundation, Kapor Center, and Open Society Foundations.

name: Algorithmic Justice League
notes_head: Founded by Joy Buolamwini to fight algorithmic bias

name: Electronic Frontier Foundation
notes_head: Leading digital rights nonprofit founded in 1990. Opposes AI-powered surveillance and use of AI in high-stakes government decisions. Advocates for civil-liberties-based, targeted AI regulation protecting privacy and due process.

name: ACLU
notes_head: The American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) is a nonpartisan nonprofit and 501(c)(4) advocacy organization defending constitutional rights. Its Speech, Privacy & Technology Project focuses on AI-driven surveillance, opposing facial recognition use by law enforcement, and calling for targeted civil rights protections. The ACLU supports the AI Civil Rights Act of 2025, partners with the AI Now Institute, and is funded through membership dues, individual donations, and private foundations.

name: Data & Society
notes_head: Independent nonprofit research institute studying social and political implications of AI, data, and automation. Research programs include AI on the Ground, Labor Futures, and Trustworthy Infrastructure. January 2026 brief critiqued US government market intervention to bolster AI sector. Funded by independent grants; fiercely protects researcher autonomy from funder influence.

name: Center for Democracy & Technology
notes_head: Center for Democracy & Technology (CDT) is a Washington, DC nonprofit founded in 1994 focused on civil liberties, privacy, and democratic values in digital policy. Its AI Governance Lab (led by Miranda Bogen) is a prominent civil-society voice in U.S. AI policy, engaging Congress, federal agencies, and international bodies on algorithmic accountability, surveillance, and AI rights.

name: ORCAA
notes_head: O'Neil Risk Consulting & Algorithmic Auditing, founded by Cathy O'Neil

name: Center for Humane Technology
notes_head: The Center for Humane Technology is a nonprofit organization founded in 2018 and headquartered in San Francisco, California, dedicated to radically reimagining digital infrastructure and driving a comprehensive shift toward humane technology. The organization was co-founded by Tristan Harris and Aza Raskin, with Harris previously serving as a Design Ethicist at Google where he created the viral presentation 'A Call to Minimize Distraction & Respect Users' Attention'. CHT employs 22 people and focuses on transforming the incentives that drive technology, from social media to artificial intellig

name: DAIR Institute
notes_head: The Distributed Artificial Intelligence Research Institute (or DAIR Institute) is a research institute founded by Timnit Gebru in December 2021. The institute announced itself as "an independent, community-rooted institute set to counter Big Tech's pervasive influence on the research, development and deployment of AI". In February 2023, two other members of Google's Ethical AI research group, researcher Alex Hanna and developer Dylan Baker, left Google to join DAIR. DAIR is committed to interdisciplinary and globally distributed AI research.

name: Signal
notes_head: Nonprofit developer of the Signal encrypted-messaging app. President Meredith Whittaker argues AI is fundamentally a surveillance technology that expands harmful data-collection business models. Signal has publicly warned that agentic AI poses an existential threat to secure messaging, and opposes mandatory message-scanning laws such as the EU Chat Control proposal.

name: Common Sense Media
notes_head: Common Sense Media is an American nonprofit organization founded by Jim Steyer in 2003 that reviews and provides ratings for media and technology with the goal of providing information on their suitability for children. The organization is headquartered in San Francisco, California and is dedicated to improving the lives of kids and families by providing trustworthy information, education, and independent voice they need to thrive. Common Sense Media rates movies, TV shows, books, and more so parents can feel good about the entertainment choices they make for their kids, and offers the largest

name: Amnesty International
notes_head: Major international human rights organization with strong positions on AI governance and digital rights. Calls for outright ban on facial recognition technology for mass surveillance by police and state agencies, citing racial discrimination (higher false positive rates for women and people of color) and chilling effects on peaceful assembly. Runs the 'Ban the Scan' campaign documenting FRT harms in New York, Israel/OPT, and Hyderabad. Advocates for legally binding AI regulation centered on documented harms, not voluntary industry commitments. Criticized the EU AI Act as insufficient to protec

name: Pathos Consulting Group
notes_head: AI ethics consulting firm focused on K-12 education, founded by Evan J. Harris (President). Specializes in helping independent schools integrate AI tools responsibly — staff department automation, student AI empowerment, and ethical professional development. Harris holds master's in private school leadership from Teachers College and studied tech ethics at Stanford's Human-Centered AI Institute (HAI). National expert on emerging AI risks in schools, particularly deepfake abuse and digital safety. Employs holistic systems approach addressing data privacy, bias, authentic communication. Partnere

name: Lawyers' Committee for Civil Rights Under Law
notes_head: Nonpartisan civil rights legal organization founded in 1963 at the request of President Kennedy. Champions the AI Civil Rights Act (co-endorsed with Rep. Pressley, Sen. Markey, Rep. Clarke) requiring transparency, accountability, and anti-discrimination safeguards in AI systems used in housing, lending, healthcare, employment, education, and law enforcement. Applauded Biden's AI Executive Order and White House AI Bill of Rights. Opposed Trump-era executive order seeking to bar states from addressing AI harms. Leverages the nation's largest pro bono legal network (62,000+ hours/year). Headquart

name: The Leadership Conference on Civil and Human Rights
notes_head: America's oldest and largest civil rights coalition (240+ member organizations including ACLU, NAACP LDF, Sierra Club). Launched the Center for Civil Rights and Technology in 2023 focused on AI policy impacts on civil and human rights. Published an Innovation Framework guiding responsible AI investment, creation, and use. Led a 50+ organization letter to the Senate opposing a 10-year ban on state/local AI laws. Submitted comments to NSF on national AI R&D strategy and to NIST on AI standards, emphasizing civil rights must be embedded at every stage of the AI lifecycle. Headquarters: Washington

name: Human Rights Data Analysis Group
notes_head: Nonprofit, non-partisan organization (est. 1991, founded by Patrick Ball) applying rigorous statistical science to human rights violations worldwide. Pioneered research demonstrating how predictive policing algorithms amplify racial bias — showed PredPol and similar tools learn from biased police recording patterns rather than actual crime patterns, reinforcing discriminatory enforcement. Fiscal sponsorship through Community Partners since 2013. Funded by MacArthur Foundation ($2M+ since 2015), Open Society Foundations, Oak Foundation, Sigrid Rausing Trust, Ethics & Governance of AI Initiative

name: Distributed AI Research Institute
notes_head: DUPLICATE of entity 875 (DAIR) and entity 1052 (DAIR Institute). The Distributed AI Research Institute is the full name of Timnit Gebru's DAIR. See entity 875 for full enrichment. This entity should be flagged for deduplication/merge.

name: Anthropic
loc: San Francisco, CA (with Sydney, Bengaluru offices)
notes_head: Anthropic is an artificial intelligence company founded in 2021 by former OpenAI leaders including Dario Amodei and Daniela Amodei. The company specializes in Constitutional AI and has developed Claude, a family of large language models designed for safety and enterprise use. Anthropic has experienced rapid growth, scaling from $1 billion ARR in 2024 to $14 billion in February 2026. The company is structured as a Public Benefit Corporation focused on AI safety research.

name: OpenAI
loc: San Francisco, CA (HQ) with offices in London, Dublin, Tokyo, Singapore
notes_head: OpenAI is an American artificial intelligence research organization comprising both a nonprofit foundation and a controlled for-profit public benefit corporation, headquartered in San Francisco. The company was founded in December 2015 as a nonprofit, with a for-profit subsidiary created in 2019, and restructured in 2025 to operate more independently from the nonprofit. OpenAI's mission is to ensure that artificial general intelligence benefits all of humanity. The company has 4,500 employees as of 2026 and reported revenue of US$13.1 billion in 2025.

name: Google DeepMind
loc: London, UK (HQ) + Mountain View, CA, New York, Montreal, Paris offices
notes_head: Google DeepMind brings together two of the world's leading AI labs — Google Brain and DeepMind — into a single, focused team led by CEO Demis Hassabis. The company's mission is to build AI responsibly to benefit humanity, working to advance artificial general intelligence (AGI). Google DeepMind has achieved notable breakthroughs including AlphaGo defeating world champion Go players, AlphaFold solving protein folding problems, and developing the Transformer architecture that underlies most large language models. The organization employs over 1,000 researchers and engineers from diverse discipli

name: xAI
notes_head: xAI is an American AI company founded by Elon Musk on March 9, 2023, with a mission to "understand the true nature of the universe". SpaceX acquired xAI in February 2026 in an all-stock transaction that valued SpaceX at $1 trillion and xAI at $250 billion. The company's flagship product is Grok, an AI chatbot that is accessible on grok.com, iOS, and Android. xAI raised $20 billion in a Series E funding round in January 2026, with strategic investors including Nvidia and Cisco Investments.

name: Safe Superintelligence Inc.
loc: Palo Alto, CA + Tel Aviv, Israel
notes_head: Safe Superintelligence Inc. is an Israeli-American artificial intelligence company founded by Ilya Sutskever, the former chief scientist of OpenAI; Daniel Gross, former head of Apple's AI efforts; and Daniel Levy, an investor and AI researcher. The company was founded on June 19, 2024, with the goal to safely develop superintelligent AI. SSI raised $1 billion from venture capital firms including SV Angel, DST Global, Sequoia Capital, and Andreessen Horowitz in September 2024. In March 2025, SSI reached a $30 billion valuation in a funding round led by Greenoaks Capital.

name: Meta AI
loc: Menlo Park, CA (global HQ) + offices in NYC, London, Paris, Seattle, Montreal, Tel Aviv
notes_head: Meta AI is a research division of Meta (formerly Facebook) that develops artificial intelligence and augmented reality technologies. The division was founded on December 11, 2015, with founders including Rob Fergus, Yann LeCun, Serkan Piantino, and Mark Zuckerberg. Meta AI has workspaces in Menlo Park, London, New York City, Paris, Seattle, Pittsburgh, Tel Aviv, and Montreal as of 2025. The organization produces products including Llama and operates under the ownership of Meta Platforms.

name: Mistral AI
loc: Paris, France (+ Palo Alto, CA office)
notes_head: Mistral AI is a French artificial intelligence company headquartered in Paris, founded in April 2023 by three AI researchers: Arthur Mensch, Guillaume Lample, and Timothée Lacroix. The company develops open-source large language models and AI systems, with a mission to democratize artificial intelligence through open-source, efficient, and innovative AI models, products, and solutions. As of 2025, the company has a valuation of more than US$14 billion and employs over 800 team members from 30+ different nationalities. Mistral AI has secured significant funding including a €2 billion investment

name: DeepSeek
notes_head: DeepSeek was founded in 2023 in Hangzhou, China by Liang Wenfeng, who previously co-founded one of China's top hedge funds, High-Flyer, which focuses on AI-driven quantitative trading. The company released its first AI large language model later in 2023. DeepSeek operates independently but is financially backed by High-Flyer, receiving a significant $50 million investment from the hedge fund with no institutional or angel investors. The company is fully funded by High-Flyer, which manages approximately Rmb60 billion (around $8 billion) as of 2023.

name: Humain
notes_head: HUMAIN is a Saudi artificial intelligence company established under the Public Investment Fund and launched on May 12, 2025, by Mohammed bin Salman, Crown Prince of Saudi Arabia. The company operates across the entire AI value chain, providing comprehensive AI services including next-generation data centers, AI infrastructure and cloud capabilities, and advanced AI models and solutions. HUMAIN aims to position Saudi Arabia as a globally competitive AI hub and is chaired by HRH the Crown Prince. The company's CEO is Tareq Amin.

name: Sakana AI
notes_head: Founded 2023, raised $302M total funding. Develops nature-inspired foundation models and automated scientific discovery systems. Created 'AI Scientist' tool that generates complete research papers for ~$15 each. First AI system to pass peer review at academic conference (ICLR 2025). Partnered with Google in 2026 for enterprise AI solutions targeting finance and government sectors.

name: World Labs
notes_head: Founded 2024 by AI pioneer Fei-Fei Li and team, developing 'spatial intelligence' and Large World Models for 3D environments. Raised $1.23B total funding including $1B round in Feb 2026 from AMD, Nvidia, Autodesk. Released Marble product in Nov 2025 for generating 3D virtual worlds. 39+ employees, valued at ~$5B.

name: Thinking Machines Lab
notes_head: AI frontier lab founded February 2025 by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati; raised $2B at $12B valuation led by a16z, with Nvidia, AMD, Cisco as investors. Chief scientist is John Schulman (ex-OpenAI). Focus on post-training efficiency and developer tools; released Tinker for model fine-tuning. Pursuing $5B raise at ~$50B valuation as of late 2025.

name: Google
notes_head: Google is an American multinational technology corporation focused on artificial intelligence and other technologies. The company was founded in 1998 by Larry Page and Sergey Brin. Sundar Pichai was appointed CEO of Google in 2015, replacing Larry Page, and in 2019, Pichai also became the CEO of Alphabet. Google is Alphabet's largest subsidiary and is a holding company for Alphabet's internet properties and interests.

name: Microsoft
notes_head: Microsoft Corporation is an American multinational technology conglomerate headquartered in Redmond, Washington. Founded in 1975, the company became influential in the rise of personal computers through software like Windows, and has since expanded to Internet services, cloud computing, artificial intelligence, video gaming, and other fields. Microsoft is the largest software company by revenue, one of the most valuable public companies, and one of the most valuable brands globally. Revenue was $281.7 billion, up 15 percent in fiscal 2025.

name: Meta
notes_head: Meta Platforms, Inc. is the developer of the Meta AI app and various AI products including Meta AI assistant built with Llama technology. The company develops and releases the Llama series of open-source large language models, with Llama 3 and Llama 4 being their latest offerings. Meta's mission is to build the future of human connection and the technology that makes it possible. The company reported revenue of $200.97 billion for full year 2025, representing a 22% year-over-year increase.

name: Google Brain
notes_head: Google Brain was a deep learning artificial intelligence research team that served as the sole AI branch of Google before being incorporated under the newer umbrella of Google AI, a research division at Google dedicated to artificial intelligence. Formed in 2011, it combined open-ended machine learning research with information systems and large-scale computing resources. It was merged into former Google sister company DeepMind to form Google DeepMind in April 2023. Google Brain was initially established by Google Fellow Jeff Dean and visiting Stanford professor Andrew Ng.

name: Google Research
notes_head: Google Research is the research division of Google, a subsidiary of Alphabet Inc. According to its official website, Google Research publishes findings, releases open-source software, and applies research results within Google products and services as well as within the wider scientific community. Google Research maintains a portfolio of research projects driven by fundamental research, new product innovation, product contribution and infrastructure goals. The organization is led by Yossi Matias, Vice President & Head of Google Research.

name: Microsoft Research
notes_head: Microsoft Research is a multidisciplinary global organization working to create scientific knowledge and deliver innovation that leads to positive human impact at scale. For nearly 35 years, Microsoft Research has shaped the technology people use every day through a commitment to hiring the world's top minds and giving them autonomy, resources, and support to conduct research. Microsoft Research has research labs across the globe and mission-focused efforts in areas like scientific discovery and human health, conducting fundamental science and technology research that benefits humanity. The or

name: Facebook AI Research (FAIR)
notes_head: Facebook AI Research (FAIR) Paris was created in 2015 to advance the field of artificial intelligence through open research for the benefit of all. FAIR has grown into an international research organization with labs in Menlo Park, New York, Paris, Montreal, Tel Aviv, Seattle, Pittsburgh, and London. For more than a decade, Meta's Fundamental AI Research (FAIR) team has focused on advancing the state of the art in AI through open research. FAIR has applied an open model to all aspects of their work, collaborating broadly with the community by publishing cutting-edge research early and often, a

name: McGill AI Lab
notes_head: Umbrella term for McGill University's AI research labs, most prominently the Reasoning and Learning Lab co-directed by Prakash Panangaden, Doina Precup, Joelle Pineau, Jackie Chi Kit Cheung, Siva Reddy, Reihaneh Rabbany, Siamak Ravanbakhsh, and David Rolnick. McGill is a world leader in machine learning with particular strengths in reinforcement learning and natural language processing. Connected to Mila (Quebec AI Institute) through shared faculty. The iSMART Lab (Prof. Narges Armanfard) focuses on computer vision, time series analysis, and physics-informed modeling. Also participates in the

name: High-Flyer AI
notes_head: High-Flyer (Zhejiang High-Flyer Asset Management) is a Chinese quantitative hedge fund founded by Liang Wenfeng. In 2023 it incubated DeepSeek as a spin-off AI lab, funding it internally. High-Flyer posted 56.6% average returns in 2025 and manages over 70 billion yuan (~$10B USD).

name: Stability AI
notes_head: Open-source generative AI company (est. 2019). Creator of Stable Diffusion. Founded by Emad Mostaque, who stepped down as CEO in March 2024 citing desire to 'fix concentration of power in AI.' Current CEO: Prem Akkaraju (since June 2024). Raised $80M from Eric Schmidt, Coatue, Lightspeed. Strong open-source advocate: released model weights under permissive licenses. Wrote to US Senate urging open-source AI preservation. B2B2C model: open-source distribution + commercial API. Expanding into video, audio, 3D. Growing at triple-digit rates as of late 2024.

name: Advanced Machine Intelligence Labs
notes_head: Full legal name: Advanced Machine Intelligence (AMI Labs). AI startup co-founded by Yann LeCun (exec chairman) and Alex LeBrun (CEO) after LeCun left Meta (Nov 2025). Raised $1.03B seed at $3.5B pre-money valuation (Mar 2026) — one of largest seed rounds ever. Building 'world models' using JEPA architecture as alternative to LLMs. Backed by Nvidia, Samsung, Bezos Expeditions, Eric Schmidt. Key team: Saining Xie (CSO), Pascale Fung (CRIO), Michael Rabbat (VP World Models), Laurent Solly (COO). Target: industrial control, robotics, healthcare, wearables. NOTE: Entity 1433 (AMI Labs) is the SAME

name: AMI Labs
notes_head: DUPLICATE of entity 1432 (Advanced Machine Intelligence Labs). 'AMI Labs' is the short/common name for Advanced Machine Intelligence Labs, co-founded by Yann LeCun and Alex LeBrun. See entity 1432 for full enrichment. Recommend merging edges to 1432 and soft-deleting this entity.

name: Cohere
notes_head: Canadian enterprise-focused AI company founded in 2019 by Aidan Gomez (co-author of 'Attention Is All You Need'), Nick Frosst, and Ivan Zhang. Builds large language models and operates the Cohere For AI non-profit research lab. Backed by Inovia, Tiger Global, NVIDIA, and Fujitsu; reached a $5.5B valuation in 2024. Participates in North American frontier-AI policy discussions and has signed multiple voluntary AI commitments.

name: Inflection AI
notes_head: AI startup founded in 2022 by Mustafa Suleyman, Reid Hoffman, and Karén Simonyan. Built the 'Pi' conversational AI assistant positioned as emotionally intelligent and personal. In March 2024, Microsoft hired most of Inflection's staff (including Suleyman as CEO of Microsoft AI) and licensed Inflection's technology in a ~$650M deal. The remaining company pivoted to enterprise AI under CEO Sean White.

name: Senate AI Working Group
notes_head: The Senate AI Working Group is a bipartisan organization formed in the 118th Congress, consisting of Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-NY), Senator Mike Rounds (R-SD), Senator Martin Heinrich (D-NM), and Senator Todd Young (R-IN). The Working Group was formed due to shared recognition of the profound changes artificial intelligence could bring to the world and the need to proactively work to harness opportunities and address risks of this transformational technology. The Working Group's objective has been to complement the traditional congressional committee-driven policy process, consid

name: Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI)
loc: Washington, D.C. and San Francisco, CA
notes_head: The Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) is a body within the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) at the U.S. Department of Commerce. It was established on June 3, 2025 when Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick renamed and refocused the U.S. AI Safety Institute (AISI), which had been created under the Biden administration in November 2023 by Commerce Secretary Gina Raimondo. The rebrand dropped "safety" from the title and pivoted the mission from catastrophic-risk research toward national security, cybersecurity, chemical/biological weapons misuse evaluation, and globa

name: Senate Commerce Committee (AI jurisdiction)
notes_head: The Senate Commerce Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation has primary jurisdiction over artificial intelligence policy and legislation. Senator Ted Cruz (R-Texas) serves as Chairman of the committee and has convened multiple full committee hearings on AI topics, including "Winning the AI Race: Strengthening U.S. Capabilities in Computing and Innovation". The committee has passed bipartisan legislation including the Future of AI Innovation Act to maintain United States leadership in the global race to develop artificial intelligence and other emerging technologies. Senator Maria Ca

name: European AI Office
loc: Brussels, Belgium (within European Commission DG CNECT)
notes_head: Established February 2024 within European Commission as world's first comprehensive AI regulatory body. Employs 125+ staff including technology specialists, lawyers, and economists across 6 units. Enforces EU AI Act (entered force August 2024) with phased implementation through 2027. Led by Director Lucilla Sioli, coordinates with European AI Board representing 27 member states.

name: Global Partnership on AI
loc: Paris, France (OECD-hosted secretariat) + Montreal, Canada (Center of Expertise)
notes_head: Founded June 2020 by 15 countries (now 29 members including US, EU, Japan, India). Intergovernmental initiative hosted by OECD. Four working groups: Responsible AI, Data Governance, Future of Work, Innovation & Commercialization. Produces policy guidance, safety frameworks, and coordinates international AI governance. Key projects include AI safety risk mapping, Hiroshima AI Process reporting framework, and transformative AI policy recommendations.

name: White House Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP)
notes_head: Led by Director Michael Kratsios under Trump administration. Coordinates AI policy across federal agencies through America's AI Action Plan (July 2025). Key initiatives include removing regulatory barriers, establishing AI procurement standards, creating federal AI framework to preempt state laws, and promoting AI adoption in government. Staff of approximately 22 people with $325M proposed budget cuts to NIST creating tension with innovation goals.

name: Advanced Research + Invention Agency (ARIA)
notes_head: UK government R&D agency established 2023 by Act of Parliament, modeled after DARPA. £800M budget over 5 years (£184M allocated 2025-26). CEO Ilan Gur, 95 employees. Eight program directors with £50M+ budgets each. Major programs: Safeguarded AI (£69M), Scaling Compute (£50M), Precision Neurotechnologies. Portfolio includes AI hardware startups like Fractile (£5M) and Callosum (partnership). Operates independently from government intervention.

name: UK AI Security Institute
notes_head: The UK AI Security Institute (AISI) is a government research organisation within the UK's Department for Science, Innovation and Technology, established in 2023 as the AI Safety Institute and renamed in February 2025. AISI's mission is to equip governments with scientific understanding of advanced AI risks — particularly catastrophic risks such as AI-assisted development of chemical and biological weapons, cyberattacks, and AI-enabled crime. Under Chair Ian Hogarth and Interim Director Adam Beaumont (formerly GCHQ's Chief AI Officer), the institute employs 100+ technical staff including alumni

name: Federal Trade Commission
notes_head: The Federal Trade Commission (FTC) is an independent U.S. federal agency responsible for consumer protection and preventing anticompetitive business practices. In the AI policy ecosystem, the FTC is a primary enforcement body for AI-related consumer harms, using Section 5 of the FTC Act — which prohibits unfair or deceptive acts or practices — as its principal enforcement vehicle. Under Biden-era Chair Lina Khan (2021–2025), the FTC took an aggressive posture on AI and Big Tech. Under Trump-appointed Chair Andrew Ferguson (2025–present), the FTC shifted to a lighter-touch model: enforcing exis

name: NTIA
notes_head: The National Telecommunications and Information Administration (NTIA) is a Commerce Department agency that advises the President on telecom and information policy. It published a landmark AI Accountability Policy Report (2024) urging independent audits of high-risk AI systems. Under the Trump 2025 AI Action Plan, NTIA shifted focus toward promoting U.S.-led AI and 6G connectivity, and gained authority to withhold broadband funding from states with laws deemed to obstruct national AI policy.

name: Department of Justice
notes_head: Federal law enforcement agency operating the AI Litigation Task Force (est. Jan 2026) to challenge state AI laws. Pursues AI antitrust cases and False Claims Act enforcement; historically oversaw civil rights compliance with law enforcement AI use.

name: CISA
notes_head: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is the DHS component established in 2018 as the nation''s cyber defense agency. Leads federal efforts on critical-infrastructure security and has taken an increasingly active role in AI policy — co-authoring secure-AI-development guidance with NSA and allied cyber agencies and operating as a convening body for AI safety and security incident reporting.

name: Department of Commerce
notes_head: Houses NIST, which develops the AI Risk Management Framework, and BIS, which controls AI chip exports. Under Trump, overseeing the American AI Exports Program and revising NIST's AI RMF to remove DEI and climate references.

name: European Commission
notes_head: EU executive body responsible for implementing the AI Act, which entered into force August 2024. Key 2025 milestones: prohibited practices applied February 2025, GPAI model obligations August 2025. Full application by August 2026. Issues binding guidelines and manages the AI Office for enforcement. Proposed Digital Omnibus amendments in November 2025 to adjust high-risk system timelines.

name: Taiwan Ministry of Digital Affairs
notes_head: Taiwan''s Ministry of Digital Affairs (MoDA), established August 2022, is the cabinet-level ministry responsible for digital development, cybersecurity, and AI policy in Taiwan. Audrey Tang served as its first minister (2022–2024) and made it a widely cited example of participatory digital governance, open-source civic tech, and democratic AI policymaking.

name: United States Senate
notes_head: The United States Senate is a legislative chamber in the bicameral legislature of the United States of America, and together with the U.S. House of Representatives makes up the U.S. Congress. First convened in 1789, the composition and powers of the Senate are established in Article One of the U.S. Constitution. Each U.S. state is represented by two senators, regardless of population, who serve staggered six-year terms. The chamber of the United States Senate is located in the north wing of the Capitol, in Washington, D.C., the national capital.

name: U.S. House of Representatives
notes_head: The U.S. House of Representatives passed a federal budget bill that includes a controversial 10-year moratorium on states regulating artificial intelligence with a vote of 215 to 214. The House established a Bipartisan Task Force on Artificial Intelligence that delivered a comprehensive report in December 2024 with 66 findings and 89 recommendations. The House held hearings on AI topics including "Advancing America's AI Action Plan" and "Building an AI-Ready America" in January 2026. The 119th Congress was sworn-in in January 2025, with Republicans holding a majority with 220 seats and Democra

name: California State Senate
notes_head: The California State Senate is the upper house of the California State Legislature, consisting of 40 members elected from districts apportioned on the basis of population, with one-half elected or re-elected every two years for four-year terms. The California State Senate employs 664 people and has an annual revenue of $143.0M, founded in 1849 and headquartered in Sacramento, California. In the 2024 elections, Democrats lost one seat for a 30 seat majority to Republicans' nine seats. The Senate has been actively considering multiple artificial intelligence-related bills in 2025-2026, including

name: New York State Assembly
notes_head: The New York State Assembly is known as the "People's House" in New York. Since its creation in 1777, the state Assembly has helped shape history, often leading the way with progressive action and reform. Carl E. Heastie serves as Assembly Speaker, becoming New York's first African American Assembly Speaker when elected on February 3, 2015. Following the 2024 election, Democrats maintained a 103-47 majority in the Assembly.

name: White House
notes_head: The White House released its National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence on March 20, 2026, outlining the Trump administration's legislative priorities for federal AI governance. The framework stems from an executive order President Donald Trump signed in December that blocked states from enforcing their own regulations around artificial intelligence. The White House established the Genesis Mission in November 2025 as a national effort to accelerate the application of AI for transformative scientific discovery focused on pressing national challenges. The administration has implemente

name: State of California
notes_head: The State of California has issued multiple executive orders and guidelines related to artificial intelligence under Governor Gavin Newsom, including Executive Order N-12-23 on Generative Artificial Intelligence signed on September 6, 2023. The state published GenAI Guidelines for Public Sector Procurement, Uses and Training in March 2024. Governor Newsom signed another executive order in 2026 to strengthen AI protections and responsible use while the Trump administration was rolling back federal protections. California leads the nation in AI regulations and is home to many of the largest AI c

name: State of Texas
notes_head: The State of Texas signed the Texas Responsible Artificial Intelligence Governance Act (TRAIGA) into law on June 22, 2025, with an effective date of January 1, 2026. The law prohibits AI systems designed to manipulate behavior causing harm, discriminate based on protected characteristics, produce child sexual abuse material, or enable social scoring by government. The Texas Attorney General has exclusive authority to enforce TRAIGA. The state's Consolidated General Revenue Fund ended fiscal 2025 with a cash balance of $41.4 billion, an increase of $4.0 billion from fiscal 2024.

name: State of Colorado
notes_head: The State of Colorado enacted Senate Bill 24-205, also known as the Colorado Anti-Discrimination in AI Law (ADAI), which was signed into law on May 17, 2024. The ADAI is part of the State of Colorado's Consumer Protection Act and its provisions go into effect February 1, 2026. The Colorado Attorney General has exclusive enforcement authority for this legislation and is tasked with creating rules to implement and enforce the ADAI's requirements. History Colorado is established as a 501(c)(3) charitable organization and an agency of the State of Colorado under the Department of Higher Education.

name: State of New York
notes_head: The State of New York Office of Information Technology Services established the NYS-P24-001 policy for acceptable use of AI technologies by state entities, updated on March 11, 2025. Governor Kathy Hochul announced the FY 2025 State Budget which included a $275 million investment over 10 years to support Empire AI, a consortium to secure New York's position in artificial intelligence. The New York State Executive Chamber works to meet the needs of all New Yorkers by advancing the Governor's policy initiatives and agenda, as well as overseeing government operations. The State of New York has va

name: State of Ohio
notes_head: Ohio became the 17th state in the Union in 1803, the same year as the Louisiana Purchase. The Ohio Department of Job and Family Services received the Merrill Baumgardner Award from the Washington-based National Association of State Workforce Agencies, which honors states that demonstrate excellence in information technology and set benchmarks nationwide. Ohio has released a new policy that includes guidance and requirements for how Ohio state government workers can use AI and generative AI for work within their agencies. Governor Mike DeWine has signed Ohio's Fiscal Year 2024-2025 operating bu

name: Office of the Governor of Indiana
