Raised $204M over 5 rounds; made 86+ investments. Funds Foresight Institute's AI for Science & Safety program. Co-hosts LabWeek Field Building and Funding The Commons events with Foresight Institute. Focuses on decentralized infrastructure, open-source protocols, and Web3. Expanding into AI, AR/VR, BCI, and hardware via its network model. Headquartered in Palo Alto.

name: Gigafund
notes_head: Venture capital firm founded in 2017 by Luke Nosek (PayPal co-founder, Founders Fund) and Stephen Oskoui in Austin, TX. Runs an extremely concentrated, multi-decade strategy focused on capital-intensive physical-world technology companies (SpaceX, Neuralink, Boring Company). Funder of Foresight Institute's AI for Science & Safety Nodes program, supporting AI-driven nanotechnology and molecular assembly research. Portfolio of ~18 companies spanning space, biotech, neurotech, and AI.

name: 100 Plus Capital
notes_head: Longevity-focused venture capital firm founded in 2007 by Sonia Arrison in Menlo Park, CA. Invests in companies positively impacting human longevity, both directly (anti-aging) and broadly (clean food, water). Portfolio of ~15 companies concentrated in HealthTech and Life Sciences. Arrison is also a Foresight Institute board member and chairs the Alliance for Longevity Initiatives. Funder of Foresight Institute's AI for Science & Safety Nodes alongside Gigafund.

name: Players Philanthropy Fund, Inc.
notes_head: Nonprofit fiscal sponsor (501(c)(3)) providing tax-exempt status, back-office services, and operational support to 500+ organizations and family funds. Founded in 2010 by retired NFL player Matt Stover and nonprofit professional Seth McDonnell. Widely used within the effective altruism community as a fiscal sponsor for EA-aligned organizations, including Charity Entrepreneurship incubatees and CEARCH. Funded by Centre for Effective Altruism; FWD.us Education Fund gave $200K in 2022. Serves as fiscal sponsor for CaML (Compassion in Machine Learning).

name: Survival & Flourishing Fund
notes_head: Major EA/longtermist grantmaker focused on long-term survival and flourishing of sentient life. Founded 2019, primarily funded by Jaan Tallinn (Skype co-founder). Distributed ~$152M since inception, $34.9M in 2025 alone — 86% to AI safety. Second-largest AI safety funder after Open Philanthropy. Uses S-Process algorithm for grant recommendations, plus Speculation Grants for expedited funding (~35 grantors). Also funds biosecurity and institutional resilience. Key funder of CaML and many other AI safety orgs.

name: Open Philanthropy Project
notes_head: Open Philanthropy Project is an earlier name for what is now Coefficient Giving (entity 128, renamed November 2025). This entity likely represents the organization under its prior branding. Open Philanthropy has been a leading funder of AI safety, security, and governance since 2015. By 2025, it had directed over $4 billion in total grants, with roughly $50 million committed to technical AI safety research in 2024 alone, followed by a $40 million RFP for technical AI safety research across 21 research areas. The organization views AI risk as a classic case for philanthropic investment because

name: Carnegie Corporation of New York
notes_head: Major U.S. philanthropy founded by Andrew Carnegie in 1911. One of the largest private foundations with ~$4.5B in assets and ~$174M in annual grantmaking (FY 2023-24, 353 grants). Funds AI governance and technology policy through multiple channels: grants to CNAS (already an edge), AI-nuclear risk research ($8.2M package in Jan 2026 including grants to Arms Control Association and Institute for Security and Technology for the Andrew Carnegie AI-Nuclear Policy Accelerator), AI capacity building in African higher education ($900K to African Technology Policy Studies Network), AI ethics for famil

name: Effective Ventures
notes_head: Umbrella organization for the effective altruism movement, comprising Effective Ventures Foundation (UK) and Effective Ventures Foundation USA, Inc. Provides fiscal sponsorship and operational support to high-impact projects. Renamed from CEA Ops in 2022. Sponsored projects include Centre for Effective Altruism (CEA), 80,000 Hours, Giving What We Can, EA Funds, Forethought Foundation, Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI, already an edge as fiscal sponsor), Longview Philanthropy, Asterisk, Atlas Fellowship, and Non-trivial. Budget exceeds $50M. Significant funder of AI safety and existential
